typed_metadata_context_namespaces_(config.typed_metadata_context_namespaces().begin(),\n   111\u2192                                         config.typed_metadata_context_namespaces().end()),\n   112\u2192      route_metadata_context_namespaces_(config.route_metadata_context_namespaces().begin(),\n   113\u2192                                         config.route_metadata_context_namespaces().end()),\n   114\u2192      route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces_(\n   115\u2192          config.route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces().begin(),\n   116\u2192          config.route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces().end()),\n   117\u2192      include_peer_certificate_(config.include_peer_certificate()),\n   118\u2192      include_tls_session_(config.include_tls_session()),\n   119\u2192      charge_cluster_response_stats_(\n   120\u2192          PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, charge_cluster_response_stats, true)),\n   121\u2192      stats_(generateStats(stats_prefix, config.stat_prefix(), scope)),\n   122\u2192      ext_authz_ok_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \"ok\"))),\n   123\u2192      ext_authz_denied_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \"denied\"))),\n   124\u2192      ext_authz_error_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \"error\"))),\n   125\u2192      ext_authz_invalid_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \"invalid\"))),\n   126\u2192      ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_(\n   127\u2192          pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \"failure_mode_allowed\"))) {\n   128\u2192  auto bootstrap = factory_context.bootstrap();\n   129\u2192  auto labels_key_it =\n   130\u2192      bootstrap.node().metadata().fields().find(config.bootstrap_metadata_labels_key());\n   131\u2192  if (labels_key_it != bootstrap.node().metadata().fields().end()) {\n   132\u2192    for (const auto& labels_it : labels_key_it->second.struct_value().fields()) {\n   133\u2192      destination_labels_[labels_it.first] = labels_it.second.string_value();\n   134\u2192    }\n   135\u2192  }\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192  if (config.has_allowed_headers() &&\n   138\u2192      config.http_service().authorization_request().has_allowed_headers()) {\n   139\u2192    throw EnvoyException(\"Invalid duplicate configuration for allowed_headers.\");\n   140\u2192  }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192  // An unset request_headers_matchers_ means that all client request headers are allowed through\n   143\u2192  // to the authz server; this is to preserve backwards compatibility when introducing\n   144\u2192  // allowlisting of request headers for gRPC authz servers. Pre-existing support is for\n   145\u2192  // HTTP authz servers only and defaults to blocking all but a few headers (i.e. Authorization,\n   146\u2192  // Method, Path and Host).\n   147\u2192  if (config.has_grpc_service() && config.has_allowed_headers()) {\n   148\u2192    allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n   149\u2192        config.allowed_headers(), false, factory_context);\n   150\u2192  } else if (config.has_http_service()) {\n   151\u2192    if (config.http_service().authorization_request().has_allowed_headers()) {\n   152\u2192      allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n   153\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_request().allowed_headers(), true, factory_context);\n   154\u2192    } else {\n   155\u2192      allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n   156\u2192          config.allowed_headers(), true, factory_context);\n   157\u2192    }\n   158\u2192  }\n   159\u2192  if (config.has_disallowed_headers()) {\n   160\u2192    disallowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n   161\u2192        config.disallowed_headers(), false, factory_context);\n   162\u2192  }\n   163\u2192}\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192void FilterConfigPerRoute::merge(const FilterConfigPerRoute& other) {\n   166\u2192  // We only merge context extensions here, and leave boolean flags untouched since those flags are\n   167\u2192  // not used from the merged config.\n   168\u2192  auto begin_it = other.context_extensions_.begin();\n   169\u2192  auto end_it = other.context_extensions_.end();\n   170\u2192  for (auto it = begin_it; it != end_it; ++it) {\n   171\u2192    context_extensions_[it->first] = it->second;\n   172\u2192  }\n   173\u2192}\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192void Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n   176\u2192  if (filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding) {\n   177\u2192    return;\n   178\u2192  }\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192  // Now that we'll definitely be making the request, add filter state stats if configured to do so.\n   181\u2192  const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\n   182\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n   183\u2192  if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value())) {\n   184\u2192    if (!filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\n   185\u2192      filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\n   186\u2192                            std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\n   187\u2192                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\n   188\u2192                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192      // This may return nullptr (if there's a value at this name whose type doesn't match or isn't\n   191\u2192      // mutable, for example), so we must check logging_info_ is not nullptr later.\n   192\u2192      logging_info_ =\n   193\u2192          filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n   194\u2192    }\n   195\u2192    if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n   196\u2192      stats_.filter_state_name_collision_.inc();\n   197\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug,\n   198\u2192                       \"Could not find logging info at {}! (Did another filter already put data \"\n   199\u2192                       \"at this name?)\",\n   200\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n   201\u2192    }\n   202\u2192  }\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192  absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\n   205\u2192  for (const FilterConfigPerRoute& cfg :\n   206\u2192       Http::Utility::getAllPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_)) {\n   207\u2192    if (maybe_merged_per_route_config.has_value()) {\n   208\u2192      maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().merge(cfg);\n   209\u2192    } else {\n   210\u2192      maybe_merged_per_route_config = cfg;\n   211\u2192    }\n   212\u2192  }\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192  Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string> context_extensions;\n   215\u2192  if (maybe_merged_per_route_config) {\n   216\u2192    context_extensions = maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().takeContextExtensions();\n   217\u2192  }\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192  // If metadata_context_namespaces or typed_metadata_context_namespaces is specified,\n   220\u2192  // pass matching filter metadata to the ext_authz service.\n   221\u2192  // If metadata key is set in both the connection and request metadata,\n   222\u2192  // then the value will be the request metadata value.\n   223\u2192  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata metadata_context;\n   224\u2192  fillMetadataContext({&decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata(),\n   225\u2192                       &decoder_callbacks_->connection()->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata()},\n   226\u2192                      config_->metadataContextNamespaces(),\n   227\u2192                      config_->typedMetadataContextNamespaces(), metadata_context);\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192  // Fill route_metadata_context from the selected route's metadata.\n   230\u2192  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata route_metadata_context;\n   231\u2192  if (decoder_callbacks_->route() != nullptr) {\n   232\u2192    fillMetadataContext({&decoder_callbacks_->route()->metadata()},\n   233\u2192                        config_->routeMetadataContextNamespaces(),\n   234\u2192                        config_->routeTypedMetadataContextNamespaces(), route_metadata_context);\n   235\u2192  }\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::createHttpCheck(\n   238\u2192      decoder_callbacks_, headers, std::move(context_extensions), std::move(metadata_context),\n   239\u2192      std::move(route_metadata_context), check_request_, max_request_bytes_, config_->packAsBytes(),\n   240\u2192      config_->headersAsBytes(), config_->includePeerCertificate(), config_->includeTLSSession(),\n   241\u2192      config_->destinationLabels(), config_->allowedHeadersMatcher(),\n   242\u2192      config_->disallowedHeadersMatcher());\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   245\u2192  // Store start time of ext_authz filter call\n   246\u2192  start_time_ = decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime();\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192  state_ = State::Calling;\n   249\u2192  filter_return_ = FilterReturn::StopDecoding; // Don't let the filter chain continue as we are\n   250\u2192                                               // going to invoke check call.\n   251\u2192  cluster_ = decoder_callbacks_->clusterInfo();\n   252\u2192  initiating_call_ = true;\n   253\u2192  client_->check(*this, check_request_, decoder_callbacks_->activeSpan(),\n   254\u2192                 decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo());\n   255\u2192  initiating_call_ = false;\n   256\u2192}\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {\n   259\u2192  Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route = decoder_callbacks_->route();\n   260\u2192  const auto per_route_flags = getPerRouteFlags(route);\n   261\u2192  skip_check_ = per_route_flags.skip_check_;\n   262\u2192  if (skip_check_) {\n   263\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   264\u2192  }\n   265\u2192\n   266\u2192  if (!config_->filterEnabled(decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata())) {\n   267\u2192    stats_.disabled_.inc();\n   268\u2192    if (config_->denyAtDisable()) {\n   269\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter is disabled. Deny the request.\",\n   270\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n   271\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\n   272\u2192          StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\n   273\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n   274\u2192          config_->statusOnError(), EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n   275\u2192          Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzError);\n   276\u2192      return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n   277\u2192    }\n   278\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   279\u2192  }\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192  request_headers_ = &headers;\n   282\u2192  const auto check_settings = per_route_flags.check_settings_;\n   283\u2192  buffer_data_ = (config_->withRequestBody() || check_settings.has_with_request_body()) &&\n   284\u2192                 !check_settings.disable_request_body_buffering() &&\n   285\u2192                 !(end_stream || Http::Utility::isWebSocketUpgradeRequest(headers) ||\n   286\u2192                   Http::Utility::isH2UpgradeRequest(headers));\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192  max_request_bytes_ = config_->maxRequestBytes();\n   289\u2192  if (buffer_data_) {\n   290\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192    allow_partial_message_ = check_settings.has_with_request_body()\n   293\u2192                                 ? check_settings.with_request_body().allow_partial_message()\n   294\u2192                                 : config_->allowPartialMessage();\n   295\u2192    if (check_settings.has_with_request_body()) {\n   296\u2192      max_request_bytes_ = check_settings.with_request_body().max_request_bytes();\n   297\u2192    }\n   298\u2192    if (!allow_partial_message_) {\n   299\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->setDecoderBufferLimit(max_request_bytes_);\n   300\u2192    }\n   301\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n   302\u2192  }\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192  // Initiate a call to the authorization server since we are not disabled.\n   305\u2192  initiateCall(headers);\n   306\u2192  return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding\n   307\u2192             ? Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark\n   308\u2192             : Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   309\u2192}\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   312\u2192  if (buffer_data_ && !skip_check_) {\n   313\u2192    const bool buffer_is_full = isBufferFull(data.length());\n   314\u2192    if (end_stream || buffer_is_full) {\n   315\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {}\",\n   316\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, buffer_is_full ? \"buffer is full\" : \"stream is ended\");\n   317\u2192      // Make sure data is available in initiateCall.\n   318\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->addDecodedData(data, true);\n   319\u2192      initiateCall(*request_headers_);\n   320\u2192      return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding\n   321\u2192                 ? Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark\n   322\u2192                 : Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\n   323\u2192    } else {\n   324\u2192      return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndBuffer;\n   325\u2192    }\n   326\u2192  }\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\n   329\u2192}\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap&) {\n   332\u2192  if (buffer_data_ && !skip_check_) {\n   333\u2192    if (filter_return_ != FilterReturn::StopDecoding) {\n   334\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request\",\n   335\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n   336\u2192      initiateCall(*request_headers_);\n   337\u2192    }\n   338\u2192    return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding ? Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration\n   339\u2192                                                        : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\n   340\u2192  }\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192  return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\n   343\u2192}\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus Filter::encode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&) {\n   346\u2192  return Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   347\u2192}\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::encodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers, bool) {\n   350\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   351\u2192                   \"ext_authz filter has {} response header(s) to add and {} response header(s) to \"\n   352\u2192                   \"set to the encoded response:\",\n   353\u2192                   *encoder_callbacks_, response_headers_to_add_.size(),\n   354\u2192                   response_headers_to_set_.size());\n   355\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_add_.empty()) {\n   356\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   357\u2192        trace, \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the encoded response:\", *encoder_callbacks_);\n   358\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_add_) {\n   359\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\n   360\u2192      headers.addCopy(key, value);\n   361\u2192    }\n   362\u2192  }\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_set_.empty()) {\n   365\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   366\u2192        trace, \"ext_authz filter set header(s) to the encoded response:\", *encoder_callbacks_);\n   367\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_set_) {\n   368\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\n   369\u2192      headers.setCopy(key, value);\n   370\u2192    }\n   371\u2192  }\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_add_if_absent_.empty()) {\n   374\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_add_if_absent_) {\n   375\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\n   376\u2192      if (auto header_entry = headers.get(key); header_entry.empty()) {\n   377\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the encoded response:\",\n   378\u2192                         *encoder_callbacks_);\n   379\u2192        headers.addCopy(key, value);\n   380\u2192      }\n   381\u2192    }\n   382\u2192  }\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_.empty()) {\n   385\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_) {\n   386\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *encoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   387\u2192      if (auto header_entry = headers.get(key); !header_entry.empty()) {\n   388\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   389\u2192            trace, \"ext_authz filter set header(s) to the encoded response:\", *encoder_callbacks_);\n   390\u2192        headers.setCopy(key, value);\n   391\u2192      }\n   392\u2192    }\n   393\u2192  }\n   394\u2192  return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   395\u2192}\n   396\u2192\n   397\u2192Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::encodeData(Buffer::Instance&, bool) {\n   398\u2192  return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\n   399\u2192}\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap&) {\n   402\u2192  return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\n   403\u2192}\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192Http::FilterMetadataStatus Filter::encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap&) {\n   406\u2192  return Http::FilterMetadataStatus::Continue;\n   407\u2192}\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192void Filter::setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n   410\u2192  decoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n   411\u2192}\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192void Filter::updateLoggingInfo() {\n   414\u2192  if (!config_->emitFilterStateStats()) {\n   415\u2192    return;\n   416\u2192  }\n   417\u2192\n   418\u2192  if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n   419\u2192    return;\n   420\u2192  }\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192  // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\n   423\u2192  if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n   424\u2192    logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>(\n   425\u2192        decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value()));\n   426\u2192  }\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192  auto const* stream_info = client_->streamInfo();\n   429\u2192  if (stream_info == nullptr) {\n   430\u2192    return;\n   431\u2192  }\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192  const auto& bytes_meter = stream_info->getUpstreamBytesMeter();\n   434\u2192  if (bytes_meter != nullptr) {\n   435\u2192    logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());\n   436\u2192    logging_info_->setBytesReceived(bytes_meter->wireBytesReceived());\n   437\u2192  }\n   438\u2192  if (stream_info->upstreamInfo().has_value()) {\n   439\u2192    logging_info_->setUpstreamHost(stream_info->upstreamInfo()->upstreamHost());\n   440\u2192  }\n   441\u2192  absl::optional<Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr> cluster_info =\n   442\u2192      stream_info->upstreamClusterInfo();\n   443\u2192  if (cluster_info) {\n   444\u2192    logging_info_->setClusterInfo(std::move(*cluster_info));\n   445\u2192  }\n   446\u2192}\n   447\u2192\n   448\u2192void Filter::setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n   449\u2192  encoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n   450\u2192}\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192void Filter::onDestroy() {\n   453\u2192  if (state_ == State::Calling) {\n   454\u2192    state_ = State::Complete;\n   455\u2192    client_->cancel();\n   456\u2192  }\n   457\u2192}\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192CheckResult Filter::validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n   460\u2192    Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation, absl::string_view key,\n   461\u2192    absl::string_view value) const {\n   462\u2192  if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n   463\u2192                                       !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n   464\u2192    return CheckResult::FAIL;\n   465\u2192  }\n   466\u2192  // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder mutation rules.\n   467\u2192  return config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(operation, Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n   468\u2192}\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\n   471\u2192  state_ = State::Complete;\n   472\u2192  using Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus;\n   473\u2192  Stats::StatName empty_stat_name;\n   474\u2192\n   475\u2192  updateLoggingInfo();\n   476\u2192\n   477\u2192  if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\n   478\u2192    if (!config_->enableDynamicMetadataIngestion()) {\n   479\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   480\u2192                       \"Response is trying to inject dynamic metadata, but dynamic metadata \"\n   481\u2192                       \"ingestion is disabled. Ignoring...\",\n   482\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n   483\u2192      stats_.ignored_dynamic_metadata_.inc();\n   484\u2192    } else {\n   485\u2192      // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\n   486\u2192      if (start_time_.has_value() && response->status == CheckStatus::OK) {\n   487\u2192        ProtobufWkt::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\n   488\u2192        auto duration =\n   489\u2192            decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value();\n   490\u2192        ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\n   491\u2192            std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\n   492\u2192        (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\"ext_authz_duration\"] =\n   493\u2192            ext_authz_duration_value;\n   494\u2192      }\n   495\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\",\n   496\u2192                                                          response->dynamic_metadata);\n   497\u2192    }\n   498\u2192  }\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192  switch (response->status) {\n   501\u2192  case CheckStatus::OK: {\n   502\u2192    // Any changes to request headers or query parameters can affect how the request is going to be\n   503\u2192    // routed. If we are changing the headers we also need to clear the route\n   504\u2192    // cache.\n   505\u2192    if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\n   506\u2192        (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\n   507\u2192         !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\n   508\u2192         !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\n   509\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz is clearing route cache\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   510\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\n   511\u2192    }\n   512\u2192\n   513\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   514\u2192                     \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   515\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\n   516\u2192      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n   517\u2192          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n   518\u2192      switch (check_result) {\n   519\u2192      case CheckResult::OK:\n   520\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   521\u2192        request_headers_->setCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n   522\u2192        break;\n   523\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n   524\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   525\u2192                         key, value);\n   526\u2192        break;\n   527\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n   528\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   529\u2192                         key, value);\n   530\u2192        rejectResponse();\n   531\u2192        return;\n   532\u2192      }\n   533\u2192    }\n   534\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_add) {\n   535\u2192      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n   536\u2192          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n   537\u2192      switch (check_result) {\n   538\u2192      case CheckResult::OK:\n   539\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   540\u2192        request_headers_->addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n   541\u2192        break;\n   542\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n   543\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   544\u2192                         key, value);\n   545\u2192        break;\n   546\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n   547\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   548\u2192                         key, value);\n   549\u2192        rejectResponse();\n   550\u2192        return;\n   551\u2192      }\n   552\u2192    }\n   553\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_append) {\n   554\u2192      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n   555\u2192          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::APPEND, key, value);\n   556\u2192      switch (check_result) {\n   557\u2192      case CheckResult::OK: {\n   558\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   559\u2192        Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\n   560\u2192        const auto header_to_modify = request_headers_->get(lowercase_key);\n   561\u2192        // TODO(dio): Add a flag to allow appending non-existent headers, without setting it\n   562\u2192        // first (via `headers_to_add`). For example, given:\n   563\u2192        // 1. Original headers {\"original\": \"true\"}\n   564\u2192        // 2. Response headers from the authorization servers {{\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\":\n   565\u2192        // \"2\"}}\n   566\u2192        //\n   567\u2192        // Currently it is not possible to add {{\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\": \"2\"}} (the\n   568\u2192        // intended combined headers: {{\"original\": \"true\"}, {\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\":\n   569\u2192        // \"2\"}}) to the request to upstream server by only sets `headers_to_append`.\n   570\u2192        if (!header_to_modify.empty()) {\n   571\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   572\u2192          // The current behavior of appending is by combining entries with the same key,\n   573\u2192          // into one entry. The value of that combined entry is separated by \",\".\n   574\u2192          // TODO(dio): Consider to use addCopy instead.\n   575\u2192          request_headers_->appendCopy(lowercase_key, value);\n   576\u2192        }\n   577\u2192        break;\n   578\u2192      }\n   579\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n   580\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   581\u2192                         key, value);\n   582\u2192        break;\n   583\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n   584\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\",\n   585\u2192                         *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   586\u2192        rejectResponse();\n   587\u2192        return;\n   588\u2192      }\n   589\u2192    }\n   590\u2192\n   591\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   592\u2192                     \"ext_authz filter removed header(s) from the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   593\u2192    for (const auto& key : response->headers_to_remove) {\n   594\u2192      // If the response contains an invalid header to remove, it's the same as trying to remove a\n   595\u2192      // header that doesn't exist, so just ignore it.\n   596\u2192      if (config_->validateMutations() && !Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key)) {\n   597\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n   598\u2192        continue;\n   599\u2192      }\n   600\u2192      // We don't allow removing any :-prefixed headers, nor Host, as removing them would make the\n   601\u2192      // request malformed. checkDecoderHeaderMutation also performs this check, however, so only\n   602\u2192      // perform this check explicitly if decoder header mutation rules is empty.\n   603\u2192      if (!config_->hasDecoderHeaderMutationRules() &&\n   604\u2192          !Http::HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(key)) {\n   605\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n   606\u2192        continue;\n   607\u2192      }\n   608\u2192      // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder header mutation rules.\n   609\u2192      Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\n   610\u2192      switch (config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(CheckOperation::REMOVE, lowercase_key,\n   611\u2192                                                  EMPTY_STRING)) {\n   612\u2192      case CheckResult::OK:\n   613\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n   614\u2192        request_headers_->remove(lowercase_key);\n   615\u2192        break;\n   616\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n   617\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   618\u2192                         key);\n   619\u2192        break;\n   620\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n   621\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   622\u2192                         key);\n   623\u2192        rejectResponse();\n   624\u2192        return;\n   625\u2192      }\n   626\u2192    }\n   627\u2192\n   628\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_add.empty()) {\n   629\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to add to the response:\",\n   630\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add.size());\n   631\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add) {\n   632\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n   633\u2192                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n   634\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n   635\u2192                           value);\n   636\u2192          rejectResponse();\n                                             response->response_headers_to_add.end()};\n   642\u2192    }\n   643\u2192\n   644\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_set.empty()) {\n   645\u2192 694\u2192\n   695\u2192    absl::optional<Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti> modified_query_parameters;\n   696\u2192    if (!response->query_parameters_to_set.empty()) {\n   697\u2192      modified_query_parameters = Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti::parseQueryString(\n   698\u2192          request_headers_->Path()->value().getStringView());\n   699\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   700\u2192          trace, \"ext_authz filter set query parameter(s) on the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   701\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->query_parameters_to_set) {\n response->query_parameters_to_remove) {\n   723\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n   724\u2192        modified_query_parameters->remove(key);\n   725\u2192 (modified_query_parameters) {\n   731\u2192      const auto new_path =\n   732\u2192          modified_query_parameters->replaceQueryString(request_headers_->Path()->value());\n   733\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   734\u2192          trace, \"ext_authz filter modified query parameter(s), using new path for request: {}\",\n   735\u2192          *decoder_callbacks_, new_path);\n   736\u2192 741\u2192    }\n   742\u2192    stats_.ok_.inc();\n   743\u2192    continueDecoding();\n   744\u2192    break;\n   745\u2192  }\n   746\u2192\n   747\u2192  case CheckStatus::Denied: {\n   748\u2192 (cluster_) {\n   753\u2192      config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_denied_);\n   754\u2192      if (config_->chargeClusterResponseStats()) {\n   755\u2192 empty_stat_name,\n   758\u2192                                               enumToInt(response->status_code),\n   759\u2192                                               true,\n   760\u2192 empty_stat_name,\n   763\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\n   764\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\n   765\u2192                                               false};\n   766\u2192        config_->httpContext().codeStats().chargeResponseStat(info, false);\n   767\u2192      }\n   768\u2192    }\n   769\u2192\n   770\u2192    // Check headers are valid.\n   771\u2192    if (config_->validateMutations()) {\n   772\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\n   773\u2192        if (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n   774\u2192            !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value)) {\n   775\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n   776\u2192                           value);\n   777\u2192          rejectResponse();\n   778\u2192          return;\n   779\u2192        }\n   780\u2192      }\n   781\u2192    }\n   782\u2192\n   783\u2192    // setResponseFlag must be called before sendLocalReply\n   784\u2192    decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\n   785\u2192        StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\n   786\u2192    decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n   787\u2192        response->status_code, response->body,\n   788\u2192        [&headers = response->headers_to_set,\n   789\u2192         &callbacks = *decoder_callbacks_](Http::HeaderMap& response_headers) -> void {\n   790\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   791\u2192                           \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the local response:\", callbacks);\n   792\u2192          // Firstly, remove all headers requested by the ext_authz filter, to ensure that they will\n   793\u2192          // override existing headers.\n   794\u2192          for (const auto& [key, _] : headers) {\n   795\u2192            response_headers.remove(Http::LowerCaseString(key));\n   796\u2192          }\n   797\u2192          // Then set all of the requested headers, allowing the same header to be set multiple\n   798\u2192          // times, e.g. `Set-Cookie`.\n   799\u2192          for (const auto& [key, value] : headers) {\n   800\u2192            ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \" '{}':'{}'\", callbacks, key, value);\n   801\u2192            response_headers.addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n   802\u2192          }\n   803\u2192        },\n   804\u2192        absl::nullopt, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzDenied);\n   805\u2192    break;\n   806\u2192  }\n   807\u2192\n   808\u2192  case CheckStatus::Error: {\n   809\u2192    if (cluster_) {\n   810\u2192      config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_error_);\n   811\u2192    }\n   812\u2192    stats_.error_.inc();\n   813\u2192    if (config_->failureModeAllow()) {\n   814\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\",\n   815\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n   816\u2192      stats_.failure_mode_allowed_.inc();\n   817\u2192      if (cluster_) {\n   818\u2192        config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_);\n   819\u2192      }\n   820\u2192      if (config_->failureModeAllowHeaderAdd()) {\n   821\u2192        request_headers_->addReferenceKey(\n   822\u2192            Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Headers::get().EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed, \"true\");\n   823\u2192      }\n   824\u2192      continueDecoding();\n   825\u2192    } else {\n   826\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   827\u2192          trace, \"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error. Response status code: {}\",\n   828\u2192          *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(config_->statusOnError()));\n   829\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\n   830\u2192          StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\n   831\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n   832\u2192          config_->statusOnError(), EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n   833\u2192          Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzError);\n   834\u2192    }\n   835\u2192    break;\n   836\u2192  }\n   837\u2192  }\n   838\u2192}\n   839\u2192\n   840\u2192void Filter::rejectResponse() {\n   841\u2192  const Http::Code status = Http::Code::InternalServerError;\n   842\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter invalidated the response. Response status code: {}\",\n   843\u2192                   *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(status));\n   844\u2192  if (cluster_) {\n   845\u2192    config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_invalid_);\n   846\u2192  }\n   847\u2192  stats_.invalid_.inc();\n   848\u2192  decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\n   849\u2192      StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\n   850\u2192  decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n   851\u2192      status, EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n   852\u2192      Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzInvalid);\n   853\u2192}\n   854\u2192\n   855\u2192bool Filter::isBufferFull(uint64_t num_bytes_processing) const {\n   856\u2192  if (!allow_partial_message_) {\n   857\u2192    return false;\n   858\u2192  }\n   859\u2192\n   860\u2192  uint64_t num_bytes_buffered = num_bytes_processing;\n   861\u2192  const auto* buffer = decoder_callbacks_->decodingBuffer();\n   862\u2192  if (buffer != nullptr) {\n   863\u2192    num_bytes_buffered += buffer->length();\n   864\u2192  }\n   865\u2192\n   866\u2192  return num_bytes_buffered >= max_request_bytes_;\n   867\u2192}\n   868\u2192\n   869\u2192void Filter::continueDecoding() {\n   870\u2192  // After sending the check request, we don't need to buffer the data anymore.\n   871\u2192  buffer_data_ = false;\n   872\u2192\n   873\u2192  filter_return_ = FilterReturn::ContinueDecoding;\n   874\u2192  if (!initiating_call_) {\n   875\u2192    decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n   876\u2192  }\n   877\u2192}\n   878\u2192\n   879\u2192Filter::PerRouteFlags Filter::getPerRouteFlags(const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route) const {\n   880\u2192  if (route == nullptr) {\n   881\u2192    return PerRouteFlags{\n   882\u2192        true /*skip_check_*/,\n   883\u2192        envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings() /*check_settings_*/};\n   884\u2192  }\n   885\u2192\n   886\u2192  const auto* specific_check_settings =\n   887\u2192      Http::Utility::resolveMostSpecificPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_);\n   888\u2192  if (specific_check_settings != nullptr) {\n   889\u2192    return PerRouteFlags{specific_check_settings->disabled(),\n   890\u2192                         specific_check_settings->checkSettings()};\n   891\u2192  }\n   892\u2192\n   893\u2192  return PerRouteFlags{\n   894\u2192      false /*skip_check_*/,\n   895\u2192      envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings() /*check_settings_*/};\n   896\u2192}\n   897\u2192\n   898\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   899\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\n   900\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   901\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   902\u2192"

\"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    20\u2192namespace HttpFilters {\n    21\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192Http::FilterFactoryCb ExtAuthzFilterConfig::createFilterFactoryFromProtoWithServerContextTyped(\n    24\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& proto_config,\n    25\u2192    const std::string& stats_prefix, Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& server_context) {\n    26\u2192  const auto filter_config = std::make_shared<FilterConfig>(proto_config, server_context.scope(),\n    27\u2192                                                            stats_prefix, server_context);\n    28\u2192  // The callback is created in main thread and executed in worker thread, variables except factory\n    29\u2192  // context must be captured by value into the callback.\n    30\u2192  Http::FilterFactoryCb callback;\n    31\u2192  if (proto_config.has_http_service()) {\n    32\u2192    // Raw HTTP client.\n    33\u2192    const uint32_t timeout_ms = PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.http_service().server_uri(),\n    34\u2192                                                           timeout, DefaultTimeout);\n    35\u2192    const auto client_config =\n    36\u2192        std::make_shared<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientConfig>(\n    37\u2192            proto_config, timeout_ms, proto_config.http_service().path_prefix(), server_context);\n    38\u2192    callback = [filter_config = std::move(filter_config), client_config,\n    39\u2192                &server_context](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) {\n    40\u2192      auto client = std::make_unique<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RawHttpClientImpl>(\n    41\u2192          server_context.clusterManager(), client_config);\n    42\u2192      callbacks.addStreamFilter(std::make_shared<Filter>(filter_config, std::move(client)));\n    43\u2192    };\n    44\u2192  } else {\n    45\u2192    // gRPC client.\n    46\u2192    const uint32_t timeout_ms =\n    47\u2192        PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.grpc_service(), timeout, DefaultTimeout);\n    48\u2192    THROW_IF_NOT_OK(Config::Utility::checkTransportVersion(proto_config));\n    49\u2192    Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey config_with_hash_key =\n    50\u2192        Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey(proto_config.grpc_service());\n    51\u2192    callback = [&server_context, filter_config = std::move(filter_config), timeout_ms,\n    52\u2192                config_with_hash_key](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) {\n    53\u2192      auto client_or_error = server_context.clusterManager()\n    54\u2192                                 .grpcAsyncClientManager()\n    55\u2192                                 .getOrCreateRawAsyncClientWithHashKey(\n    56\u2192                                     config_with_hash_key, server_context.scope(), true);\n    57\u2192      THROW_IF_NOT_OK_REF(client_or_error.status());\n    58\u2192      auto client = std::make_unique<Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::GrpcClientImpl>(\n    59\u2192          client_or_error.value(), std::chrono::milliseconds(timeout_ms));\n    60\u2192      callbacks.addStreamFilter(std::make_shared<Filter>(filter_config, std::move(client)));\n    61\u2192    };\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192  return callback;\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfigConstSharedPtr\n    67\u2192ExtAuthzFilterConfig::createRouteSpecificFilterConfigTyped(\n    68\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthzPerRoute& proto_config,\n    69\u2192    Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext&, ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor&) {\n    70\u2192  return std::make_shared<FilterConfigPerRoute>(proto_config);\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192/**\n    74\u2192 * Static registration for the external authorization filter. @see RegisterFactory.\n    75\u2192 */\n    76\u2192LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY(ExtAuthzFilterConfig, Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory,\n    77\u2192                        \"envoy.ext_authz\");\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n    80\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\n    81\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n    82\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n    83\u2192"

\"envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/runtime/runtime.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"envoy/stats/scope.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"envoy/stats/stats_macros.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\"\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192#include \"source/common/common/matchers.h\"\n    18\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    19\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    22\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    23\u2192namespace NetworkFilters {\n    24\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192/**\n    27\u2192 * All tcp external authorization stats. @see stats_macros.h\n    28\u2192 */\n    29\u2192#define ALL_TCP_EXT_AUTHZ_STATS(COUNTER, GAUGE)                                                    \\\n    30\u2192  COUNTER(cx_closed)                                                                               \\\n    31\u2192  COUNTER(denied)                                                                                  \\\n    32\u2192  COUNTER(error)                                                                                   \\\n    33\u2192  COUNTER(failure_mode_allowed)                                                                    \\\n    34\u2192  COUNTER(ok)                                                                                      \\\n    35\u2192  COUNTER(total)                                                                                   \\\n    36\u2192  COUNTER(disabled)                                                                                \\\n    37\u2192  GAUGE(active, Accumulate)\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192/**\n    40\u2192 * Struct definition for all external authorization stats. @see stats_macros.h\n    41\u2192 */\n    42\u2192struct InstanceStats {\n    43\u2192  ALL_TCP_EXT_AUTHZ_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT, GENERATE_GAUGE_STRUCT)\n    44\u2192};\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192/**\n    47\u2192 * Global configuration for ExtAuthz filter.\n    48\u2192 */\n    49\u2192class Config {\n    50\u2192  using LabelsMap = Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>;\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192public:\n    53\u2192  Config(const envoy::extensions::filters::network::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\n    54\u2192         Stats::Scope& scope, Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& context)\n    55\u2192      : stats_(generateStats(config.stat_prefix(), scope)),\n    56\u2192        failure_mode_allow_(config.failure_mode_allow()),\n    57\u2192        include_peer_certificate_(config.include_peer_certificate()),\n    58\u2192        include_tls_session_(config.include_tls_session()),\n    59\u2192        filter_enabled_metadata_(\n    60\u2192            config.has_filter_enabled_metadata()\n    61\u2192                ? absl::optional<Matchers::MetadataMatcher>(\n    62\u2192                      Matchers::MetadataMatcher(config.filter_enabled_metadata(), context))\n    63\u2192                : absl::nullopt) {\n    64\u2192    auto labels_key_it =\n    65\u2192        context.bootstrap().node().metadata().fields().find(config.bootstrap_metadata_labels_key());\n    66\u2192    if (labels_key_it != context.bootstrap().node().metadata().fields().end()) {\n    67\u2192      for (const auto& labels_it : labels_key_it->second.struct_value().fields()) {\n    68\u2192        destination_labels_[labels_it.first] = labels_it.second.string_value();\n    69\u2192      }\n    70\u2192    }\n    71\u2192  }\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192  const InstanceStats& stats() { return stats_; }\n    74\u2192  bool failureModeAllow() const { return failure_mode_allow_; }\n    75\u2192  void setFailModeAllow(bool value) { failure_mode_allow_ = value; }\n    76\u2192  bool includePeerCertificate() const { return include_peer_certificate_; }\n    77\u2192  bool includeTLSSession() const { return include_tls_session_; }\n    78\u2192  const LabelsMap& destinationLabels() const { return destination_labels_; }\n    79\u2192  bool filterEnabledMetadata(const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& metadata) const {\n    80\u2192    return filter_enabled_metadata_.has_value() ? filter_enabled_metadata_->match(metadata) : true;\n    81\u2192  }\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192private:\n    84\u2192  static InstanceStats generateStats(const std::string& name, Stats::Scope& scope);\n    85\u2192  const InstanceStats stats_;\n    86\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow_;\n    87\u2192  LabelsMap destination_labels_;\n    88\u2192  const bool include_peer_certificate_;\n    89\u2192  const bool include_tls_session_;\n    90\u2192  const absl::optional<Matchers::MetadataMatcher> filter_enabled_metadata_;\n    91\u2192};\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192using ConfigSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<Config>;\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192/**\n    96\u2192 * ExtAuthz filter instance. This filter will call the Authorization service with the given\n    97\u2192 * configuration parameters. If the authorization service returns an error or a deny the\n    98\u2192 * connection will be closed without any further filters being called. Otherwise all buffered\n    99\u2192 * data will be released to further filters.\n   100\u2192 */\n   101\u2192class Filter : public Network::ReadFilter,\n   102\u2192               public Network::ConnectionCallbacks,\n   103\u2192               public Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks {\n   104\u2192  using LabelsMap = std::map<std::string, std::string>;\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192public:\n   107\u2192  Filter(ConfigSharedPtr config, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr&& client)\n   108\u2192      : config_(config), client_(std::move(client)) {}\n   109\u2192  ~Filter() override = default;\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192  // Network::ReadFilter\n   112\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   113\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onNewConnection() override;\n   114\u2192  void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override {\n   115\u2192    filter_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n   116\u2192    filter_callbacks_->connection().addConnectionCallbacks(*this);\n   117\u2192  }\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\n   120\u2192  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override;\n   121\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {}\n   122\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override {}\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192  // ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks\n   125\u2192  void onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&&) override;\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192private:\n   128\u2192  // State of this filter's communication with the external authorization service.\n   129\u2192  // The filter has either not started calling the external service, in the middle of calling\n   130\u2192  // it or has completed.\n   131\u2192  enum class Status { NotStarted, Calling, Complete };\n   132\u2192  // FilterReturn is used to capture what the return code should be to the filter chain.\n   133\u2192  // if this filter is either in the middle of calling the external service or the result is denied\n   134\u2192  // then the filter chain should stop. Otherwise the filter chain can continue to the next filter.\n   135\u2192  enum class FilterReturn { Stop, Continue };\n   136\u2192  void callCheck();\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192  bool filterEnabled(const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& metadata) {\n   139\u2192    return config_->filterEnabledMetadata(metadata);\n   140\u2192  }\n   141\u2192  absl::optional<MonotonicTime> start_time_;\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192  ConfigSharedPtr config_;\n   144\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr client_;\n   145\u2192  Network::ReadFilterCallbacks* filter_callbacks_{};\n   146\u2192  Status status_{Status::NotStarted};\n   147\u2192  FilterReturn filter_return_{FilterReturn::Stop};\n   148\u2192  // Used to identify if the callback to onComplete() is synchronous (on the stack) or asynchronous.\n   149\u2192  bool calling_check_{};\n   150\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest check_request_{};\n   151\u2192};\n   152\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   153\u2192} // namespace NetworkFilters\n   154\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   155\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   156\u2192"

"     1\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\n     4\u2192#include <cstdint>\n     5\u2192#include <string>\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/stats/scope.h\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/tracing/http_tracer_impl.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/network/well_known_names.h\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    14\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    15\u2192namespace NetworkFilters {\n    16\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192InstanceStats Config::generateStats(const std::string& name, Stats::Scope& scope) {\n    19\u2192  const std::string final_prefix = fmt::format(\"ext_authz.{}.\", name);\n    20\u2192  return {ALL_TCP_EXT_AUTHZ_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix),\n    21\u2192                                  POOL_GAUGE_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\n    22\u2192}\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192void Filter::callCheck() {\n    25\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::createTcpCheck(\n    26\u2192      filter_callbacks_, check_request_, config_->includePeerCertificate(),\n    27\u2192      config_->includeTLSSession(), config_->destinationLabels());\n    28\u2192  // Store start time of ext_authz filter call\n    29\u2192  start_time_ = filter_callbacks_->connection().dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime();\n    30\u2192  status_ = Status::Calling;\n    31\u2192  config_->stats().active_.inc();\n    32\u2192  config_->stats().total_.inc();\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192  calling_check_ = true;\n    35\u2192  client_->check(*this, check_request_, Tracing::NullSpan::instance(),\n    36\u2192                 filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo());\n    37\u2192  calling_check_ = false;\n    38\u2192}\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192Network::FilterStatus Filter::onData(Buffer::Instance&, bool /* end_stream */) {\n    41\u2192  if (!filterEnabled(filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().dynamicMetadata())) {\n    42\u2192    config_->stats().disabled_.inc();\n    43\u2192    return Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\n    44\u2192  }\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192  if (status_ == Status::NotStarted) {\n    47\u2192    // By waiting to invoke the check at onData() the call to authorization service will have\n    48\u2192    // sufficient information to fill out the checkRequest_.\n    49\u2192    callCheck();\n    50\u2192  }\n    51\u2192  return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::Stop ? Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration\n    52\u2192                                              : Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\n    53\u2192}\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192Network::FilterStatus Filter::onNewConnection() {\n    56\u2192  // Wait till onData() happens.\n    57\u2192  return Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\n    58\u2192}\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192void Filter::onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) {\n    61\u2192  if (event == Network::ConnectionEvent::RemoteClose ||\n    62\u2192      event == Network::ConnectionEvent::LocalClose) {\n    63\u2192    if (status_ == Status::Calling) {\n    64\u2192      // Make sure that any pending request in the client is cancelled. This will be NOP if the\n    65\u2192      // request already completed.\n    66\u2192      client_->cancel();\n    67\u2192      config_->stats().active_.dec();\n    68\u2192    }\n    69\u2192  }\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\n    73\u2192  status_ = Status::Complete;\n    74\u2192  config_->stats().active_.dec();\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  switch (response->status) {\n    77\u2192  case Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::OK:\n    78\u2192    config_->stats().ok_.inc();\n    79\u2192    // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\n    80\u2192    if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n    81\u2192      ProtobufWkt::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\n    82\u2192      auto duration = filter_callbacks_->connection().dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() -\n    83\u2192                      start_time_.value();\n    84\u2192      ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\n    85\u2192          std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\n    86\u2192      (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\"ext_authz_duration\"] =\n    87\u2192          ext_authz_duration_value;\n    88\u2192    }\n    89\u2192    break;\n    90\u2192  case Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Error:\n    91\u2192    config_->stats().error_.inc();\n    92\u2192    break;\n    93\u2192  case Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Denied:\n    94\u2192    config_->stats().denied_.inc();\n    95\u2192    break;\n    96\u2192  }\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\n   101\u2192        NetworkFilterNames::get().ExtAuthorization, response->dynamic_metadata);\n   102\u2192  }\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192  // Fail open only if configured to do so and if the check status was a error.\n   105\u2192  if (response->status == Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Denied ||\n   106\u2192      (response->status == Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Error &&\n   107\u2192       !config_->failureModeAllow())) {\n   108\u2192    config_->stats().cx_closed_.inc();\n   109\u2192    filter_callbacks_->connection().close(Network::ConnectionCloseType::NoFlush, \"ext_authz_close\");\n   110\u2192    filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\n   111\u2192        StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\n   112\u2192    filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(\n   113\u2192        response->status == Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Denied\n   114\u2192            ? Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzDenied\n   115\u2192            : Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzError);\n   116\u2192  } else {\n   117\u2192    // Let the filter chain continue.\n   118\u2192    filter_return_ = FilterReturn::Continue;\n   119\u2192    if (config_->failureModeAllow() &&\n   120\u2192        response->status == Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Error) {\n   121\u2192      // Status is Error and yet we are configured to allow traffic. Click a counter.\n   122\u2192      config_->stats().failure_mode_allowed_.inc();\n   123\u2192    }\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192    // We can get completion inline, so only call continue if that isn't happening.\n   126\u2192    if (!calling_check_) {\n   127\u2192      filter_callbacks_->continueReading();\n   128\u2192    }\n   129\u2192  }\n   130\u2192}\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   133\u2192} // namespace NetworkFilters\n   134\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   135\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   136\u2192"

"     1\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\n     4\u2192#include <string>\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/grpc_service.pb.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.validate.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/registry/registry.h\"\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192#include \"source/common/config/utility.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"source/common/protobuf/utility.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    20\u2192namespace NetworkFilters {\n    21\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192Network::FilterFactoryCb ExtAuthzConfigFactory::createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped(\n    24\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::network::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& proto_config,\n    25\u2192    Server::Configuration::FactoryContext& context) {\n    26\u2192  ConfigSharedPtr ext_authz_config =\n    27\u2192      std::make_shared<Config>(proto_config, context.scope(), context.serverFactoryContext());\n    28\u2192  const uint32_t timeout_ms = PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.grpc_service(), timeout, 200);\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192  THROW_IF_NOT_OK(Envoy::Config::Utility::checkTransportVersion(proto_config));\n    31\u2192  return [grpc_service = proto_config.grpc_service(), &context, ext_authz_config,\n    32\u2192          timeout_ms](Network::FilterManager& filter_manager) -> void {\n    33\u2192    auto factory_or_error = context.serverFactoryContext()\n    34\u2192                                .clusterManager()\n    35\u2192                                .grpcAsyncClientManager()\n    36\u2192                                .factoryForGrpcService(grpc_service, context.scope(), true);\n    37\u2192    THROW_IF_NOT_OK_REF(factory_or_error.status());\n    38\u2192    auto client = std::make_unique<Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::GrpcClientImpl>(\n    39\u2192        factory_or_error.value()->createUncachedRawAsyncClient(),\n    40\u2192        std::chrono::milliseconds(timeout_ms));\n    41\u2192    filter_manager.addReadFilter(Network::ReadFilterSharedPtr{\n    42\u2192        std::make_shared<Filter>(ext_authz_config, std::move(client))});\n    43\u2192  };\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192/**\n    47\u2192 * Static registration for the external authorization filter. @see RegisterFactory.\n    48\u2192 */\n    49\u2192LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY(ExtAuthzConfigFactory,\n    50\u2192                        Server::Configuration::NamedNetworkFilterConfigFactory, \"envoy.ext_authz\");\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n    53\u2192} // namespace NetworkFilters\n    54\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n    55\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n    56\u2192"

"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\n     4\u2192#include <memory>\n     5\u2192#include <string>\n     6\u2192#include <vector>\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/common/pure.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/http/codes.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/stream_info/stream_info.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192#include \"source/common/http/headers.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"source/common/singleton/const_singleton.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    20\u2192namespace Filters {\n    21\u2192namespace Common {\n    22\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192/**\n    25\u2192 * Constant values used for tracing metadata.\n    26\u2192 */\n    27\u2192struct TracingConstantValues {\n    28\u2192  const std::string TraceStatus = \"ext_authz_status\";\n    29\u2192  const std::string TraceUnauthz = \"ext_authz_unauthorized\";\n    30\u2192  const std::string TraceOk = \"ext_authz_ok\";\n    31\u2192  const std::string HttpStatus = \"ext_authz_http_status\";\n    32\u2192};\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192using TracingConstants = ConstSingleton<TracingConstantValues>;\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192/**\n    37\u2192 * Possible constant response code details values for a check call.\n    38\u2192 */\n    39\u2192struct ResponseCodeDetailsValues {\n    40\u2192  // The ext_authz filter denied the downstream request/connection.\n    41\u2192  const std::string AuthzDenied = \"ext_authz_denied\";\n    42\u2192  // The ext_authz filter encountered a failure, and was configured to fail-closed.\n    43\u2192  const std::string AuthzError = \"ext_authz_error\";\n    44\u2192  // The ext_authz filter invalidated the CheckResponse and dropped the client request.\n    45\u2192  const std::string AuthzInvalid = \"ext_authz_invalid\";\n    46\u2192};\n    47\u2192using ResponseCodeDetails = ConstSingleton<ResponseCodeDetailsValues>;\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192/**\n    50\u2192 * Constant auth related HTTP headers. All lower case. This group of headers can\n    51\u2192 * contain prefix override headers.\n    52\u2192 */\n    53\u2192class HeaderValues {\n    54\u2192public:\n    55\u2192  const char* prefix() const { return ThreadSafeSingleton<Http::PrefixValue>::get().prefix(); }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthPartialBody{absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-partial-body\")};\n    58\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove{\n    59\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-headers-to-remove\")};\n    60\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed{\n    61\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-failure-mode-allowed\")};\n    62\u2192};\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192using Headers = ConstSingleton<HeaderValues>;\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192/**\n    67\u2192 * Possible async results for a check call.\n    68\u2192 */\n    69\u2192enum class CheckStatus {\n    70\u2192  // The request is authorized.\n    71\u2192  OK,\n    72\u2192  // The authz service could not be queried.\n    73\u2192  Error,\n    74\u2192  // The request is denied.\n    75\u2192  Denied\n    76\u2192};\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192using UnsafeHeader = std::pair<std::string, std::string>;\n    79\u2192using UnsafeHeaderVector = std::vector<UnsafeHeader>;\n    80\u2192/**\n    81\u2192 * Authorization response object for a RequestCallback.\n    82\u2192 *\n    83\u2192 * Header mutations and query parameter mutations are not expected to already be validated by the\n    84\u2192 * client before packing them into this struct (hence they are \"unsafe\").\n    85\u2192 */\n    86\u2192struct Response {\n    87\u2192  // Call status.\n    88\u2192  CheckStatus status;\n    89\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, that will be optionally appended\n    90\u2192  // to the request to the upstream server.\n    91\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_append;\n    92\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set\n    93\u2192  // (using \"setCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n    94\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_set;\n    95\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n    96\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n    97\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_add;\n    98\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n    99\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n   100\u2192  // responses.\n   101\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add;\n   102\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n   103\u2192  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses.\n   104\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_set;\n   105\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n   106\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n   107\u2192  // responses only if the headers were not returned from the authz server.\n   108\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent;\n   109\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n   110\u2192  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses\n   111\u2192  // only if the headers were returned from the authz server.\n   112\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists;\n   113\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers consumed by the authorization server, will be removed\n   114\u2192  // from the request to the upstream server.\n   115\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\n   116\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to be set (possibly overwritten) on the\n   117\u2192  // request to the upstream server.\n   118\u2192  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector query_parameters_to_set;\n   119\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to remove from the request to the upstream server.\n   120\u2192  std::vector<std::string> query_parameters_to_remove;\n   121\u2192  // Optional http body used only on denied response.\n   122\u2192  std::string body;\n   123\u2192  // Optional http status used only on denied response.\n   124\u2192  Http::Code status_code{};\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192  // A set of metadata returned by the authorization server, that will be emitted as filter's\n   127\u2192  // dynamic metadata that other filters can leverage.\n   128\u2192  ProtobufWkt::Struct dynamic_metadata;\n   129\u2192};\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192using ResponsePtr = std::unique_ptr<Response>;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192/**\n   134\u2192 * Async callbacks used during check() calls.\n   135\u2192 */\n   136\u2192class RequestCallbacks {\n   137\u2192public:\n   138\u2192  virtual ~RequestCallbacks() = default;\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  /**\n   141\u2192   * Called when a check request is complete. The resulting ResponsePtr is supplied.\n   142\u2192   */\n   143\u2192  virtual void onComplete(ResponsePtr&& response) PURE;\n   144\u2192};\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192class Client {\n   147\u2192public:\n   148\u2192  // Destructor\n   149\u2192  virtual ~Client() = default;\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  /**\n   152\u2192   * Cancel an inflight Check request.\n   153\u2192   */\n   154\u2192  virtual void cancel() PURE;\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  /**\n   157\u2192   * Request a check call to an external authorization service which can use the\n   158\u2192   * passed request parameters to make a permit/deny decision.\n   159\u2192   * @param callback supplies the completion callbacks.\n   160\u2192   *        NOTE: The callback may happen within the calling stack.\n   161\u2192   * @param request is the proto message with the attributes of the specific payload.\n   162\u2192   * @param parent_span source for generating an egress child span as part of the trace.\n   163\u2192   * @param stream_info supplies the client's stream info.\n   164\u2192   */\n   165\u2192  virtual void check(RequestCallbacks& callback,\n   166\u2192                     const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   167\u2192                     Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) PURE;\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  /**\n   170\u2192   * Returns streamInfo of the current request if possible. By default just return a nullptr.\n   171\u2192   */\n   172\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const { return nullptr; }\n   173\u2192};\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192using ClientPtr = std::unique_ptr<Client>;\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   178\u2192} // namespace Common\n   179\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   180\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   181\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   182\u2192"

"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\n     4\u2192#include <cstdint>\n     5\u2192#include <memory>\n     6\u2192#include <string>\n     7\u2192#include <vector>\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/grpc/async_client.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/grpc/async_client_manager.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"envoy/http/filter.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"envoy/http/protocol.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"envoy/network/address.h\"\n    17\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n    18\u2192#include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n    19\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n    20\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n    21\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192#include \"source/common/grpc/typed_async_client.h\"\n    24\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h\"\n    25\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    28\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    29\u2192namespace Filters {\n    30\u2192namespace Common {\n    31\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192using ExtAuthzAsyncCallbacks = Grpc::AsyncRequestCallbacks<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>;\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192/*\n    36\u2192 * This client implementation is used when the Ext_Authz filter needs to communicate with an gRPC\n    37\u2192 * authorization server. Unlike the HTTP client, the gRPC allows the server to define response\n    38\u2192 * objects which contain the HTTP attributes to be sent to the upstream or to the downstream client.\n    39\u2192 * The gRPC client does not rewrite path. NOTE: We create gRPC client for each filter stack instead\n    40\u2192 * of a client per thread. That is ok since this is unary RPC and the cost of doing this is minimal.\n    41\u2192 */\n    42\u2192class GrpcClientImpl : public Client,\n    43\u2192                       public ExtAuthzAsyncCallbacks,\n    44\u2192                       public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::ext_authz> {\n    45\u2192public:\n    46\u2192  GrpcClientImpl(const Grpc::RawAsyncClientSharedPtr& async_client,\n    47\u2192                 const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& timeout);\n    48\u2192  ~GrpcClientImpl() override;\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192  // ExtAuthz::Client\n    51\u2192  void cancel() override;\n    52\u2192  void check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks, const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n    53\u2192             Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) override;\n    54\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const override {\n    55\u2192    return request_ ? &request_->streamInfo() : nullptr;\n    56\u2192  }\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192  // Grpc::AsyncRequestCallbacks\n    59\u2192  void onCreateInitialMetadata(Http::RequestHeaderMap&) override {}\n    60\u2192  void onSuccess(std::unique_ptr<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>&& response,\n    61\u2192                 Tracing::Span& span) override;\n    62\u2192  void onFailure(Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus status, const std::string& message,\n    63\u2192                 Tracing::Span& span) override;\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192private:\n    66\u2192  Grpc::AsyncClient<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest, envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>\n    67\u2192      async_client_;\n    68\u2192  Grpc::AsyncRequest* request_{};\n    69\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> timeout_;\n    70\u2192  RequestCallbacks* callbacks_{};\n    71\u2192  const Protobuf::MethodDescriptor& service_method_;\n    72\u2192};\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192using GrpcClientImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<GrpcClientImpl>;\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n    77\u2192} // namespace Common\n    78\u2192} // namespace Filters\n    79\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n    80\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n    81\u2192"
