"     1\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"source/common/grpc/async_client_impl.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"source/common/http/headers.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/network/utility.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/protobuf/protobuf.h\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    14\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    15\u2192namespace Filters {\n    16\u2192namespace Common {\n    17\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192void copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(\n    20\u2192    ResponsePtr& response,\n    21\u2192    const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::core::v3::HeaderValueOption>& headers) {\n    22\u2192  for (const auto& header : headers) {\n    23\u2192    if (header.append().value()) {\n    24\u2192      response->headers_to_append.emplace_back(header.header().key(), header.header().value());\n    25\u2192    } else {\n    26\u2192      response->headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(), header.header().value());\n    27\u2192    }\n    28\u2192  }\n    29\u2192}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192void copyOkResponseMutations(ResponsePtr& response,\n    32\u2192                             const envoy::service::auth::v3::OkHttpResponse& ok_response) {\n    33\u2192  copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(response, ok_response.headers());\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192  for (const auto& header : ok_response.response_headers_to_add()) {\n    36\u2192    if (header.has_append()) {\n    37\u2192      if (header.append().value()) {\n    38\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    39\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    40\u2192      } else {\n    41\u2192        response->response_headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    42\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    43\u2192      }\n    44\u2192    } else {\n    45\u2192      switch (header.append_action()) {\n    46\u2192        PANIC_ON_PROTO_ENUM_SENTINEL_VALUES;\n    47\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::APPEND_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD:\n    48\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    49\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    50\u2192        break;\n    51\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::ADD_IF_ABSENT:\n    52\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    53\u2192                                                                 header.header().value());\n    54\u2192        break;\n    55\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS:\n    56\u2192        response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    57\u2192                                                                       header.header().value());\n    58\u2192        break;\n    59\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD:\n    60\u2192        response->response_headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    61\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    62\u2192        break;\n    63\u2192      }\n    64\u2192    }\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192  response->headers_to_remove = std::vector<std::string>{ok_response.headers_to_remove().begin(),\n    68\u2192                                                         ok_response.headers_to_remove().end()};\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192  for (const auto& query_parameter : ok_response.query_parameters_to_set()) {\n    71\u2192    response->query_parameters_to_set.emplace_back(query_parameter.key(), query_parameter.value());\n    72\u2192  }\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192  response->query_parameters_to_remove =\n    75\u2192      std::vector<std::string>{ok_response.query_parameters_to_remove().begin(),\n    76\u2192                               ok_response.query_parameters_to_remove().end()};\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192GrpcClientImpl::GrpcClientImpl(const Grpc::RawAsyncClientSharedPtr& async_client,\n    80\u2192                               const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& timeout)\n    81\u2192    : async_client_(async_client), timeout_(timeout),\n    82\u2192      service_method_(*Protobuf::DescriptorPool::generated_pool()->FindMethodByName(\n    83\u2192          \"envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization.Check\")) {}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192GrpcClientImpl::~GrpcClientImpl() { ASSERT(!callbacks_); }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::cancel() {\n    88\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ != nullptr);\n    89\u2192  request_->cancel();\n    90\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n    91\u2192}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks,\n    94\u2192                           const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n    95\u2192                           Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) {\n    96\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr);\n    97\u2192  callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n    98\u2192  Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions options;\n    99\u2192  options.setTimeout(timeout_);\n   100\u2192  options.setParentContext(Http::AsyncClient::ParentContext{&stream_info});\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"Sending CheckRequest: {}\", request.DebugString());\n   103\u2192  request_ = async_client_->send(service_method_, request, *this, parent_span, options);\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::onSuccess(std::unique_ptr<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>&& response,\n   107\u2192                               Tracing::Span& span) {\n   108\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"Received CheckResponse: {}\", response->DebugString());\n   109\u2192  ResponsePtr authz_response = std::make_unique<Response>(Response{});\n   110\u2192  if (response->status().code() == Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Ok) {\n   111\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, TracingConstants::get().TraceOk);\n   112\u2192    authz_response->status = CheckStatus::OK;\n   113\u2192    if (response->has_ok_response()) {\n   114\u2192      const auto& ok_response = response->ok_response();\n   115\u2192      copyOkResponseMutations(authz_response, ok_response);\n   116\u2192    }\n   117\u2192  } else {\n   118\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, TracingConstants::get().TraceUnauthz);\n   119\u2192    authz_response->status = CheckStatus::Denied;\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    // The default HTTP status code for denied response is 403 Forbidden.\n   122\u2192    authz_response->status_code = Http::Code::Forbidden;\n   123\u2192    if (response->has_denied_response()) {\n   124\u2192      copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(authz_response, response->denied_response().headers());\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192      const uint32_t status_code = response->denied_response().status().code();\n   127\u2192      if (status_code > 0) {\n   128\u2192        authz_response->status_code = static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code);\n   129\u2192      }\n   130\u2192      authz_response->body = response->denied_response().body();\n   131\u2192    }\n   132\u2192  }\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192  // OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata is deprecated. Until OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata is\n   135\u2192  // removed, it overrides dynamic_metadata field of the outer check response.\n   136\u2192  if (response->has_ok_response() && response->ok_response().has_dynamic_metadata()) {\n   137\u2192    authz_response->dynamic_metadata = response->ok_response().dynamic_metadata();\n   138\u2192  } else {\n   139\u2192    authz_response->dynamic_metadata = response->dynamic_metadata();\n   140\u2192  }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::move(authz_response));\n   143\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   144\u2192}\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::onFailure(Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus status, const std::string&,\n   147\u2192                               Tracing::Span&) {\n   148\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"CheckRequest call failed with status: {}\",\n   149\u2192            Grpc::Utility::grpcStatusToString(status));\n   150\u2192  ASSERT(status != Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Ok);\n   151\u2192  Response response{};\n   152\u2192  response.status = CheckStatus::Error;\n   153\u2192  response.status_code = Http::Code::Forbidden;\n   154\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(response));\n   155\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   156\u2192}\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   159\u2192} // namespace Common\n   160\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   161\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   162\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   163\u2192"

"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\"\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/type/matcher/v3/string.pb.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/common/logger.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/common/matchers.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"source/common/router/header_parser.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    17\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    18\u2192namespace Filters {\n    19\u2192namespace Common {\n    20\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192/**\n    23\u2192 * HTTP client configuration for the HTTP authorization (ext_authz) filter.\n    24\u2192 */\n    25\u2192class ClientConfig {\n    26\u2192public:\n    27\u2192  ClientConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\n    28\u2192               uint32_t timeout, absl::string_view path_prefix,\n    29\u2192               Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192  /**\n    32\u2192   * Returns the name of the authorization cluster.\n    33\u2192   */\n    34\u2192  const std::string& cluster() { return cluster_name_; }\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192  /**\n    37\u2192   * Returns the authorization request path prefix.\n    38\u2192   */\n    39\u2192  const std::string& pathPrefix() { return path_prefix_; }\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192  /**\n    42\u2192   * Returns authorization request timeout.\n    43\u2192   */\n    44\u2192  const std::chrono::milliseconds& timeout() const { return timeout_; }\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192  /**\n    47\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\n    48\u2192   * should be send back to the client.\n    49\u2192   */\n    50\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& clientHeaderMatchers() const { return client_header_matchers_; }\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192  /**\n    53\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\n    54\u2192   * should be send back to the client on a successful (i.e. non-denied) response.\n    55\u2192   */\n    56\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers() const {\n    57\u2192    return client_header_on_success_matchers_;\n    58\u2192  }\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192  /**\n    61\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the headers to emit as dynamic metadata.\n    62\u2192   */\n    63\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& dynamicMetadataMatchers() const { return to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_; }\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192  /**\n    66\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\n    67\u2192   * should be send to an the upstream server.\n    68\u2192   */\n    69\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& upstreamHeaderMatchers() const { return upstream_header_matchers_; }\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192  /**\n    72\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\n    73\u2192   * should be sent to the upstream server. The same header keys will be appended, instead of\n    74\u2192   * be replaced.\n    75\u2192   */\n    76\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& upstreamHeaderToAppendMatchers() const {\n    77\u2192    return upstream_header_to_append_matchers_;\n    78\u2192  }\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  /**\n    81\u2192   * Returns the name used for tracing.\n    82\u2192   */\n    83\u2192  const std::string& tracingName() { return tracing_name_; }\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  /**\n    86\u2192   * Returns the configured request header parser.\n    87\u2192   */\n    88\u2192  const Router::HeaderParser& requestHeaderParser() const { return *request_headers_parser_; }\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  /**\n    91\u2192   * Returns whether or not to encode raw headers (i.e. use headers_map instead of headers field).\n    92\u2192   */\n    93\u2192  bool encodeRawHeaders() const { return encode_raw_headers_; }\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192private:\n    96\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr toClientMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n    97\u2192                                           Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n    98\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr\n    99\u2192  toClientMatchersOnSuccess(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   100\u2192                            Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n   101\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr\n   102\u2192  toDynamicMetadataMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   103\u2192                            Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n   104\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr\n   105\u2192  toUpstreamMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   106\u2192                     Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr request_header_matchers_;\n   109\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr client_header_matchers_;\n   110\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr client_header_on_success_matchers_;\n   111\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_;\n   112\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr upstream_header_matchers_;\n   113\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr upstream_header_to_append_matchers_;\n   114\u2192  const std::string cluster_name_;\n   115\u2192  const std::chrono::milliseconds timeout_;\n   116\u2192  const std::string path_prefix_;\n   117\u2192  const std::string tracing_name_;\n   118\u2192  Router::HeaderParserPtr request_headers_parser_;\n   119\u2192  const bool encode_raw_headers_;\n   120\u2192};\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192using ClientConfigSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<ClientConfig>;\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192/**\n   125\u2192 * This client implementation is used when the Ext_Authz filter needs to communicate with an\n   126\u2192 * HTTP authorization server. Unlike the gRPC client that allows the server to define the\n   127\u2192 * response object, in the HTTP client, all headers and body provided in the response are\n   128\u2192 * dispatched to the downstream, and some headers to the upstream. The HTTP client also allows\n   129\u2192 * setting a path prefix witch is not available for gRPC.\n   130\u2192 */\n   131\u2192class RawHttpClientImpl : public Client,\n   132\u2192                          public Http::AsyncClient::Callbacks,\n   133\u2192                          Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n   134\u2192public:\n   135\u2192  explicit RawHttpClientImpl(Upstream::ClusterManager& cm, ClientConfigSharedPtr config);\n   136\u2192  ~RawHttpClientImpl() override;\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192  // ExtAuthz::Client\n   139\u2192  void cancel() override;\n   140\u2192  void check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks, const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   141\u2192             Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) override;\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192  // Http::AsyncClient::Callbacks\n   144\u2192  void onSuccess(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&, Http::ResponseMessagePtr&& message) override;\n   145\u2192  void onFailure(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&,\n   146\u2192                 Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason reason) override;\n   147\u2192  void onBeforeFinalizeUpstreamSpan(Tracing::Span& span,\n   148\u2192                                    const Http::ResponseHeaderMap* response_headers) override;\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192private:\n   151\u2192  ResponsePtr toResponse(Http::ResponseMessagePtr message);\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192  Upstream::ClusterManager& cm_;\n   154\u2192  ClientConfigSharedPtr config_;\n   155\u2192  Http::AsyncClient::Request* request_{};\n   156\u2192  RequestCallbacks* callbacks_{};\n   157\u2192};\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   160\u2192} // namespace Common\n   161\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   162\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   163\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   164\u2192"

"     1\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\"\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/type/matcher/v3/string.pb.h\"\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"source/common/common/fmt.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/common/matchers.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/http/async_client_impl.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"source/common/http/codes.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"source/common/runtime/runtime_features.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192#include \"absl/strings/str_cat.h\"\n    17\u2192#include \"absl/types/optional.h\"\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    20\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    21\u2192namespace Filters {\n    22\u2192namespace Common {\n    23\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192namespace {\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192// Static header map used for creating authorization requests.\n    28\u2192const Http::HeaderMap& lengthZeroHeader() {\n    29\u2192  static const auto headers = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(\n    30\u2192      {{Http::Headers::get().ContentLength, std::to_string(0)}});\n    31\u2192  return *headers;\n    32\u2192}\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192// Static response used for creating authorization ERROR responses.\n    35\u2192const Response& errorResponse() {\n    36\u2192  CONSTRUCT_ON_FIRST_USE(Response, Response{CheckStatus::Error,\n    37\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n    38\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n    39\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n    40\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n    41\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n    42\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n    43\u2192                                            UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n    44\u2192                                            {{}},\n    45\u2192                                            Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector{},\n    46\u2192                                            {},\n    47\u2192                                            EMPTY_STRING,\n    48\u2192                                            Http::Code::Forbidden,\n    49\u2192                                            ProtobufWkt::Struct{}});\n    50\u2192}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192// SuccessResponse used for creating either DENIED or OK authorization responses.\n    53\u2192struct SuccessResponse {\n    54\u2192  SuccessResponse(const Http::HeaderMap& headers, const MatcherSharedPtr& matchers,\n    55\u2192                  const MatcherSharedPtr& append_matchers,\n    56\u2192                  const MatcherSharedPtr& response_matchers,\n    57\u2192                  const MatcherSharedPtr& dynamic_metadata_matchers, Response&& response)\n    58\u2192      : headers_(headers), matchers_(matchers), append_matchers_(append_matchers),\n    59\u2192        response_matchers_(response_matchers),\n    60\u2192        to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_(dynamic_metadata_matchers),\n    61\u2192        response_(std::make_unique<Response>(response)) {\n    62\u2192    headers_.iterate([this](const Http::HeaderEntry& header) -> Http::HeaderMap::Iterate {\n    63\u2192      // UpstreamHeaderMatcher\n    64\u2192      if (matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\n    65\u2192        response_->headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.key().getStringView(),\n    66\u2192                                               header.value().getStringView());\n    67\u2192      }\n    68\u2192      if (append_matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\n    69\u2192        // If there is an existing matching key in the current headers, the new entry will be\n    70\u2192        // appended with the same key. For example, given {\"key\": \"value1\"} headers, if there is\n    71\u2192        // a matching \"key\" from the authorization response headers {\"key\": \"value2\"}, the\n    72\u2192        // request to upstream server will have two entries for \"key\": {\"key\": \"value1\", \"key\":\n    73\u2192        // \"value2\"}.\n    74\u2192        response_->headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.key().getStringView(),\n    75\u2192                                               header.value().getStringView());\n    76\u2192      }\n    77\u2192      if (response_matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\n    78\u2192        // For HTTP implementation, the response headers from the auth server will, by default, be\n    79\u2192        // appended (using addCopy) to the encoded response headers.\n    80\u2192        response_->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.key().getStringView(),\n    81\u2192                                                        header.value().getStringView());\n    82\u2192      }\n    83\u2192      if (to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\n    84\u2192        const std::string key{header.key().getStringView()};\n    85\u2192        const std::string value{header.value().getStringView()};\n    86\u2192        (*response_->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[key] = ValueUtil::stringValue(value);\n    87\u2192      }\n    88\u2192      return Http::HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\n    89\u2192    });\n    90\u2192  }\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192  const Http::HeaderMap& headers_;\n    93\u2192  // All matchers below are used on headers_.\n    94\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& matchers_;\n    95\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& append_matchers_;\n    96\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& response_matchers_;\n    97\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_;\n    98\u2192  ResponsePtr response_;\n    99\u2192};\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192absl::StatusOr<std::string> validatePathPrefix(absl::string_view path_prefix) {\n   102\u2192  if (!path_prefix.empty() && path_prefix[0] != '/') {\n   103\u2192    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\"path_prefix should start with \\\"/\\\".\");\n   104\u2192  }\n   105\u2192  return std::string(path_prefix);\n   106\u2192}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192} // namespace\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192// Config\n   111\u2192ClientConfig::ClientConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\n   112\u2192                           uint32_t timeout, absl::string_view path_prefix,\n   113\u2192                           Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context)\n   114\u2192    : client_header_matchers_(toClientMatchers(\n   115\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_response().allowed_client_headers(), context)),\n   116\u2192      client_header_on_success_matchers_(toClientMatchersOnSuccess(\n   117\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_response().allowed_client_headers_on_success(),\n   118\u2192          context)),\n   119\u2192      to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_(toDynamicMetadataMatchers(\n   120\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_response().dynamic_metadata_from_headers(), context)),\n   121\u2192      upstream_header_matchers_(toUpstreamMatchers(\n   122\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_response().allowed_upstream_headers(), context)),\n   123\u2192      upstream_header_to_append_matchers_(toUpstreamMatchers(\n   124\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_response().allowed_upstream_headers_to_append(),\n   125\u2192          context)),\n   126\u2192      cluster_name_(config.http_service().server_uri().cluster()), timeout_(timeout),\n   127\u2192      path_prefix_(THROW_OR_RETURN_VALUE(validatePathPrefix(path_prefix), std::string)),\n   128\u2192      tracing_name_(fmt::format(\"async {} egress\", config.http_service().server_uri().cluster())),\n   129\u2192      request_headers_parser_(THROW_OR_RETURN_VALUE(\n   130\u2192          Router::HeaderParser::configure(\n   131\u2192              config.http_service().authorization_request().headers_to_add(),\n   132\u2192              envoy::config::core::v3::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD),\n   133\u2192          Router::HeaderParserPtr)),\n   134\u2192      encode_raw_headers_(config.encode_raw_headers()) {}\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192MatcherSharedPtr\n   137\u2192ClientConfig::toClientMatchersOnSuccess(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   138\u2192                                        Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context) {\n   139\u2192  std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr> matchers(\n   140\u2192      CheckRequestUtils::createStringMatchers(list, context));\n   141\u2192  return std::make_shared<HeaderKeyMatcher>(std::move(matchers));\n   142\u2192}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192MatcherSharedPtr\n   145\u2192ClientConfig::toDynamicMetadataMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   146\u2192                                        Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context) {\n   147\u2192  std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr> matchers(\n   148\u2192      CheckRequestUtils::createStringMatchers(list, context));\n   149\u2192  return std::make_shared<HeaderKeyMatcher>(std::move(matchers));\n   150\u2192}\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192MatcherSharedPtr\n   153\u2192ClientConfig::toClientMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   154\u2192                               Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context) {\n   155\u2192  std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr> matchers(\n   156\u2192      CheckRequestUtils::createStringMatchers(list, context));\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192  // If list is empty, all authorization response headers, except Host, should be added to\n   159\u2192  // the client response.\n   160\u2192  if (matchers.empty()) {\n   161\u2192    envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher matcher;\n   162\u2192    matcher.set_exact(Http::Headers::get().Host.get());\n   163\u2192    matchers.push_back(\n   164\u2192        std::make_unique<Matchers::StringMatcherImpl<envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher>>(\n   165\u2192            matcher, context));\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192    return std::make_shared<NotHeaderKeyMatcher>(std::move(matchers));\n   168\u2192  }\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192  // If not empty, all user defined matchers and default matcher's list will\n   171\u2192  // be used instead.\n   172\u2192  std::vector<Http::LowerCaseString> keys{\n   173\u2192      {Http::Headers::get().Status, Http::Headers::get().ContentLength,\n   174\u2192       Http::Headers::get().WWWAuthenticate, Http::Headers::get().Location}};\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192  for (const auto& key : keys) {\n   177\u2192    envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher matcher;\n   178\u2192    matcher.set_exact(key.get());\n   179\u2192    matchers.push_back(\n   180\u2192        std::make_unique<Matchers::StringMatcherImpl<envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher>>(\n   181\u2192            matcher, context));\n   182\u2192  }\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192  return std::make_shared<HeaderKeyMatcher>(std::move(matchers));\n   185\u2192}\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192MatcherSharedPtr\n   188\u2192ClientConfig::toUpstreamMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   189\u2192                                 Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context) {\n   190\u2192  return std::make_unique<HeaderKeyMatcher>(CheckRequestUtils::createStringMatchers(list, context));\n   191\u2192}\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192RawHttpClientImpl::RawHttpClientImpl(Upstream::ClusterManager& cm, ClientConfigSharedPtr config)\n   194\u2192    : cm_(cm), config_(config) {}\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192RawHttpClientImpl::~RawHttpClientImpl() { ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr); }\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::cancel() {\n   199\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ != nullptr);\n   200\u2192  request_->cancel();\n   201\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   202\u2192}\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192// Client\n   205\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks,\n   206\u2192                              const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   207\u2192                              Tracing::Span& parent_span,\n   208\u2192                              const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) {\n   209\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr);\n   210\u2192  callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192  Http::RequestHeaderMapPtr headers;\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192  const auto& http_request = request.attributes().request().http();\n   215\u2192  const auto& http_request_body =\n   216\u2192      http_request.body().empty() ? http_request.raw_body() : http_request.body();\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192  uint64_t request_length = http_request_body.size();\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192  if (request_length > 0) {\n   221\u2192    headers = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(\n   222\u2192        {{Http::Headers::get().ContentLength, std::to_string(request_length)}});\n   223\u2192  } else {\n   224\u2192    headers = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(lengthZeroHeader());\n   225\u2192  }\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192  if (config_->encodeRawHeaders()) {\n   228\u2192    for (const auto& header : http_request.header_map().headers()) {\n   229\u2192      const Http::LowerCaseString key{header.key()};\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192      // Skip setting content-length header since it is already configured at initialization.\n   232\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().ContentLength) {\n   233\u2192        continue;\n   234\u2192      }\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().Path && !config_->pathPrefix().empty()) {\n   237\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, absl::StrCat(config_->pathPrefix(), header.raw_value()));\n   238\u2192      } else {\n   239\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, header.raw_value());\n   240\u2192      }\n   241\u2192    }\n   242\u2192  } else {\n   243\u2192    for (const auto& header : http_request.headers()) {\n   244\u2192      const Http::LowerCaseString key{header.first};\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192      // Skip setting content-length header since it is already configured at initialization.\n   247\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().ContentLength) {\n   248\u2192        continue;\n   249\u2192      }\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().Path && !config_->pathPrefix().empty()) {\n   252\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, absl::StrCat(config_->pathPrefix(), header.second));\n   253\u2192      } else {\n   254\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, header.second);\n   255\u2192      }\n   256\u2192    }\n   257\u2192  }\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192  config_->requestHeaderParser().evaluateHeaders(*headers, stream_info);\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192  Http::RequestMessagePtr message =\n   262\u2192      std::make_unique<Envoy::Http::RequestMessageImpl>(std::move(headers));\n   263\u2192  if (request_length > 0) {\n   264\u2192    message->body().add(http_request_body);\n   265\u2192  }\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192  const std::string& cluster = config_->cluster();\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192  // It's possible that the cluster specified in the filter configuration no longer exists due to a\n   270\u2192  // CDS removal.\n   271\u2192  const auto thread_local_cluster = cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(cluster);\n   272\u2192  if (thread_local_cluster == nullptr) {\n   273\u2192    // TODO(dio): Add stats related to this.\n   274\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\", cluster);\n   275\u2192    callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse()));\n   276\u2192    callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   277\u2192  } else {\n   278\u2192    // Do not enforce a sampling decision on this span; instead keep the parent's sampling status.\n   279\u2192    auto options = Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions()\n   280\u2192                       .setTimeout(config_->timeout())\n   281\u2192                       .setParentSpan(parent_span)\n   282\u2192                       .setChildSpanName(config_->tracingName())\n   283\u2192                       .setSampled(absl::nullopt);\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192    options.setSendXff(false);\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192    request_ = thread_local_cluster->httpAsyncClient().send(std::move(message), *this, options);\n   288\u2192  }\n   289\u2192}\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onSuccess(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&,\n   292\u2192                                  Http::ResponseMessagePtr&& message) {\n   293\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(toResponse(std::move(message)));\n   294\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   295\u2192}\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onFailure(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&,\n   298\u2192                                  Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason reason) {\n   299\u2192  // TODO(botengyao): handle different failure reasons.\n   300\u2192  ASSERT(reason == Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::Reset ||\n   301\u2192         reason == Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::ExceedResponseBufferLimit);\n   302\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse()));\n   303\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   304\u2192}\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onBeforeFinalizeUpstreamSpan(\n   307\u2192    Tracing::Span& span, const Http::ResponseHeaderMap* response_headers) {\n   308\u2192  if (response_headers != nullptr) {\n   309\u2192    const uint64_t status_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*response_headers);\n   310\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().HttpStatus,\n   311\u2192                Http::CodeUtility::toString(static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code)));\n   312\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)\n   313\u2192                                                         ? TracingConstants::get().TraceOk\n   314\u2192                                                         : TracingConstants::get().TraceUnauthz);\n   315\u2192  }\n   316\u2192}\n   317\u2192\n   318\u2192ResponsePtr RawHttpClientImpl::toResponse(Http::ResponseMessagePtr message) {\n   319\u2192  const uint64_t status_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(message->headers());\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192  // Set an error status if the call to the authorization server returns any of the 5xx HTTP error\n   322\u2192  // codes. A Forbidden response is sent to the client if the filter has not been configured with\n   323\u2192  // failure_mode_allow.\n   324\u2192  if (Http::CodeUtility::is5xx(status_code)) {\n   325\u2192    return std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse());\n   326\u2192  }\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  // Extract headers-to-remove from the storage header coming from the\n   329\u2192  // authorization server.\n   330\u2192  const auto& storage_header_name = Headers::get().EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove;\n   331\u2192  // If we are going to construct an Ok response we need to save the\n   332\u2192  // headers_to_remove in a variable first.\n   333\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\n   334\u2192  if (status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)) {\n   335\u2192    const auto& get_result = message->headers().get(storage_header_name);\n   336\u2192    for (size_t i = 0; i < get_result.size(); ++i) {\n   337\u2192      const Http::HeaderEntry* entry = get_result[i];\n   338\u2192      if (entry != nullptr) {\n   339\u2192        absl::string_view storage_header_value = entry->value().getStringView();\n   340\u2192        std::vector<absl::string_view> header_names = StringUtil::splitToken(\n   341\u2192            storage_header_value, \",\", /*keep_empty_string=*/false, /*trim_whitespace=*/true);\n   342\u2192        headers_to_remove.reserve(headers_to_remove.size() + header_names.size());\n   343\u2192        for (const auto& header_name : header_names) {\n   344\u2192          headers_to_remove.emplace_back(header_name);\n   345\u2192        }\n   346\u2192      }\n   347\u2192    }\n   348\u2192  }\n   349\u2192  // Now remove the storage header from the authz server response headers before\n   350\u2192  // we reuse them to construct an Ok/Denied authorization response below.\n   351\u2192  message->headers().remove(storage_header_name);\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192  // Create an Ok authorization response.\n   354\u2192  if (status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)) {\n   355\u2192    SuccessResponse ok{message->headers(),\n   356\u2192                       config_->upstreamHeaderMatchers(),\n   357\u2192                       config_->upstreamHeaderToAppendMatchers(),\n   358\u2192                       config_->clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers(),\n   359\u2192                       config_->dynamicMetadataMatchers(),\n   360\u2192                       Response{CheckStatus::OK,\n   361\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   362\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   363\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   364\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   365\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   366\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   367\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   368\u2192                                std::move(headers_to_remove),\n   369\u2192                                Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector{},\n   370\u2192                                {},\n   371\u2192                                EMPTY_STRING,\n   372\u2192                                Http::Code::OK,\n   373\u2192                                ProtobufWkt::Struct{}}};\n   374\u2192    return std::move(ok.response_);\n   375\u2192  }\n   376\u2192\n   377\u2192  // Create a Denied authorization response.\n   378\u2192  SuccessResponse denied{message->headers(),\n   379\u2192                         config_->clientHeaderMatchers(),\n   380\u2192                         config_->upstreamHeaderToAppendMatchers(),\n   381\u2192                         config_->clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers(),\n   382\u2192                         config_->dynamicMetadataMatchers(),\n   383\u2192                         Response{CheckStatus::Denied,\n   384\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   385\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   386\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   387\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   388\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   389\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   390\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   391\u2192                                  {{}},\n   392\u2192                                  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector{},\n   393\u2192                                  {},\n   394\u2192                                  message->bodyAsString(),\n   395\u2192                                  static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code),\n   396\u2192                                  ProtobufWkt::Struct{}}};\n   397\u2192  return std::move(denied.response_);\n   398\u2192}\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   401\u2192} // namespace Common\n   402\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   403\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   404\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   405\u2192"

28\u2192namespace Filters {\n    29\u2192namespace Common {\n    30\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192class Matcher;\n    33\u2192using MatcherSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<Matcher>;\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192/**\n    36\u2192 *  Matchers describe the rules for matching authorization request and response headers.\n    37\u2192 */\n    38\u2192class Matcher {\n    39\u2192public:\n    40\u2192  virtual ~Matcher() = default;\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192  /**\n    43\u2192   * Returns whether or not the header key matches the rules of the matcher.\n    44\u2192   *\n    45\u2192   * @param key supplies the header key to be evaluated.\n    46\u2192   */\n    47\u2192  virtual bool matches(absl::string_view key) const PURE;\n    48\u2192};\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192class HeaderKeyMatcher : public Matcher {\n    51\u2192public:\n    52\u2192  HeaderKeyMatcher(std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>&& list);\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  bool matches(absl::string_view key) const override;\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192private:\n    57\u2192  const std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr> matchers_;\n    58\u2192};\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192class NotHeaderKeyMatcher : public Matcher {\n    61\u2192public:\n    62\u2192  NotHeaderKeyMatcher(std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>&& list);\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192  bool matches(absl::string_view key) const override;\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192private:\n    67\u2192  const HeaderKeyMatcher matcher_;\n    68\u2192};\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192/**\n    71\u2192 * For creating ext_authz.proto (authorization) request.\n    72\u2192 * CheckRequestUtils is used to extract attributes from the TCP/HTTP request\n    73\u2192 * and fill out the details in the authorization protobuf that is sent to authorization\n    74\u2192 * service.\n    75\u2192 * The specific information in the request is as per the specification in the\n    76\u2192 * data plane API.\n    77\u2192 */\n    78\u2192class CheckRequestUtils {\n    79\u2192public:\n    80\u2192  /**\n    81\u2192   * createHttpCheck is used to extract the attributes from the stream and the http headers\n    82\u2192   * and fill them up in the CheckRequest proto message.\n    83\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the Http stream context from which data can be extracted.\n    84\u2192   * @param headers supplies the header map with http headers that will be used to create the\n    85\u2192   *        check request.\n    86\u2192   * @param request is the reference to the check request that will be filled up.\n    87\u2192   * @param with_request_body when true, will add the request body to the check request.\n    88\u2192   * @param pack_as_bytes when true, will set the check request body as bytes.\n    89\u2192   * @param encode_raw_headers  when true, will set the check request headers as bytes.\n    90\u2192   * @param include_peer_certificate whether to include the peer certificate in the check request.\n    91\u2192   * @param include_tls_session whether to include the TLS session details in the check request.\n    92\u2192   */\n    93\u2192  static void createHttpCheck(const Envoy::Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* callbacks,\n    94\u2192                              const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n    95\u2192                              Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>&& context_extensions,\n    96\u2192                              envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata&& metadata_context,\n    97\u2192                              envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata&& route_metadata_context,\n    98\u2192                              envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n    99\u2192                              uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\n   100\u2192                              bool encode_raw_headers, bool include_peer_certificate,\n   101\u2192                              bool include_tls_session,\n   102\u2192                              const Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>& destination_labels,\n   103\u2192                              const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\n   104\u2192                              const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher);\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192  /**\n   107\u2192   * createTcpCheck is used to extract the attributes from the network layer and fill them up\n   108\u2192   * in the CheckRequest proto message.\n   109\u2192   * @param callbacks supplies the network layer context from which data can be extracted.\n   110\u2192   * @param request is the reference to the check request that will be filled up.\n   111\u2192   * @param include_peer_certificate whether to include the peer certificate in the check request.\n   112\u2192   */\n   113\u2192  static void createTcpCheck(const Network::ReadFilterCallbacks* callbacks,\n   114\u2192                             envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   115\u2192                             bool include_peer_certificate, bool include_tls_session,\n   116\u2192                             const Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>& destination_labels);\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr toRequestMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   119\u2192                                            bool add_http_headers,\n   120\u2192                                            Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n   121\u2192  static std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>\n   122\u2192  createStringMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   123\u2192                       Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192private:\n   126\u2192  static void setAttrContextPeer(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::Peer& peer,\n   127\u2192                                 const Network::Connection& connection, const std::string& service,\n   128\u2192                                 const bool local, bool include_certificate);\n   129\u2192  static void setRequestTime(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::Request& req,\n   130\u2192                             const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info);\n   131\u2192  static void setHttpRequest(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::HttpRequest& httpreq,\n   132\u2192                             const uint64_t stream_id, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info,\n   133\u2192                             const Buffer::Instance* decoding_buffer,\n   134\u2192                             const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n   135\u2192                             uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\n   136\u2192                             bool encode_raw_headers,\n   137\u2192                             const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\n   138\u2192                             const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher);\n   139\u2192  static void setAttrContextRequest(\n   140\u2192      envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::Request& req, const uint64_t stream_id,\n   141\u2192      const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info, const Buffer::Instance* decoding_buffer,\n   142\u2192      const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\n   143\u2192      bool encode_raw_headers, const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\n   144\u2192      const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher);\n   145\u2192  static void setTLSSession(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::TLSSession& session,\n   146\u2192                            const Envoy::Network::Connection& connection);\n   147\u2192  static std::string getHeaderStr(const Envoy::Http::HeaderEntry* entry);\n   148\u2192  static Envoy::Http::HeaderMap::Iterate fillHttpHeaders(const Envoy::Http::HeaderEntry&, void*);\n   149\u2192};\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   152\u2192} // namespace Common\n   153\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   154\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   155\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   156\u2192"
