"     1\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\n     4\u2192#include <cstdint>\n     5\u2192#include <string>\n     6\u2192#include <vector>\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/attribute_context.pb.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/ssl/connection.h\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192#include \"source/common/buffer/buffer_impl.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"source/common/common/empty_string.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\"\n    17\u2192#include \"source/common/grpc/async_client_impl.h\"\n    18\u2192#include \"source/common/http/codes.h\"\n    19\u2192#include \"source/common/http/headers.h\"\n    20\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n    21\u2192#include \"source/common/network/utility.h\"\n    22\u2192#include \"source/common/protobuf/protobuf.h\"\n    23\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192#include \"absl/strings/str_cat.h\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    28\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    29\u2192namespace Filters {\n    30\u2192namespace Common {\n    31\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192// Matchers\n    34\u2192HeaderKeyMatcher::HeaderKeyMatcher(std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>&& list)\n    35\u2192    : matchers_(std::move(list)) {}\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192bool HeaderKeyMatcher::matches(absl::string_view key) const {\n    38\u2192  return std::any_of(matchers_.begin(), matchers_.end(),\n    39\u2192                     [&key](auto& matcher) { return matcher->match(key); });\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192NotHeaderKeyMatcher::NotHeaderKeyMatcher(std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>&& list)\n    43\u2192    : matcher_(std::move(list)) {}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192bool NotHeaderKeyMatcher::matches(absl::string_view key) const { return !matcher_.matches(key); }\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192// Convenience function.\n    48\u2192void headerMapAddHeader(envoy::config::core::v3::HeaderMap& mutable_header_map,\n    49\u2192                        const std::string& key, const std::string& value) {\n    50\u2192  auto* new_header = mutable_header_map.mutable_headers()->Add();\n    51\u2192  new_header->set_key(key);\n    52\u2192  new_header->set_raw_value(value);\n    53\u2192}\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192void CheckRequestUtils::setAttrContextPeer(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::Peer& peer,\n    56\u2192                                           const Network::Connection& connection,\n    57\u2192                                           const std::string& service, const bool local,\n    58\u2192                                           bool include_certificate) {\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192  // Set the address\n    61\u2192  auto addr = peer.mutable_address();\n    62\u2192  if (local) {\n    63\u2192    Envoy::Network::Utility::addressToProtobufAddress(\n    64\u2192        *connection.connectionInfoProvider().localAddress(), *addr);\n    65\u2192  } else {\n    66\u2192    Envoy::Network::Utility::addressToProtobufAddress(\n    67\u2192        *connection.connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress(), *addr);\n    68\u2192  }\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192  // Set the principal. Preferably the URI SAN, DNS SAN or Subject in that order from the peer's\n    71\u2192  // cert. Include the X.509 certificate of the source peer, if configured to do so.\n    72\u2192  auto ssl = connection.ssl();\n    73\u2192  if (ssl != nullptr) {\n    74\u2192    if (local) {\n    75\u2192      const auto uri_sans = ssl->uriSanLocalCertificate();\n    76\u2192      if (uri_sans.empty()) {\n    77\u2192        const auto dns_sans = ssl->dnsSansLocalCertificate();\n    78\u2192        if (dns_sans.empty()) {\n    79\u2192          peer.set_principal(MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(ssl->subjectLocalCertificate()));\n    80\u2192        } else {\n    81\u2192          peer.set_principal(MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(dns_sans[0]));\n    82\u2192        }\n    83\u2192      } else {\n    84\u2192        peer.set_principal(MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(uri_sans[0]));\n    85\u2192      }\n    86\u2192    } else {\n    87\u2192      const auto uri_sans = ssl->uriSanPeerCertificate();\n    88\u2192      if (uri_sans.empty()) {\n    89\u2192        const auto dns_sans = ssl->dnsSansPeerCertificate();\n    90\u2192        if (dns_sans.empty()) {\n    91\u2192          peer.set_principal(MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(ssl->subjectPeerCertificate()));\n    92\u2192        } else {\n    93\u2192          peer.set_principal(MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(dns_sans[0]));\n    94\u2192        }\n    95\u2192      } else {\n    96\u2192        peer.set_principal(MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(uri_sans[0]));\n    97\u2192      }\n    98\u2192      if (include_certificate) {\n    99\u2192        peer.set_certificate(ssl->urlEncodedPemEncodedPeerCertificate());\n   100\u2192      }\n   101\u2192    }\n   102\u2192  }\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192  if (!service.empty()) {\n   105\u2192    peer.set_service(MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(service));\n   106\u2192  }\n   107\u2192}\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192std::string CheckRequestUtils::getHeaderStr(const Envoy::Http::HeaderEntry* entry) {\n   110\u2192  if (entry) {\n   111\u2192    // TODO(jmarantz): plumb absl::string_view further here; there's no need\n   112\u2192    // to allocate a temp string in the local uses.\n   113\u2192    return MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(entry->value().getStringView());\n   114\u2192  }\n   115\u2192  return EMPTY_STRING;\n   116\u2192}\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192void CheckRequestUtils::setRequestTime(envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::Request& req,\n   119\u2192                                       const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) {\n   120\u2192  // Set the timestamp when the proxy receives the first byte of the request.\n   121\u2192  req.mutable_time()->MergeFrom(Protobuf::util::TimeUtil::NanosecondsToTimestamp(\n   122\u2192      std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::nanoseconds>(\n   123\u2192          stream_info.startTime().time_since_epoch())\n   124\u2192          .count()));\n   125\u2192}\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192void CheckRequestUtils::setHttpRequest(\n   128\u2192    envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::HttpRequest& httpreq, uint64_t stream_id,\n   129\u2192    const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info, const Buffer::Instance* decoding_buffer,\n   130\u2192    const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\n   131\u2192    bool encode_raw_headers, const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\n   132\u2192    const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher) {\n   133\u2192  httpreq.set_id(std::to_string(stream_id));\n   134\u2192  httpreq.set_method(getHeaderStr(headers.Method()));\n   135\u2192  httpreq.set_path(getHeaderStr(headers.Path()));\n   136\u2192  httpreq.set_host(getHeaderStr(headers.Host()));\n   137\u2192  httpreq.set_scheme(getHeaderStr(headers.Scheme()));\n   138\u2192  httpreq.set_size(stream_info.bytesReceived());\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  if (stream_info.protocol()) {\n   141\u2192    httpreq.set_protocol(Envoy::Http::Utility::getProtocolString(stream_info.protocol().value()));\n   142\u2192  }\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192  // Fill in the headers.\n   145\u2192  auto* mutable_headers = !encode_raw_headers ? httpreq.mutable_headers() : nullptr;\n   146\u2192  // Calling mutable_header_map() creates the field in the request; only do so when necessary.\n   147\u2192  auto* mutable_header_map = encode_raw_headers ? httpreq.mutable_header_map() : nullptr;\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192  headers.iterate([encode_raw_headers, allowed_headers_matcher, disallowed_headers_matcher,\n   150\u2192                   mutable_headers, mutable_header_map](const Envoy::Http::HeaderEntry& e) {\n   151\u2192    // Skip any client EnvoyAuthPartialBody header, which could interfere with internal use.\n   152\u2192    if (e.key().getStringView() == Headers::get().EnvoyAuthPartialBody.get()) {\n   153\u2192      return Envoy::Http::HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\n   154\u2192    }\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192    const std::string key(e.key().getStringView());\n   157\u2192    if (disallowed_headers_matcher != nullptr && disallowed_headers_matcher->matches(key)) {\n   158\u2192      return Envoy::Http::HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\n   159\u2192    }\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192    if (allowed_headers_matcher != nullptr && !allowed_headers_matcher->matches(key)) {\n   162\u2192      return Envoy::Http::HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\n   163\u2192    }\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192    if (encode_raw_headers) {\n   166\u2192      headerMapAddHeader(*mutable_header_map, key, std::string(e.value().getStringView()));\n   167\u2192    } else {\n   168\u2192      const std::string sanitized_value =\n   169\u2192          MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(e.value().getStringView());\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192      if (mutable_headers->find(key) == mutable_headers->end()) {\n   172\u2192        (*mutable_headers)[key] = sanitized_value;\n   173\u2192      } else {\n   174\u2192        // Merge duplicate headers.\n   175\u2192        (*mutable_headers)[key].append(\",\").append(sanitized_value);\n   176\u2192      }\n   177\u2192    }\n   178\u2192    return Envoy::Http::HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\n   179\u2192  });\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192  // Set request body.\n   182\u2192  if (max_request_bytes > 0 && decoding_buffer != nullptr) {\n   183\u2192    const uint64_t length = std::min(decoding_buffer->length(), max_request_bytes);\n   184\u2192    std::string data(length, 0);\n   185\u2192    decoding_buffer->copyOut(0, length, &data[0]);\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192    // This pack_as_bytes flag allows us to switch the content type (bytes or string) of \"body\" to\n   188\u2192    // be sent to the external authorization server without doing string encoding check (in this\n   189\u2192    // case UTF-8 check).\n   190\u2192    if (pack_as_bytes) {\n   191\u2192      httpreq.set_raw_body(std::move(data));\n   192\u2192    } else {\n   193\u2192      httpreq.set_body(MessageUtil::sanitizeUtf8String(data));\n   194\u2192    }\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192    // Add in a header to detect when a partial body is used.\n   197\u2192    std::string partial_body_value;\n   198\u2192    if (length != decoding_buffer->length()) {\n   199\u2192      partial_body_value = \"true\";\n   200\u2192    } else {\n   201\u2192      partial_body_value = \"false\";\n   202\u2192    }\n   203\u2192    if (encode_raw_headers) {\n   204\u2192      headerMapAddHeader(*mutable_header_map, Headers::get().EnvoyAuthPartialBody.get(),\n   205\u2192                         partial_body_value);\n   206\u2192    } else {\n   207\u2192      (*mutable_headers)[Headers::get().EnvoyAuthPartialBody.get()] = std::move(partial_body_value);\n   208\u2192    }\n   209\u2192  }\n   210\u2192}\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192void CheckRequestUtils::setAttrContextRequest(\n   213\u2192    envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::Request& req, const uint64_t stream_id,\n   214\u2192    const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info, const Buffer::Instance* decoding_buffer,\n   215\u2192    const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\n   216\u2192    bool encode_raw_headers, const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\n   217\u2192    const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher) {\n   218\u2192  setRequestTime(req, stream_info);\n   219\u2192  setHttpRequest(*req.mutable_http(), stream_id, stream_info, decoding_buffer, headers,\n   220\u2192                 max_request_bytes, pack_as_bytes, encode_raw_headers, allowed_headers_matcher,\n   221\u2192                 disallowed_headers_matcher);\n   222\u2192}\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192void CheckRequestUtils::setTLSSession(\n   225\u2192    envoy::service::auth::v3::AttributeContext::TLSSession& session,\n   226\u2192    const Envoy::Network::Connection& connection) {\n   227\u2192  const Ssl::ConnectionInfoConstSharedPtr ssl_info = connection.ssl();\n   228\u2192  if (ssl_info != nullptr && !ssl_info->sni().empty()) {\n   229\u2192    const std::string server_name(ssl_info->sni());\n   230\u2192    session.set_sni(server_name);\n   231\u2192  } else if (!connection.requestedServerName().empty()) {\n   232\u2192    const std::string server_name(connection.requestedServerName());\n   233\u2192    session.set_sni(server_name);\n   234\u2192  }\n   235\u2192}\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192void CheckRequestUtils::createHttpCheck(\n   238\u2192    const Envoy::Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* callbacks,\n   239\u2192    const Envoy::Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n   240\u2192    Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>&& context_extensions,\n   241\u2192    envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata&& metadata_context,\n   242\u2192    envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata&& route_metadata_context,\n   243\u2192    envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request, uint64_t max_request_bytes, bool pack_as_bytes,\n   244\u2192    bool encode_raw_headers, bool include_peer_certificate, bool include_tls_session,\n   245\u2192    const Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>& destination_labels,\n   246\u2192    const MatcherSharedPtr& allowed_headers_matcher,\n   247\u2192    const MatcherSharedPtr& disallowed_headers_matcher) {\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192  auto attrs = request.mutable_attributes();\n   250\u2192  const std::string service = getHeaderStr(headers.EnvoyDownstreamServiceCluster());\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192  // *cb->connection(), callbacks->streamInfo() and callbacks->decodingBuffer() are not qualified as\n   253\u2192  // const.\n   254\u2192  auto* cb = const_cast<Envoy::Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks*>(callbacks);\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192  setAttrContextPeer(*attrs->mutable_source(), *cb->connection(), service, false,\n   257\u2192                     include_peer_certificate);\n   258\u2192  setAttrContextPeer(*attrs->mutable_destination(), *cb->connection(), EMPTY_STRING, true,\n   259\u2192                     include_peer_certificate);\n   260\u2192  setAttrContextRequest(*attrs->mutable_request(), cb->streamId(), cb->streamInfo(),\n   261\u2192                        cb->decodingBuffer(), headers, max_request_bytes, pack_as_bytes,\n   262\u2192                        encode_raw_headers, allowed_headers_matcher, disallowed_headers_matcher);\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192  if (include_tls_session) {\n   265\u2192    setTLSSession(*attrs->mutable_tls_session(), *cb->connection());\n   266\u2192  }\n   267\u2192  (*attrs->mutable_destination()->mutable_labels()) = destination_labels;\n   268\u2192  // Fill in the context extensions and metadata context.\n   269\u2192  (*attrs->mutable_context_extensions()) = std::move(context_extensions);\n   270\u2192  (*attrs->mutable_metadata_context()) = std::move(metadata_context);\n   271\u2192  (*attrs->mutable_route_metadata_context()) = std::move(route_metadata_context);\n   272\u2192}\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192void CheckRequestUtils::createTcpCheck(\n   275\u2192    const Network::ReadFilterCallbacks* callbacks, envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   276\u2192    bool include_peer_certificate, bool include_tls_session,\n   277\u2192    const Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>& destination_labels) {\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192  auto attrs = request.mutable_attributes();\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192  auto* cb = const_cast<Network::ReadFilterCallbacks*>(callbacks);\n   282\u2192  const std::string server_name(cb->connection().requestedServerName());\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192  setAttrContextPeer(*attrs->mutable_source(), cb->connection(), \"\", false,\n   285\u2192                     include_peer_certificate);\n   286\u2192  setAttrContextPeer(*attrs->mutable_destination(), cb->connection(), server_name, true,\n   287\u2192                     include_peer_certificate);\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192  if (include_tls_session) {\n   290\u2192    setTLSSession(*attrs->mutable_tls_session(), cb->connection());\n   291\u2192  }\n   292\u2192  (*attrs->mutable_destination()->mutable_labels()) = destination_labels;\n   293\u2192}\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192MatcherSharedPtr\n   296\u2192CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   297\u2192                                     bool add_http_headers,\n   298\u2192                                     Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context) {\n   299\u2192  std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr> matchers(createStringMatchers(list, context));\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192  if (add_http_headers) {\n   302\u2192    const std::vector<Http::LowerCaseString> keys{\n   303\u2192        {Http::CustomHeaders::get().Authorization, Http::Headers::get().Method,\n   304\u2192         Http::Headers::get().Path, Http::Headers::get().Host}};\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192    for (const auto& key : keys) {\n   307\u2192      envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher matcher;\n   308\u2192      matcher.set_exact(key.get());\n   309\u2192      matchers.push_back(\n   310\u2192          std::make_unique<Matchers::StringMatcherImpl<envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher>>(\n   311\u2192              matcher, context));\n   312\u2192    }\n   313\u2192  }\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192  return std::make_shared<HeaderKeyMatcher>(std::move(matchers));\n   316\u2192}\n   317\u2192\n   318\u2192std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr>\n   319\u2192CheckRequestUtils::createStringMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   320\u2192                                        Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context) {\n   321\u2192  std::vector<Matchers::StringMatcherPtr> matchers;\n   322\u2192  for (const auto& matcher : list.patterns()) {\n   323\u2192    matchers.push_back(\n   324\u2192        std::make_unique<Matchers::StringMatcherImpl<envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher>>(\n   325\u2192            matcher, context));\n   326\u2192  }\n   327\u2192  return matchers;\n   328\u2192}\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   331\u2192} // namespace Common\n   332\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   333\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   334\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   335\u2192"
23. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto"
}
Output
"     1\u2192syntax = \"proto3\";\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192package envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3;\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192import \"envoy/config/common/mutation_rules/v3/mutation_rules.proto\";\n     6\u2192import \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.proto\";\n     7\u2192import \"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.proto\";\n     8\u2192import \"envoy/config/core/v3/grpc_service.proto\";\n     9\u2192import \"envoy/config/core/v3/http_uri.proto\";\n    10\u2192import \"envoy/type/matcher/v3/metadata.proto\";\n    11\u2192import \"envoy/type/matcher/v3/string.proto\";\n    12\u2192import \"envoy/type/v3/http_status.proto\";\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192import \"google/protobuf/struct.proto\";\n    15\u2192import \"google/protobuf/wrappers.proto\";\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192import \"envoy/annotations/deprecation.proto\";\n    18\u2192import \"udpa/annotations/sensitive.proto\";\n    19\u2192import \"udpa/annotations/status.proto\";\n    20\u2192import \"udpa/annotations/versioning.proto\";\n    21\u2192import \"validate/validate.proto\";\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192option java_package = \"io.envoyproxy.envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3\";\n    24\u2192option java_outer_classname = \"ExtAuthzProto\";\n    25\u2192option java_multiple_files = true;\n    26\u2192option go_package = \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3;ext_authzv3\";\n    27\u2192option (udpa.annotations.file_status).package_version_status = ACTIVE;\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192// [#protodoc-title: External Authorization]\n    30\u2192// External Authorization :ref:`configuration overview <config_http_filters_ext_authz>`.\n    31\u2192// [#extension: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz]\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192// [#next-free-field: 30]\n    34\u2192message ExtAuthz {\n    35\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n    36\u2192      \"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\";\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192  reserved 4;\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192  reserved \"use_alpha\";\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192  // External authorization service configuration.\n    43\u2192  oneof services {\n    44\u2192    // gRPC service configuration (default timeout: 200ms).\n    45\u2192    config.core.v3.GrpcService grpc_service = 1;\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    // HTTP service configuration (default timeout: 200ms).\n    48\u2192    HttpService http_service = 3;\n    49\u2192  }\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192  // API version for ext_authz transport protocol. This describes the ext_authz gRPC endpoint and\n    52\u2192  // version of messages used on the wire.\n    53\u2192  config.core.v3.ApiVersion transport_api_version = 12\n    54\u2192      [(validate.rules).enum = {defined_only: true}];\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192  //  Changes filter's behaviour on errors:\n    57\u2192  //\n    58\u2192  //  1. When set to true, the filter will ``accept`` client request even if the communication with\n    59\u2192  //  the authorization service has failed, or if the authorization service has returned a HTTP 5xx\n    60\u2192  //  error.\n    61\u2192  //\n    62\u2192  //  2. When set to false, ext-authz will ``reject`` client requests and return a ``Forbidden``\n    63\u2192  //  response if the communication with the authorization service has failed, or if the\n    64\u2192  //  authorization service has returned a HTTP 5xx error.\n    65\u2192  //\n    66\u2192  // Note that errors can be ``always`` tracked in the :ref:`stats\n    67\u2192  // <config_http_filters_ext_authz_stats>`.\n    68\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow = 2;\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192  // When ``failure_mode_allow`` and ``failure_mode_allow_header_add`` are both set to true,\n    71\u2192  // ``x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true`` will be added to request headers if the communication\n    72\u2192  // with the authorization service has failed, or if the authorization service has returned a\n    73\u2192  // HTTP 5xx error.\n    74\u2192  bool failure_mode_allow_header_add = 19;\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  // Enables filter to buffer the client request body and send it within the authorization request.\n    77\u2192  // A ``x-envoy-auth-partial-body: false|true`` metadata header will be added to the authorization\n    78\u2192  // request message indicating if the body data is partial.\n    79\u2192  BufferSettings with_request_body = 5;\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192  // Clears route cache in order to allow the external authorization service to correctly affect\n    82\u2192  // routing decisions. Filter clears all cached routes when:\n    83\u2192  //\n    84\u2192  // 1. The field is set to ``true``.\n    85\u2192  //\n    86\u2192  // 2. The status returned from the authorization service is a HTTP 200 or gRPC 0.\n    87\u2192  //\n    88\u2192  // 3. At least one ``authorization response header`` is added to the client request, or is used for\n    89\u2192  // altering another client request header.\n    90\u2192  //\n    91\u2192  bool clear_route_cache = 6;\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  // Sets the HTTP status that is returned to the client when the authorization server returns an error\n    94\u2192  // or cannot be reached. The default status is HTTP 403 Forbidden.\n    95\u2192  type.v3.HttpStatus status_on_error = 7;\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192  // When this is set to true, the filter will check the :ref:`ext_authz response\n    98\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse>` for invalid header &\n    99\u2192  // query parameter mutations. If the side stream response is invalid, it will send a local reply\n   100\u2192  // to the downstream request with status HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.\n   101\u2192  //\n   102\u2192  // Note that headers_to_remove & query_parameters_to_remove are validated, but invalid elements in\n   103\u2192  // those fields should not affect any headers & thus will not cause the filter to send a local\n   104\u2192  // reply.\n   105\u2192  //\n   106\u2192  // When set to false, any invalid mutations will be visible to the rest of envoy and may cause\n   107\u2192  // unexpected behavior.\n   108\u2192  //\n   109\u2192  // If you are using ext_authz with an untrusted ext_authz server, you should set this to true.\n   110\u2192  bool validate_mutations = 24;\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192  // Specifies a list of metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\n   113\u2192  // ext_authz service. The :ref:`filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.filter_metadata>`\n   114\u2192  // is passed as an opaque ``protobuf::Struct``.\n   115\u2192  //\n   116\u2192  // Please note that this field exclusively applies to the gRPC ext_authz service and has no effect on the HTTP service.\n   117\u2192  //\n   118\u2192  // For example, if the ``jwt_authn`` filter is used and :ref:`payload_in_metadata\n   119\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtProvider.payload_in_metadata>` is set,\n   120\u2192  // then the following will pass the jwt payload to the authorization server.\n   121\u2192  //\n   122\u2192  // .. code-block:: yaml\n   123\u2192  //\n   124\u2192  //    metadata_context_namespaces:\n   125\u2192  //    - envoy.filters.http.jwt_authn\n   126\u2192  //\n   127\u2192  repeated string metadata_context_namespaces = 8;\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192  // Specifies a list of metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\n   130\u2192  // ext_authz service. :ref:`typed_filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.typed_filter_metadata>`\n   131\u2192  // is passed as a ``protobuf::Any``.\n   132\u2192  //\n   133\u2192  // Please note that this field exclusively applies to the gRPC ext_authz service and has no effect on the HTTP service.\n   134\u2192  //\n   135\u2192  // It works in a way similar to ``metadata_context_namespaces`` but allows Envoy and ext_authz server to share\n   136\u2192  // the protobuf message definition in order to do a safe parsing.\n   137\u2192  //\n   138\u2192  repeated string typed_metadata_context_namespaces = 16;\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  // Specifies a list of route metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\n   141\u2192  // ext_authz service at :ref:`route_metadata_context <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.route_metadata_context>` in\n   142\u2192  // :ref:`CheckRequest <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.CheckRequest.attributes>`.\n   143\u2192  // :ref:`filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.filter_metadata>` is passed as an opaque ``protobuf::Struct``.\n   144\u2192  repeated string route_metadata_context_namespaces = 21;\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192  // Specifies a list of route metadata namespaces whose values, if present, will be passed to the\n   147\u2192  // ext_authz service at :ref:`route_metadata_context <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.route_metadata_context>` in\n   148\u2192  // :ref:`CheckRequest <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.CheckRequest.attributes>`.\n   149\u2192  // :ref:`typed_filter_metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.Metadata.typed_filter_metadata>` is passed as an ``protobuf::Any``.\n   150\u2192  repeated string route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces = 22;\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192  // Specifies if the filter is enabled.\n   153\u2192  //\n   154\u2192  // If :ref:`runtime_key <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.RuntimeFractionalPercent.runtime_key>` is specified,\n   155\u2192  // Envoy will lookup the runtime key to get the percentage of requests to filter.\n   156\u2192  //\n   157\u2192  // If this field is not specified, the filter will be enabled for all requests.\n   158\u2192  config.core.v3.RuntimeFractionalPercent filter_enabled = 9;\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192  // Specifies if the filter is enabled with metadata matcher.\n   161\u2192  // If this field is not specified, the filter will be enabled for all requests.\n   162\u2192  type.matcher.v3.MetadataMatcher filter_enabled_metadata = 14;\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192  // Specifies whether to deny the requests, when the filter is disabled.\n   165\u2192  // If :ref:`runtime_key <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.RuntimeFeatureFlag.runtime_key>` is specified,\n   166\u2192  // Envoy will lookup the runtime key to determine whether to deny request for\n   167\u2192  // filter protected path at filter disabling. If filter is disabled in\n   168\u2192  // typed_per_filter_config for the path, requests will not be denied.\n   169\u2192  //\n   170\u2192  // If this field is not specified, all requests will be allowed when disabled.\n   171\u2192  //\n   172\u2192  // If a request is denied due to this setting, the response code in :ref:`status_on_error\n   173\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.status_on_error>` will\n   174\u2192  // be returned.\n   175\u2192  config.core.v3.RuntimeFeatureFlag deny_at_disable = 11;\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192  // Specifies if the peer certificate is sent to the external service.\n   178\u2192  //\n   179\u2192  // When this field is true, Envoy will include the peer X.509 certificate, if available, in the\n   180\u2192  // :ref:`certificate<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.Peer.certificate>`.\n   181\u2192  bool include_peer_certificate = 10;\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192  // Optional additional prefix to use when emitting statistics. This allows to distinguish\n   184\u2192  // emitted statistics between configured ``ext_authz`` filters in an HTTP filter chain. For example:\n   185\u2192  //\n   186\u2192  // .. code-block:: yaml\n   187\u2192  //\n   188\u2192  //   http_filters:\n   189\u2192  //     - name: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\n   190\u2192  //       typed_config:\n   191\u2192  //         \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\n   192\u2192  //         stat_prefix: waf # This emits ext_authz.waf.ok, ext_authz.waf.denied, etc.\n   193\u2192  //     - name: envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\n   194\u2192  //       typed_config:\n   195\u2192  //         \"@type\": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz\n   196\u2192  //         stat_prefix: blocker # This emits ext_authz.blocker.ok, ext_authz.blocker.denied, etc.\n   197\u2192  //\n   198\u2192  string stat_prefix = 13;\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192  // Optional labels that will be passed to :ref:`labels<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.Peer.labels>` in\n   201\u2192  // :ref:`destination<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.destination>`.\n   202\u2192  // The labels will be read from :ref:`metadata<envoy_v3_api_msg_config.core.v3.Node>` with the specified key.\n   203\u2192  string bootstrap_metadata_labels_key = 15;\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192  // Check request to authorization server will include the client request headers that have a correspondent match\n   206\u2192  // in the :ref:`list <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher>`. If this option isn't specified, then\n   207\u2192  // all client request headers are included in the check request to a gRPC authorization server, whereas no client request headers\n   208\u2192  // (besides the ones allowed by default - see note below) are included in the check request to an HTTP authorization server.\n   209\u2192  // This inconsistency between gRPC and HTTP servers is to maintain backwards compatibility with legacy behavior.\n   210\u2192  //\n   211\u2192  // .. note::\n   212\u2192  //\n   213\u2192  //  1. For requests to an HTTP authorization server: in addition to the the user's supplied matchers, ``Host``, ``Method``, ``Path``,\n   214\u2192  //     ``Content-Length``, and ``Authorization`` are **additionally included** in the list.\n   215\u2192  //\n   216\u2192  // .. note::\n   217\u2192  //\n   218\u2192  //  2. For requests to an HTTP authorization server: *Content-Length* will be set to 0 and the request to the\n   219\u2192  //  authorization server will not have a message body. However, the check request can include the buffered\n   220\u2192  //  client request body (controlled by :ref:`with_request_body\n   221\u2192  //  <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.with_request_body>` setting),\n   222\u2192  //  consequently the value of *Content-Length* of the authorization request reflects the size of\n   223\u2192  //  its payload size.\n   224\u2192  //\n   225\u2192  // .. note::\n   226\u2192  //\n   227\u2192  //  3. This can be overridden by the field ``disallowed_headers`` below. That is, if a header\n   228\u2192  //  matches for both ``allowed_headers`` and ``disallowed_headers``, the header will NOT be sent.\n   229\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher allowed_headers = 17;\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192  // If set, specifically disallow any header in this list to be forwarded to the external\n   232\u2192  // authentication server. This overrides the above ``allowed_headers`` if a header matches both.\n   233\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher disallowed_headers = 25;\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192  // Specifies if the TLS session level details like SNI are sent to the external service.\n   236\u2192  //\n   237\u2192  // When this field is true, Envoy will include the SNI name used for TLSClientHello, if available, in the\n   238\u2192  // :ref:`tls_session<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.tls_session>`.\n   239\u2192  bool include_tls_session = 18;\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192  // Whether to increment cluster statistics (e.g. cluster.<cluster_name>.upstream_rq_*) on authorization failure.\n   242\u2192  // Defaults to true.\n   243\u2192  google.protobuf.BoolValue charge_cluster_response_stats = 20;\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192  // Whether to encode the raw headers (i.e. unsanitized values & unconcatenated multi-line headers)\n   246\u2192  // in authentication request. Works with both HTTP and GRPC clients.\n   247\u2192  //\n   248\u2192  // When this is set to true, header values are not sanitized. Headers with the same key will also\n   249\u2192  // not be combined into a single, comma-separated header.\n   250\u2192  // Requests to GRPC services will populate the field\n   251\u2192  // :ref:`header_map<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.HttpRequest.header_map>`.\n   252\u2192  // Requests to HTTP services will be constructed with the unsanitized header values and preserved\n   253\u2192  // multi-line headers with the same key.\n   254\u2192  //\n   255\u2192  // If this field is set to false, header values will be sanitized, with any non-UTF-8-compliant\n   256\u2192  // bytes replaced with '!'. Headers with the same key will have their values concatenated into a\n   257\u2192  // single comma-separated header value.\n   258\u2192  // Requests to GRPC services will populate the field\n   259\u2192  // :ref:`headers<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.HttpRequest.headers>`.\n   260\u2192  // Requests to HTTP services will have their header values sanitized and will not preserve\n   261\u2192  // multi-line headers with the same key.\n   262\u2192  //\n   263\u2192  // It's recommended you set this to true unless you already rely on the old behavior. False is the\n   264\u2192  // default only for backwards compatibility.\n   265\u2192  bool encode_raw_headers = 23;\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192  // Rules for what modifications an ext_authz server may make to the request headers before\n   268\u2192  // continuing decoding / forwarding upstream.\n   269\u2192  //\n   270\u2192  // If set to anything, enables header mutation checking against configured rules. Note that\n   271\u2192  // :ref:`HeaderMutationRules <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.common.mutation_rules.v3.HeaderMutationRules>`\n   272\u2192  // has defaults that change ext_authz behavior. Also note that if this field is set to anything,\n   273\u2192  // ext_authz can no longer append to :-prefixed headers.\n   274\u2192  //\n   275\u2192  // If empty, header mutation rule checking is completely disabled.\n   276\u2192  //\n   277\u2192  // Regardless of what is configured here, ext_authz cannot remove :-prefixed headers.\n   278\u2192  //\n   279\u2192  // This field and ``validate_mutations`` have different use cases. ``validate_mutations`` enables\n   280\u2192  // correctness checks for all header / query parameter mutations (e.g. for invalid characters).\n   281\u2192  // This field allows the filter to reject mutations to specific headers.\n   282\u2192  config.common.mutation_rules.v3.HeaderMutationRules decoder_header_mutation_rules = 26;\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192  // Enable / disable ingestion of dynamic metadata from ext_authz service.\n   285\u2192  //\n   286\u2192  // If false, the filter will ignore dynamic metadata injected by the ext_authz service. If the\n   287\u2192  // ext_authz service tries injecting dynamic metadata, the filter will log, increment the\n   288\u2192  // ``ignored_dynamic_metadata`` stat, then continue handling the response.\n   289\u2192  //\n   290\u2192  // If true, the filter will ingest dynamic metadata entries as normal.\n   291\u2192  //\n   292\u2192  // If unset, defaults to true.\n   293\u2192  google.protobuf.BoolValue enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion = 27;\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192  // Additional metadata to be added to the filter state for logging purposes. The metadata will be\n   296\u2192  // added to StreamInfo's filter state under the namespace corresponding to the ext_authz filter\n   297\u2192  // name.\n   298\u2192  google.protobuf.Struct filter_metadata = 28;\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192  // When set to true, the filter will emit per-stream stats for access logging. The filter state\n   301\u2192  // key will be the same as the filter name.\n   302\u2192  //\n   303\u2192  // If using Envoy GRPC, emits latency, bytes sent / received, upstream info, and upstream cluster\n   304\u2192  // info. If not using Envoy GRPC, emits only latency. Note that stats are ONLY added to filter\n   305\u2192  // state if a check request is actually made to an ext_authz service.\n   306\u2192  //\n   307\u2192  // If this is false the filter will not emit stats, but filter_metadata will still be respected if\n   308\u2192  // it has a value.\n   309\u2192  bool emit_filter_state_stats = 29;\n   310\u2192}\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192// Configuration for buffering the request data.\n   313\u2192message BufferSettings {\n   314\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n   315\u2192      \"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v2.BufferSettings\";\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192  // Sets the maximum size of a message body that the filter will hold in memory. Envoy will return\n   318\u2192  // ``HTTP 413`` and will *not* initiate the authorization process when buffer reaches the number\n   319\u2192  // set in this field. Note that this setting will have precedence over :ref:`failure_mode_allow\n   320\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.failure_mode_allow>`.\n   321\u2192  uint32 max_request_bytes = 1 [(validate.rules).uint32 = {gt: 0}];\n   322\u2192\n   323\u2192  // When this field is true, Envoy will buffer the message until ``max_request_bytes`` is reached.\n   324\u2192  // The authorization request will be dispatched and no 413 HTTP error will be returned by the\n   325\u2192  // filter.\n   326\u2192  bool allow_partial_message = 2;\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  // If true, the body sent to the external authorization service is set with raw bytes, it sets\n   329\u2192  // the :ref:`raw_body<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.HttpRequest.raw_body>`\n   330\u2192  // field of HTTP request attribute context. Otherwise, :ref:`body\n   331\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.HttpRequest.body>` will be filled\n   332\u2192  // with UTF-8 string request body.\n   333\u2192  //\n   334\u2192  // This field only affects configurations using a :ref:`grpc_service\n   335\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.grpc_service>`. In configurations that use\n   336\u2192  // an :ref:`http_service <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.http_service>`, this\n   337\u2192  // has no effect.\n   338\u2192  bool pack_as_bytes = 3;\n   339\u2192}\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192// HttpService is used for raw HTTP communication between the filter and the authorization service.\n   342\u2192// When configured, the filter will parse the client request and use these attributes to call the\n   343\u2192// authorization server. Depending on the response, the filter may reject or accept the client\n   344\u2192// request. Note that in any of these events, metadata can be added, removed or overridden by the\n   345\u2192// filter:\n   346\u2192//\n   347\u2192// *On authorization request*, a list of allowed request headers may be supplied. See\n   348\u2192// :ref:`allowed_headers\n   349\u2192// <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.AuthorizationRequest.allowed_headers>`\n   350\u2192// for details. Additional headers metadata may be added to the authorization request. See\n   351\u2192// :ref:`headers_to_add\n   352\u2192// <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.AuthorizationRequest.headers_to_add>` for\n   353\u2192// details.\n   354\u2192//\n   355\u2192// On authorization response status HTTP 200 OK, the filter will allow traffic to the upstream and\n   356\u2192// additional headers metadata may be added to the original client request. See\n   357\u2192// :ref:`allowed_upstream_headers\n   358\u2192// <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.AuthorizationResponse.allowed_upstream_headers>`\n   359\u2192// for details. Additionally, the filter may add additional headers to the client's response. See\n   360\u2192// :ref:`allowed_client_headers_on_success\n   361\u2192// <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.AuthorizationResponse.allowed_client_headers_on_success>`\n   362\u2192// for details.\n   363\u2192//\n   364\u2192// On other authorization response statuses, the filter will not allow traffic. Additional headers\n   365\u2192// metadata as well as body may be added to the client's response. See :ref:`allowed_client_headers\n   366\u2192// <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.AuthorizationResponse.allowed_client_headers>`\n   367\u2192// for details.\n   368\u2192// [#next-free-field: 9]\n   369\u2192message HttpService {\n   370\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n   371\u2192      \"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v2.HttpService\";\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192  reserved 3, 4, 5, 6;\n   374\u2192\n   375\u2192  // Sets the HTTP server URI which the authorization requests must be sent to.\n   376\u2192  config.core.v3.HttpUri server_uri = 1;\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192  // Sets a prefix to the value of authorization request header ``Path``.\n   379\u2192  string path_prefix = 2;\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192  // Settings used for controlling authorization request metadata.\n   382\u2192  AuthorizationRequest authorization_request = 7;\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  // Settings used for controlling authorization response metadata.\n   385\u2192  AuthorizationResponse authorization_response = 8;\n   386\u2192}\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192message AuthorizationRequest {\n   389\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n   390\u2192      \"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v2.AuthorizationRequest\";\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192  // Authorization request includes the client request headers that have a correspondent match\n   393\u2192  // in the :ref:`list <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher>`.\n   394\u2192  // This field has been deprecated in favor of :ref:`allowed_headers\n   395\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.allowed_headers>`.\n   396\u2192  //\n   397\u2192  // .. note::\n   398\u2192  //\n   399\u2192  //   In addition to the the user's supplied matchers, ``Host``, ``Method``, ``Path``,\n   400\u2192  //   ``Content-Length``, and ``Authorization`` are **automatically included** to the list.\n   401\u2192  //\n   402\u2192  // .. note::\n   403\u2192  //\n   404\u2192  //   By default, ``Content-Length`` header is set to ``0`` and the request to the authorization\n   405\u2192  //   service has no message body. However, the authorization request *may* include the buffered\n   406\u2192  //   client request body (controlled by :ref:`with_request_body\n   407\u2192  //   <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.with_request_body>`\n   408\u2192  //   setting) hence the value of its ``Content-Length`` reflects the size of its payload size.\n   409\u2192  //\n   410\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher allowed_headers = 1\n   411\u2192      [deprecated = true, (envoy.annotations.deprecated_at_minor_version) = \"3.0\"];\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192  // Sets a list of headers that will be included to the request to authorization service. Note that\n   414\u2192  // client request of the same key will be overridden.\n   415\u2192  repeated config.core.v3.HeaderValue headers_to_add = 2;\n   416\u2192}\n   417\u2192\n   418\u2192// [#next-free-field: 6]\n   419\u2192message AuthorizationResponse {\n   420\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n   421\u2192      \"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v2.AuthorizationResponse\";\n   422\u2192\n   423\u2192  // When this :ref:`list <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher>` is set, authorization\n   424\u2192  // response headers that have a correspondent match will be added to the original client request.\n   425\u2192  // Note that coexistent headers will be overridden.\n   426\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher allowed_upstream_headers = 1;\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192  // When this :ref:`list <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher>` is set, authorization\n   429\u2192  // response headers that have a correspondent match will be added to the original client request.\n   430\u2192  // Note that coexistent headers will be appended.\n   431\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher allowed_upstream_headers_to_append = 3;\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192  // When this :ref:`list <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher>` is set, authorization\n   434\u2192  // response headers that have a correspondent match will be added to the client's response. Note\n   435\u2192  // that when this list is *not* set, all the authorization response headers, except ``Authority\n   436\u2192  // (Host)`` will be in the response to the client. When a header is included in this list, ``Path``,\n   437\u2192  // ``Status``, ``Content-Length``, ``WWWAuthenticate`` and ``Location`` are automatically added.\n   438\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher allowed_client_headers = 2;\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192  // When this :ref:`list <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher>` is set, authorization\n   441\u2192  // response headers that have a correspondent match will be added to the client's response when\n   442\u2192  // the authorization response itself is successful, i.e. not failed or denied. When this list is\n   443\u2192  // *not* set, no additional headers will be added to the client's response on success.\n   444\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher allowed_client_headers_on_success = 4;\n   445\u2192\n   446\u2192  // When this :ref:`list <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher>` is set, authorization\n   447\u2192  // response headers that have a correspondent match will be emitted as dynamic metadata to be consumed\n   448\u2192  // by the next filter. This metadata lives in a namespace specified by the canonical name of extension filter\n   449\u2192  // that requires it:\n   450\u2192  //\n   451\u2192  // - :ref:`envoy.filters.http.ext_authz <config_http_filters_ext_authz_dynamic_metadata>` for HTTP filter.\n   452\u2192  // - :ref:`envoy.filters.network.ext_authz <config_network_filters_ext_authz_dynamic_metadata>` for network filter.\n   453\u2192  type.matcher.v3.ListStringMatcher dynamic_metadata_from_headers = 5;\n   454\u2192}\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192// Extra settings on a per virtualhost/route/weighted-cluster level.\n   457\u2192message ExtAuthzPerRoute {\n   458\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n   459\u2192      \"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v2.ExtAuthzPerRoute\";\n   460\u2192\n   461\u2192  oneof override {\n   462\u2192    option (validate.required) = true;\n   463\u2192\n   464\u2192    // Disable the ext auth filter for this particular vhost or route.\n   465\u2192    // If disabled is specified in multiple per-filter-configs, the most specific one will be used.\n   466\u2192    bool disabled = 1 [(validate.rules).bool = {const: true}];\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192    // Check request settings for this route.\n   469\u2192    CheckSettings check_settings = 2 [(validate.rules).message = {required: true}];\n   470\u2192  }\n   471\u2192}\n   472\u2192\n   473\u2192// Extra settings for the check request.\n   474\u2192message CheckSettings {\n   475\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n   476\u2192      \"envoy.config.filter.http.ext_authz.v2.CheckSettings\";\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192  // Context extensions to set on the CheckRequest's\n   479\u2192  // :ref:`AttributeContext.context_extensions<envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.context_extensions>`\n   480\u2192  //\n   481\u2192  // You can use this to provide extra context for the external authorization server on specific\n   482\u2192  // virtual hosts/routes. For example, adding a context extension on the virtual host level can\n   483\u2192  // give the ext-authz server information on what virtual host is used without needing to parse the\n   484\u2192  // host header. If CheckSettings is specified in multiple per-filter-configs, they will be merged\n   485\u2192  // in order, and the result will be used.\n   486\u2192  //\n   487\u2192  // Merge semantics for this field are such that keys from more specific configs override.\n   488\u2192  //\n   489\u2192  // .. note::\n   490\u2192  //\n   491\u2192  //   These settings are only applied to a filter configured with a\n   492\u2192  //   :ref:`grpc_service<envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.grpc_service>`.\n   493\u2192  map<string, string> context_extensions = 1 [(udpa.annotations.sensitive) = true];\n   494\u2192\n   495\u2192  // When set to true, disable the configured :ref:`with_request_body\n   496\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.with_request_body>` for a specific route.\n   497\u2192  //\n   498\u2192  // Please note that only one of *disable_request_body_buffering* or\n   499\u2192  // :ref:`with_request_body <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.CheckSettings.with_request_body>`\n   500\u2192  // may be specified.\n   501\u2192  bool disable_request_body_buffering = 2;\n   502\u2192\n   503\u2192  // Enable or override request body buffering, which is configured using the\n   504\u2192  // :ref:`with_request_body <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.with_request_body>`\n   505\u2192  // option for a specific route.\n   506\u2192  //\n   507\u2192  // Please note that only only one of *with_request_body* or\n   508\u2192  // :ref:`disable_request_body_buffering <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.CheckSettings.disable_request_body_buffering>`\n   509\u2192  // may be specified.\n   510\u2192  BufferSettings with_request_body = 3;\n   511\u2192}\n   512\u2192"
24. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/api/envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.proto"
}
Output
"     1\u2192syntax = \"proto3\";\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192package envoy.service.auth.v3;\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192import \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.proto\";\n     6\u2192import \"envoy/service/auth/v3/attribute_context.proto\";\n     7\u2192import \"envoy/type/v3/http_status.proto\";\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192import \"google/protobuf/struct.proto\";\n    10\u2192import \"google/rpc/status.proto\";\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192import \"envoy/annotations/deprecation.proto\";\n    13\u2192import \"udpa/annotations/status.proto\";\n    14\u2192import \"udpa/annotations/versioning.proto\";\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192option java_package = \"io.envoyproxy.envoy.service.auth.v3\";\n    17\u2192option java_outer_classname = \"ExternalAuthProto\";\n    18\u2192option java_multiple_files = true;\n    19\u2192option go_package = \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/auth/v3;authv3\";\n    20\u2192option (udpa.annotations.file_status).package_version_status = ACTIVE;\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192// [#protodoc-title: Authorization service]\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192// The authorization service request messages used by external authorization :ref:`network filter\n    25\u2192// <config_network_filters_ext_authz>` and :ref:`HTTP filter <config_http_filters_ext_authz>`.\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192// A generic interface for performing authorization check on incoming\n    28\u2192// requests to a networked service.\n    29\u2192service Authorization {\n    30\u2192  // Performs authorization check based on the attributes associated with the\n    31\u2192  // incoming request, and returns status `OK` or not `OK`.\n    32\u2192  rpc Check(CheckRequest) returns (CheckResponse) {\n    33\u2192  }\n    34\u2192}\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192message CheckRequest {\n    37\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type = \"envoy.service.auth.v2.CheckRequest\";\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192  // The request attributes.\n    40\u2192  AttributeContext attributes = 1;\n    41\u2192}\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192// HTTP attributes for a denied response.\n    44\u2192message DeniedHttpResponse {\n    45\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n    46\u2192      \"envoy.service.auth.v2.DeniedHttpResponse\";\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192  // This field allows the authorization service to send an HTTP response status code to the\n    49\u2192  // downstream client. If not set, Envoy sends ``403 Forbidden`` HTTP status code by default.\n    50\u2192  type.v3.HttpStatus status = 1;\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192  // This field allows the authorization service to send HTTP response headers\n    53\u2192  // to the downstream client. Note that the :ref:`append field in HeaderValueOption <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption.append>` defaults to\n    54\u2192  // false when used in this message.\n    55\u2192  repeated config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption headers = 2;\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  // This field allows the authorization service to send a response body data\n    58\u2192  // to the downstream client.\n    59\u2192  string body = 3;\n    60\u2192}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192// HTTP attributes for an OK response.\n    63\u2192// [#next-free-field: 9]\n    64\u2192message OkHttpResponse {\n    65\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n    66\u2192      \"envoy.service.auth.v2.OkHttpResponse\";\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192  // HTTP entity headers in addition to the original request headers. This allows the authorization\n    69\u2192  // service to append, to add or to override headers from the original request before\n    70\u2192  // dispatching it to the upstream. Note that the :ref:`append field in HeaderValueOption <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption.append>` defaults to\n    71\u2192  // false when used in this message. By setting the ``append`` field to ``true``,\n    72\u2192  // the filter will append the correspondent header value to the matched request header.\n    73\u2192  // By leaving ``append`` as false, the filter will either add a new header, or override an existing\n    74\u2192  // one if there is a match.\n    75\u2192  repeated config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption headers = 2;\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  // HTTP entity headers to remove from the original request before dispatching\n    78\u2192  // it to the upstream. This allows the authorization service to act on auth\n    79\u2192  // related headers (like ``Authorization``), process them, and consume them.\n    80\u2192  // Under this model, the upstream will either receive the request (if it's\n    81\u2192  // authorized) or not receive it (if it's not), but will not see headers\n    82\u2192  // containing authorization credentials.\n    83\u2192  //\n    84\u2192  // Pseudo headers (such as ``:authority``, ``:method``, ``:path`` etc), as well as\n    85\u2192  // the header ``Host``, may not be removed as that would make the request\n    86\u2192  // malformed. If mentioned in ``headers_to_remove`` these special headers will\n    87\u2192  // be ignored.\n    88\u2192  //\n    89\u2192  // When using the HTTP service this must instead be set by the HTTP\n    90\u2192  // authorization service as a comma separated list like so:\n    91\u2192  // ``x-envoy-auth-headers-to-remove: one-auth-header, another-auth-header``.\n    92\u2192  repeated string headers_to_remove = 5;\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192  // This field has been deprecated in favor of :ref:`CheckResponse.dynamic_metadata\n    95\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse.dynamic_metadata>`. Until it is removed,\n    96\u2192  // setting this field overrides :ref:`CheckResponse.dynamic_metadata\n    97\u2192  // <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse.dynamic_metadata>`.\n    98\u2192  google.protobuf.Struct dynamic_metadata = 3\n    99\u2192      [deprecated = true, (envoy.annotations.deprecated_at_minor_version) = \"3.0\"];\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192  // This field allows the authorization service to send HTTP response headers\n   102\u2192  // to the downstream client on success. Note that the :ref:`append field in HeaderValueOption <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption.append>`\n   103\u2192  // defaults to false when used in this message.\n   104\u2192  repeated config.core.v3.HeaderValueOption response_headers_to_add = 6;\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192  // This field allows the authorization service to set (and overwrite) query\n   107\u2192  // string parameters on the original request before it is sent upstream.\n   108\u2192  repeated config.core.v3.QueryParameter query_parameters_to_set = 7;\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192  // This field allows the authorization service to specify which query parameters\n   111\u2192  // should be removed from the original request before it is sent upstream. Each\n   112\u2192  // element in this list is a case-sensitive query parameter name to be removed.\n   113\u2192  repeated string query_parameters_to_remove = 8;\n   114\u2192}\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192// Intended for gRPC and Network Authorization servers ``only``.\n   117\u2192message CheckResponse {\n   118\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n   119\u2192      \"envoy.service.auth.v2.CheckResponse\";\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  // Status ``OK`` allows the request. Any other status indicates the request should be denied, and\n   122\u2192  // for HTTP filter, if not overridden by :ref:`denied HTTP response status <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.DeniedHttpResponse.status>`\n   123\u2192  // Envoy sends ``403 Forbidden`` HTTP status code by default.\n   124\u2192  google.rpc.Status status = 1;\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192  // An message that contains HTTP response attributes. This message is\n   127\u2192  // used when the authorization service needs to send custom responses to the\n   128\u2192  // downstream client or, to modify/add request headers being dispatched to the upstream.\n   129\u2192  oneof http_response {\n   130\u2192    // Supplies http attributes for a denied response.\n   131\u2192    DeniedHttpResponse denied_response = 2;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192    // Supplies http attributes for an ok response.\n   134\u2192    OkHttpResponse ok_response = 3;\n   135\u2192  }\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192  // Optional response metadata that will be emitted as dynamic metadata to be consumed by the next\n   138\u2192  // filter. This metadata lives in a namespace specified by the canonical name of extension filter\n   139\u2192  // that requires it:\n   140\u2192  //\n   141\u2192  // - :ref:`envoy.filters.http.ext_authz <config_http_filters_ext_authz_dynamic_metadata>` for HTTP filter.\n   142\u2192  // - :ref:`envoy.filters.network.ext_authz <config_network_filters_ext_authz_dynamic_metadata>` for network filter.\n   143\u2192  google.protobuf.Struct dynamic_metadata = 4;\n   144\u2192}\n   145\u2192"
25. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/api/envoy/service/auth/v3/attribute_context.proto"
}
Output
"     1\u2192syntax = \"proto3\";\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192package envoy.service.auth.v3;\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192import \"envoy/config/core/v3/address.proto\";\n     6\u2192import \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.proto\";\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192import \"google/protobuf/timestamp.proto\";\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192import \"udpa/annotations/migrate.proto\";\n    11\u2192import \"udpa/annotations/status.proto\";\n    12\u2192import \"udpa/annotations/versioning.proto\";\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192option java_package = \"io.envoyproxy.envoy.service.auth.v3\";\n    15\u2192option java_outer_classname = \"AttributeContextProto\";\n    16\u2192option java_multiple_files = true;\n    17\u2192option go_package = \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/auth/v3;authv3\";\n    18\u2192option (udpa.annotations.file_status).package_version_status = ACTIVE;\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192// [#protodoc-title: Attribute context]\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192// See :ref:`network filter configuration overview <config_network_filters_ext_authz>`\n    23\u2192// and :ref:`HTTP filter configuration overview <config_http_filters_ext_authz>`.\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192// An attribute is a piece of metadata that describes an activity on a network.\n    26\u2192// For example, the size of an HTTP request, or the status code of an HTTP response.\n    27\u2192//\n    28\u2192// Each attribute has a type and a name, which is logically defined as a proto message field\n    29\u2192// of the ``AttributeContext``. The ``AttributeContext`` is a collection of individual attributes\n    30\u2192// supported by Envoy authorization system.\n    31\u2192// [#comment: The following items are left out of this proto\n    32\u2192// Request.Auth field for jwt tokens\n    33\u2192// Request.Api for api management\n    34\u2192// Origin peer that originated the request\n    35\u2192// Caching Protocol\n    36\u2192// request_context return values to inject back into the filter chain\n    37\u2192// peer.claims -- from X.509 extensions\n    38\u2192// Configuration\n    39\u2192// - field mask to send\n    40\u2192// - which return values from request_context are copied back\n    41\u2192// - which return values are copied into request_headers]\n    42\u2192// [#next-free-field: 14]\n    43\u2192message AttributeContext {\n    44\u2192  option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n    45\u2192      \"envoy.service.auth.v2.AttributeContext\";\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192  // This message defines attributes for a node that handles a network request.\n    48\u2192  // The node can be either a service or an application that sends, forwards,\n    49\u2192  // or receives the request. Service peers should fill in the ``service``,\n    50\u2192  // ``principal``, and ``labels`` as appropriate.\n    51\u2192  // [#next-free-field: 6]\n    52\u2192  message Peer {\n    53\u2192    option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n    54\u2192        \"envoy.service.auth.v2.AttributeContext.Peer\";\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    // The address of the peer, this is typically the IP address.\n    57\u2192    // It can also be UDS path, or others.\n    58\u2192    config.core.v3.Address address = 1;\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192    // The canonical service name of the peer.\n    61\u2192    // It should be set to :ref:`the HTTP x-envoy-downstream-service-cluster\n    62\u2192    // <config_http_conn_man_headers_downstream-service-cluster>`\n    63\u2192    // If a more trusted source of the service name is available through mTLS/secure naming, it\n    64\u2192    // should be used.\n    65\u2192    string service = 2;\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    // The labels associated with the peer.\n    68\u2192    // These could be pod labels for Kubernetes or tags for VMs.\n    69\u2192    // The source of the labels could be an X.509 certificate or other configuration.\n    70\u2192    map<string, string> labels = 3;\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    // The authenticated identity of this peer.\n    73\u2192    // For example, the identity associated with the workload such as a service account.\n    74\u2192    // If an X.509 certificate is used to assert the identity this field should be sourced from\n    75\u2192    // ``URI Subject Alternative Names``, ``DNS Subject Alternate Names`` or ``Subject`` in that order.\n    76\u2192    // The primary identity should be the principal. The principal format is issuer specific.\n    77\u2192    //\n    78\u2192    // Examples:\n    79\u2192    //\n    80\u2192    // - SPIFFE format is ``spiffe://trust-domain/path``.\n    81\u2192    // - Google account format is ``https://accounts.google.com/{userid}``.\n    82\u2192    string principal = 4;\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    // The X.509 certificate used to authenticate the identify of this peer.\n    85\u2192    // When present, the certificate contents are encoded in URL and PEM format.\n    86\u2192    string certificate = 5;\n    87\u2192  }\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192  // Represents a network request, such as an HTTP request.\n    90\u2192  message Request {\n    91\u2192    option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n    92\u2192        \"envoy.service.auth.v2.AttributeContext.Request\";\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192    // The timestamp when the proxy receives the first byte of the request.\n    95\u2192    google.protobuf.Timestamp time = 1;\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192    // Represents an HTTP request or an HTTP-like request.\n    98\u2192    HttpRequest http = 2;\n    99\u2192  }\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192  // This message defines attributes for an HTTP request.\n   102\u2192  // HTTP/1.x, HTTP/2, gRPC are all considered as HTTP requests.\n   103\u2192  // [#next-free-field: 14]\n   104\u2192  message HttpRequest {\n   105\u2192    option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type =\n   106\u2192        \"envoy.service.auth.v2.AttributeContext.HttpRequest\";\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192    // The unique ID for a request, which can be propagated to downstream\n   109\u2192    // systems. The ID should have low probability of collision\n   110\u2192    // within a single day for a specific service.\n   111\u2192    // For HTTP requests, it should be X-Request-ID or equivalent.\n   112\u2192    string id = 1;\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    // The HTTP request method, such as ``GET``, ``POST``.\n   115\u2192    string method = 2;\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192    // The HTTP request headers. If multiple headers share the same key, they\n   118\u2192    // must be merged according to the HTTP spec. All header keys must be\n   119\u2192    // lower-cased, because HTTP header keys are case-insensitive.\n   120\u2192    // Header value is encoded as UTF-8 string. Non-UTF-8 characters will be replaced by \"!\".\n   121\u2192    // This field will not be set if\n   122\u2192    // :ref:`encode_raw_headers <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.encode_raw_headers>`\n   123\u2192    // is set to true.\n   124\u2192    map<string, string> headers = 3\n   125\u2192        [(udpa.annotations.field_migrate).oneof_promotion = \"headers_type\"];\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192    // A list of the raw HTTP request headers. This is used instead of\n   128\u2192    // :ref:`headers <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.HttpRequest.headers>` when\n   129\u2192    // :ref:`encode_raw_headers <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.encode_raw_headers>`\n   130\u2192    // is set to true.\n   131\u2192    //\n   132\u2192    // Note that this is not actually a map type. ``header_map`` contains a single repeated field\n   133\u2192    // ``headers``.\n   134\u2192    //\n   135\u2192    // Here, only the ``key`` and ``raw_value`` fields will be populated for each HeaderValue, and\n   136\u2192    // that is only when\n   137\u2192    // :ref:`encode_raw_headers <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.encode_raw_headers>`\n   138\u2192    // is set to true.\n   139\u2192    //\n   140\u2192    // Also, unlike the\n   141\u2192    // :ref:`headers <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.HttpRequest.headers>`\n   142\u2192    // field, headers with the same key are not combined into a single comma separated header.\n   143\u2192    config.core.v3.HeaderMap header_map = 13\n   144\u2192        [(udpa.annotations.field_migrate).oneof_promotion = \"headers_type\"];\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192    // The request target, as it appears in the first line of the HTTP request. This includes\n   147\u2192    // the URL path and query-string. No decoding is performed.\n   148\u2192    string path = 4;\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192    // The HTTP request ``Host`` or ``:authority`` header value.\n   151\u2192    string host = 5;\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    // The HTTP URL scheme, such as ``http`` and ``https``.\n   154\u2192    string scheme = 6;\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192    // This field is always empty, and exists for compatibility reasons. The HTTP URL query is\n   157\u2192    // included in ``path`` field.\n   158\u2192    string query = 7;\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192    // This field is always empty, and exists for compatibility reasons. The URL fragment is\n   161\u2192    // not submitted as part of HTTP requests; it is unknowable.\n   162\u2192    string fragment = 8;\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192    // The HTTP request size in bytes. If unknown, it must be -1.\n   165\u2192    int64 size = 9;\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192    // The network protocol used with the request, such as \"HTTP/1.0\", \"HTTP/1.1\", or \"HTTP/2\".\n   168\u2192    //\n   169\u2192    // See :repo:`headers.h:ProtocolStrings <source/common/http/headers.h>` for a list of all\n   170\u2192    // possible values.\n   171\u2192    string protocol = 10;\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192    // The HTTP request body.\n   174\u2192    string body = 11;\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192    // The HTTP request body in bytes. This is used instead of\n   177\u2192    // :ref:`body <envoy_v3_api_field_service.auth.v3.AttributeContext.HttpRequest.body>` when\n   178\u2192    // :ref:`pack_as_bytes <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.BufferSettings.pack_as_bytes>`\n   179\u2192    // is set to true.\n   180\u2192    bytes raw_body = 12;\n   181\u2192  }\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192  // This message defines attributes for the underlying TLS session.\n   184\u2192  message TLSSession {\n   185\u2192    // SNI used for TLS session.\n   186\u2192    string sni = 1;\n   187\u2192  }\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192  // The source of a network activity, such as starting a TCP connection.\n   190\u2192  // In a multi hop network activity, the source represents the sender of the\n   191\u2192  // last hop.\n   192\u2192  Peer source = 1;\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192  // The destination of a network activity, such as accepting a TCP connection.\n   195\u2192  // In a multi hop network activity, the destination represents the receiver of\n   196\u2192  // the last hop.\n   197\u2192  Peer destination = 2;\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192  // Represents a network request, such as an HTTP request.\n   200\u2192  Request request = 4;\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192  // This is analogous to http_request.headers, however these contents will not be sent to the\n   203\u2192  // upstream server. Context_extensions provide an extension mechanism for sending additional\n   204\u2192  // information to the auth server without modifying the proto definition. It maps to the\n   205\u2192  // internal opaque context in the filter chain.\n   206\u2192  map<string, string> context_extensions = 10;\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192  // Dynamic metadata associated with the request.\n   209\u2192  config.core.v3.Metadata metadata_context = 11;\n   210\u2192\n   211\u2192  // Metadata associated with the selected route.\n   212\u2192  config.core.v3.Metadata route_metadata_context = 13;\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192  // TLS session details of the underlying connection.\n   215\u2192  // This is not populated by default and will be populated only if the ext_authz filter has\n   216\u2192  // been specifically configured to include this information.\n   217\u2192  // For HTTP ext_authz, that requires :ref:`include_tls_session <config_http_filters_ext_authz>`\n   218\u2192  // to be set to true.\n   219\u2192  // For network ext_authz, that requires :ref:`include_tls_session <config_network_filters_ext_authz>`\n   220\u2192  // to be set to true.\n   221\u2192  TLSSession tls_session = 12;\n   222\u2192}\n   223\u2192"

Input
{
