  "max_turns": 25,
  "prompt": "Very thoroughly explore the Envoy codebase to find ALL test files related to the ext_authz filter. Search in:\n\n1. test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/\n2. test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/\n3. test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/\n4. Any integration tests related to ext_authz (check test/integration/ and similar directories)\n5. Any fuzz tests\n\nFor each test file found, note:\n- Full file path\n- What type of test it is (unit test, integration test, fuzz test)\n- Key test scenarios covered\n- What BUILD file references them\n\nAlso look for any test utility/helper files specific to ext_authz.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a4d8975",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive report.\n\n## Comprehensive Ext_Authz Test Files Exploration Report\n\nI have thoroughly explored the Envoy codebase and found all test files related to the ext_authz filter. Here's the complete inventory:\n\n---\n\n### 1. HTTP FILTER TESTS\n\n#### A. Unit Tests\n\n**File:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc`\n- **Type:** Unit test\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Key Test Classes and Scenarios:**\n  - `HttpFilterTestBase<T>` - Base class for HTTP filter tests\n  - `HttpFilterTest` - Main unit test fixture\n  - `HttpFilterTestParam` - Parameterized tests\n  - `EmitFilterStateTest` - Filter state emission tests\n  - `InvalidMutationTest` - Header/query parameter mutation validation\n  - `DecoderHeaderMutationRulesTest` - Decoder header mutation rule enforcement\n\n- **Key Test Scenarios (80+ tests):**\n  - Merge configuration\n  - Stats collection with prefixes\n  - Error handling (fail-close, fail-open, custom status codes)\n  - Request data buffering (partial messages, large buffers)\n  - Non-UTF8 request data and headers\n  - Header-only requests\n  - WebSocket and HTTP/2 upgrades\n  - Route-based configuration\n  - Header manipulation (add, append, remove, set)\n  - Cache clearing\n  - Metadata context handling\n  - Filter enable/disable behavior\n  - Deny at disable scenarios\n  - Dynamic metadata ingestion\n  - Query parameter mutations\n  - Response header manipulation\n  - Header mutation rules validation\n  - Filter state statistics emission\n\n---\n\n#### B. Configuration Test\n\n**File:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config_test.cc`\n- **Type:** Configuration/factory test\n- **Build Reference:** `config_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Test Classes:**\n  - `ExtAuthzFilterTest` - Configuration validation test\n  - `ExtAuthzFilterHttpTest` - HTTP service configuration test\n  - `ExtAuthzFilterGrpcTest` - GRPC service configuration test\n\n- **Key Test Scenarios:**\n  - HTTP service filter factory test\n  - GRPC service filter factory test\n  - Filter configuration with server context\n  - Envoy GRPC client creation\n  - Google GRPC client creation\n\n---\n\n#### C. Integration Tests\n\n**File:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc` (1793 lines)\n- **Type:** Full end-to-end integration tests with real GRPC/HTTP servers\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_integration_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Test Data File:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.yaml`\n- **Test Classes:**\n  - `ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest` - GRPC-based authorization server integration (29 parameterized tests)\n  - `ExtAuthzHttpIntegrationTest` - HTTP-based authorization server integration\n  - `ExtAuthzLocalReplyIntegrationTest` - Local response handling integration\n\n- **ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest Scenarios (29 tests):**\n  - HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 downstream requests with/without body\n  - Large request bodies (2MB+)\n  - Headers to add/append to upstream\n  - Allow/deny at filter disable\n  - Disable with metadata matchers\n  - Buffering completion checks\n  - Downstream headers on success\n  - Timeout handling (fail-close, fail-open)\n  - Response retries on 5xx\n  - Response mutation validation\n  - Failure mode allow with non-UTF8 data\n  - Google async client creation\n\n- **ExtAuthzHttpIntegrationTest Scenarios:**\n  - Legacy direct response (deprecated)\n  - Legacy redirect response (deprecated)\n  - Case-sensitive string matching\n  - Forwarded-for header handling\n  - Body handling and non-UTF8 bodies\n  - Direct response scenarios\n  - Error response with buffer limits\n  - Redirect response handling\n  - Timeout handling (fail-closed, fail-open)\n\n- **ExtAuthzLocalReplyIntegrationTest Scenarios (2 tests):**\n  - Denied header test\n  - Async client send local reply\n\n---\n\n#### D. Fuzz Tests\n\n**File 1:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_fuzz_test.cc`\n- **Type:** GRPC-based fuzz test\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_grpc_fuzz_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Corpus Directory:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_corpus/` (11 seed files)\n- **Seed Files:**\n  - `bad_config` - Invalid configuration handling\n  - `custom_status` - Custom HTTP status codes\n  - `denied_response` - Denial response scenarios\n  - `error_fail_close` - Error handling in fail-close mode\n  - `example` - Basic example\n  - `invalid_headers_to_remove` - Invalid header removal\n  - `invalid_query_parameters_to_set` - Invalid query param mutation\n  - `metadata_context` - Metadata context handling\n  - `no_regex_engine` - Regex engine absence handling\n  - `ok_response` - Success response handling\n  - `request_data` - Request data handling\n\n**File 2:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_fuzz_test.cc`\n- **Type:** HTTP-based fuzz test\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_http_fuzz_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Corpus Directory:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_corpus/` (9 seed files)\n- **Similar seed files to GRPC variant**\n\n---\n\n#### E. Fuzz Test Library/Utilities\n\n**File 1:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_lib.h`\n- **Type:** Fuzz test utility library header\n- **Classes:**\n  - `ReusableFilterFactory` - Reusable filter creation for fuzzing\n  - `ReusableFuzzerUtil` - Fuzzer utility for setup and validation\n\n**File 2:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_lib.cc`\n- **Type:** Fuzz test utility library implementation\n\n**File 3:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz.proto`\n- **Type:** Protobuf definition for fuzz test inputs\n- **Messages:**\n  - `ExtAuthzTestCaseBase` - Base configuration with HTTP request data and metadata\n  - `ExtAuthzTestCaseGrpc` - GRPC-specific test case with response or failure reason\n  - `ExtAuthzTestCaseHttp` - HTTP-specific test case with auth result\n\n---\n\n#### F. Test Filter (Integration Test Support)\n\n**Files:**\n- `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/logging_test_filter.cc`\n- `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/logging_test_filter.proto`\n- **Build Reference:** `logging_test_filter_lib` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Purpose:** Logging test filter used in integration tests to verify filter state statistics\n\n---\n\n### 2. NETWORK FILTER TESTS\n\n#### A. Unit Tests\n\n**File:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc`\n- **Type:** Unit test for network (L4) ext_authz filter\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Test Class:** `ExtAuthzFilterTest`\n- **Key Test Scenarios (13 tests):**\n  - Bad configuration handling\n  - OK response with onData call\n  - Denied response with onData call\n  - Fail-open mode\n  - Fail-close mode\n  - Connection closure handling (verify/do-not-call cancel)\n  - Immediate OK response\n  - Immediate NOK (denied) response\n  - Immediate error with fail-open\n  - Filter disable with metadata\n  - Filter enable with metadata\n\n---\n\n#### B. Configuration Test\n\n**File:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config_test.cc`\n- **Type:** Configuration/factory test\n- **Build Reference:** `config_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Key Test Scenarios:**\n  - Validate fail scenarios\n  - Correct proto handling\n\n---\n\n#### C. Fuzz Test\n\n**File:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_test.cc`\n- **Type:** Fuzz test for network filter\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_fuzz_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Corpus Directory:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_corpus/` (3 seed files)\n- **Seed Files:**\n  - `crash-72c994c40b30ff66b72f401055681e9851fea7a2` - Previous crash reproduction\n  - `crash-309531f09ce8c0c71f272c7145da9d5528c3e8fc` - Previous crash reproduction\n  - `re_program_size_too_big` - Regex program size boundary testing\n\n**Proto Definition:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz.proto`\n- **Messages:**\n  - `Result` - Authorization check status (error/denied/ok)\n  - `OnData` - Data and stream end handling\n  - `Action` - Fuzz actions (onNewConnection, onData, connection close)\n  - `ExtAuthzTestCase` - Network filter fuzz test case\n\n---\n\n### 3. COMMON FILTER TESTS (Shared Infrastructure)\n\n#### A. Implementation Tests\n\n**File 1:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl_test.cc`\n- **Type:** Unit test for GRPC implementation\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_grpc_impl_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Test Class:** `ExtAuthzGrpcClientTest`\n- **Key Test Scenarios (17 tests):**\n  - Authorization OK\n  - Stream info handling\n  - Authorization with all attributes\n  - Indifference to invalid headers\n  - Authorization denied\n  - Denied with unknown GRPC status\n  - Denied with all attributes\n  - Denied with empty denied response status\n  - Unknown error handling\n  - Cancelled authorization request\n  - Authorization request timeout\n  - Dynamic metadata handling\n  - Query parameter handling\n  - Append actions handling\n\n**File 2:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl_test.cc`\n- **Type:** Unit test for HTTP implementation\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_http_impl_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Test Class:** `ExtAuthzHttpClientTest`\n- **Key Test Scenarios (20+ tests):**\n  - Stream info handling\n  - Client configuration\n  - Default allowed client and upstream headers\n  - Path prefix sanitization\n  - Authorization with path rewrite\n  - Raw body handling\n  - Content-length zero handling\n  - Encode raw headers\n  - Authorization OK with various header manipulations\n  - Authorization with added headers from stream info\n  - Authorization with dynamic metadata\n  - Authorization denied\n  - Authorization denied with all attributes\n  - Request error handling\n  - 5xx error handling\n  - Cancelled authorization requests\n  - No cluster scenarios\n\n**File 3:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils_test.cc`\n- **Type:** Unit test for check request utility functions\n- **Build Reference:** `check_request_utils_test` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Test Class:** `CheckRequestUtilsTest`\n- **Key Test Scenarios (24 tests):**\n  - TCP basic request building\n  - TCP with peer certificate\n  - TCP with TLS session\n  - TCP with TLS session without SNI\n  - HTTP basic request building\n  - HTTP with duplicate headers\n  - HTTP with request header allowlist\n  - HTTP with request header denylist\n  - HTTP with allowlist and denylist\n  - HTTP with partial body\n  - HTTP with full body\n  - HTTP with body packed as bytes\n  - HTTP with headers as bytes\n  - Headers as bytes without concatenation\n  - Headers as bytes with existing partial body\n  - Check attribute context for peer\n  - Check attribute context for peer URI SANs\n  - Check attribute context for peer DNS SANs\n  - Check attribute context subject\n  - Check attribute context peer certificate\n  - Check attribute context peer TLS session\n  - Check attribute context peer TLS session without SNI\n\n---\n\n#### B. Mock/Utility Classes\n\n**File 1:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/mocks.h` & `mocks.cc`\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_mocks` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Classes:**\n  - `MockClient` - Mock implementation of authorization client\n  - `MockRequestCallbacks` - Mock implementation of request callbacks\n\n**File 2:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/test_common.h` & `test_common.cc`\n- **Build Reference:** `ext_authz_test_common` (in `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD`)\n- **Class:** `TestCommon`\n- **Utilities:**\n  - `makeMessageResponse()` - Create HTTP message responses\n  - `makeCheckResponse()` - Create GRPC check responses\n  - `makeAuthzResponse()` - Create authorization responses\n  - `makeHeaderValueOption()` - Create header value options\n  - Comparison functions for headers, query params, and header names\n  - Custom GTEST matchers: `AuthzErrorResponse`, `AuthzResponseNoAttributes`, `AuthzDeniedResponse`, `AuthzOkResponse`, `ContainsPairAsHeader`\n\n---\n\n### 4. SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| Test Location | File Count | Test Type | Build Targets | Key Coverage |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| HTTP Filters | 5 files | Unit, Config, Integration, Fuzz | 6 targets | Filter behavior, config validation, E2E scenarios, fuzzing |\n| Network Filters | 3 files | Unit, Config, Fuzz | 3 targets | L4 filter behavior, connection handling, fuzzing |\n| Common Filters | 5 files | Unit, Mocks | 5 targets | GRPC/HTTP client implementations, check request utilities |\n| **TOTAL** | **13 files** | **Multiple types** | **14+ targets** | **Comprehensive ext_authz testing** |\n\n---\n\n### 5. BUILD FILE REFERENCES\n\n**HTTP Filters BUILD:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD`\n- Defines 8 targets: 3 unit tests, 2 fuzz tests, 1 integration test, 1 library, 1 filter\n\n**Network Filters BUILD:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/BUILD`\n- Defines 4 targets: 2 unit tests, 1 fuzz test, 1 proto library\n\n**Common Filters BUILD:** `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD`\n- Defines 5 targets: 3 unit tests, 2 mock libraries\n\n---\n\nThis comprehensive exploration covers all ext_authz test files across HTTP, network, and common filter categories, including unit tests, configuration tests, integration tests, fuzz tests, and supporting utilities.",

\"6gig\",\n    50\u2192    deps = [\n    51\u2192        \"//source/common/formatter:formatter_extension_lib\",\n    52\u2192        \"//source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_lib\",\n    53\u2192        \"//test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_test_common\",\n    54\u2192        \"//test/mocks/server:server_factory_context_mocks\",\n    55\u2192        \"//test/mocks/stream_info:stream_info_mocks\",\n    56\u2192        \"//test/mocks/upstream:cluster_manager_mocks\",\n    57\u2192        \"@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto\",\n    58\u2192        \"@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto\",\n    59\u2192    ],\n    60\u2192)\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192envoy_cc_mock(\n    63\u2192    name = \"ext_authz_mocks\",\n    64\u2192    srcs = [\"mocks.cc\"],\n    65\u2192    hdrs = [\"mocks.h\"],\n    66\u2192    deps = [\n    67\u2192        \"//source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_interface\",\n    68\u2192        \"@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto\",\n    69\u2192    ],\n    70\u2192)\n = [\n    77\u2192        \"//source/common/http:headers_lib\",\n    78\u2192        \"//source/common/protobuf\",\n    79\u2192        \"//source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_lib\",\n    80\u2192        \"//test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_mocks\",\n    81\u2192        \"//test/mocks/grpc:grpc_mocks\",\n    82\u2192        \"//test/test_common:utility_lib\",\n    83\u2192        \"@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto\",\n    84\u2192        \"@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto\",\n    85\u2192        \"@envoy_api//envoy/type/v3:pkg_cc_proto\",\n    86\u2192    ],\n    87\u2192)\n    88\u2192"
40. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore ext_authz failure modes",
  "max_turns": 25,
  "prompt": "Thoroughly explore the Envoy ext_authz filter implementation to understand all failure modes and error handling. Focus on:\n\n1. In source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc - Look at:\n   - How does onComplete() handle different CheckStatus values (OK, Error, Denied)?\n   - What happens when failure_mode_allow is set?\n   - What happens on timeout?\n   - What happens when the auth service is unavailable?\n   - What response codes are sent back on denial vs error?\n\n2. In source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc:\n   - How does onFailure() handle gRPC failures?\n   - How is the response status determined?\n\n3. In source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.cc:\n   - How does the HTTP client handle failures?\n   - What status codes trigger what behavior?\n\n4. Look for any rate limiting, circuit breaking, or retry logic.\n\n5. What are the ResponseCodeDetails strings used (e.g., \"ext_authz_error\", \"ext_authz_denied\")?\n\nProvide specific function names, line numbers where possible, and the exact error handling logic.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a7935ae",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now let me compile all the findings into a comprehensive report:\n\n## Comprehensive Analysis: Envoy ext_authz Filter Failure Modes and Error Handling\n\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Envoy ext_authz filter implementation, here is a detailed analysis of all failure modes and error handling mechanisms:\n\n---\n\n### 1. HTTP FILTER FAILURE HANDLING (`ext_authz.cc`)\n\n#### **onComplete() Function - Line 470**\n\nThe main handler for authorization responses with three distinct paths:\n\n##### **Case 1: CheckStatus::OK (Lines 501-744)**\n- **Status Code Details**: Returns `ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzOk` (implicitly, no special code detail)\n- **Request Mutations**: Applies all response headers and query parameters\n- **Dynamic Metadata**: Injects `ext_authz_duration` if timing data available\n- **Statistics**: Increments `ext_authz_ok_` stat counter\n- **Flow**: Calls `continueDecoding()` to allow request through\n- **Header Mutations**:\n  - `headers_to_set`: Uses `setCopy()` (replaces)\n  - `headers_to_add`: Uses `addCopy()` (appends)\n  - `headers_to_append`: Appends to existing headers with comma separation\n  - All mutations validated if `validate_mutations_` is true\n- **Route Cache**: Clears if configured and any mutations present (line 505-510)\n- **Validation**: Invalid headers trigger `rejectResponse()` (500 Internal Server Error)\n\n##### **Case 2: CheckStatus::Denied (Lines 747-805)**\n- **Status Code Details**: `AuthzDenied` = `\"ext_authz_denied\"`\n- **HTTP Status Code**: Uses response's `status_code` (default 403 Forbidden from line 122 in gRPC impl)\n- **Statistics**: \n  - Increments `ext_authz_denied_` counter\n  - Charges cluster response stats if configured\n- **Response Code**: Sends configured status code (default 403)\n- **Response Headers**: Applied from `response->headers_to_set` only\n- **Response Flag**: Sets `UnauthorizedExternalService` flag\n- **Body**: Sends `response->body` from authz service\n- **Flow**: Calls `sendLocalReply()` and stops request\n\n##### **Case 3: CheckStatus::Error (Lines 808-836)**\n\n**Sub-case 3a: failure_mode_allow = true (Lines 813-824)**\n- **Behavior**: Allows request through despite error\n- **Statistics**:\n  - Increments `ext_authz_error_` counter\n  - Increments `failure_mode_allowed_` counter\n  - Increments cluster scope `ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_` if available\n- **Header Injection**: If `failure_mode_allow_header_add_` is true:\n  - Adds header: `x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true` (from line 821-822)\n- **Flow**: Calls `continueDecoding()` - request continues\n- **Log**: \"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\"\n\n**Sub-case 3b: failure_mode_allow = false (Lines 825-834)**\n- **Behavior**: Rejects request (fail-closed)\n- **Status Code**: Uses `config_->statusOnError()` (configurable, default 403 Forbidden per line 80)\n- **Status Code Details**: `AuthzError` = `\"ext_authz_error\"`\n- **Response Code**: HTTP 403 or configured `status_on_error` code\n- **Response Flag**: Sets `UnauthorizedExternalService` flag\n- **Body**: Empty string\n- **Statistics**: Increments `ext_authz_error_` counter\n- **Flow**: Calls `sendLocalReply()` and stops request\n- **Log**: \"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error\"\n\n---\n\n#### **Invalid Response Handling - rejectResponse() (Lines 840-852)**\n\nCalled when authz response contains invalid headers/query parameters (when validation enabled):\n- **Status Code**: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error\n- **Status Code Details**: `AuthzInvalid` = `\"ext_authz_invalid\"`\n- **Statistics**: Increments `ext_authz_invalid_` counter\n- **Response Flag**: Sets `UnauthorizedExternalService` flag\n- **Body**: Empty string\n- **Log**: \"ext_authz filter invalidated the response\"\n\n---\n\n#### **Filter Disabled Handling - decodeHeaders() (Lines 266-278)**\n\nWhen filter is disabled via runtime or metadata:\n- **Statistics**: Increments `disabled_` counter\n- **If deny_at_disable = true**: \n  - Rejects with status code from `statusOnError()`\n  - Sets `UnauthorizedExternalService` flag\n  - Response code detail: `AuthzError`\n- **If deny_at_disable = false**: Continues (passes through)\n\n---\n\n### 2. GRPC CLIENT IMPLEMENTATION (`ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc`)\n\n#### **onSuccess() (Lines 106-143)**\n- **gRPC Status Check**: `response->status().code() == Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Ok`\n- **If OK**:\n  - Sets `CheckStatus::OK`\n  - Applies mutations from `ok_response`\n  - Sets tracing tag: `TraceOk`\n- **If NOT OK** (any non-zero gRPC status):\n  - Sets `CheckStatus::Denied`\n  - Default status code: **403 Forbidden** (line 122)\n  - Can override with `denied_response.status().code()` if provided\n  - Applies headers from `denied_response.headers()`\n  - Sets tracing tag: `TraceUnauthz`\n- **Dynamic Metadata**: Prefers `ok_response.dynamic_metadata()` if present, falls back to outer `dynamic_metadata` (lines 136-140)\n\n#### **onFailure() (Lines 146-156)**\n\nHandles any gRPC failure (network error, timeout, unavailable service, etc.):\n- **All Failure Types**: gRPC error status != Ok\n- **Response Status**: `CheckStatus::Error`\n- **Status Code**: HTTP 403 Forbidden\n- **Tracing**: Logs grpc status with `grpcStatusToString(status)`\n- **Examples of failures**:\n  - `DeadlineExceeded`: Timeout scenario\n  - `Unavailable`: Service unavailable\n  - `Unknown`: Generic gRPC errors\n  - Network failures, service crashes, etc.\n\n#### **Timeout Handling (Lines 80-99)**\n- **Configuration**: Optional timeout in milliseconds via constructor\n- **Implementation**: `options.setTimeout(timeout_)` passed to async client\n- **Result**: When timeout expires, gRPC client returns `DeadlineExceeded` status to `onFailure()`\n\n---\n\n### 3. HTTP CLIENT IMPLEMENTATION (`ext_authz_http_impl.cc`)\n\n#### **onSuccess() (Lines 291-294)**\n- Converts HTTP response to `Response` object via `toResponse()`\n- Calls callback with result\n\n#### **toResponse() (Lines 318-397)**\n\n**5xx Status Code Handling (Lines 324-325)**\n- **Trigger**: Any HTTP 5xx status (500-599)\n- **Response Status**: `CheckStatus::Error`\n- **Behavior**: Returns error response (same as network failure)\n- **Log**: Via static `errorResponse()` singleton\n\n**HTTP 200 OK (Lines 354-374)**\n- **Response Status**: `CheckStatus::OK`\n- **Headers Processing**:\n  - Extracts `x-envoy-auth-headers-to-remove` header\n  - Uses `upstream_header_matchers_` for request headers\n  - Uses `client_header_on_success_matchers_` for response headers\n- **Header Storage**: Removes storage header before processing\n\n**All Other Status Codes (Lines 377-397)**\n- **Response Status**: `CheckStatus::Denied`\n- **HTTP Status Code**: From response (default varies, but not 403 - uses actual response code)\n- **Headers Processing**:\n  - Uses `client_header_matchers_` for response headers\n  - Uses `upstream_header_to_append_matchers_` for request headers\n- **Body**: Sends `message->bodyAsString()` from authz service\n- **Header Matchers**:\n  - If empty, blocks all except Host header\n  - If configured, uses specified patterns + default set (Status, Content-Length, WWW-Authenticate, Location)\n\n#### **onFailure() (Lines 297-303)**\n\nNetwork/async client failures:\n- **Failure Reasons**: \n  - `Reset`: Connection reset by peer\n  - `ExceedResponseBufferLimit`: Response too large\n- **Response Status**: `CheckStatus::Error`\n- **Behavior**: Returns error response\n- **Note**: No direct retry logic at this level\n\n#### **Cluster Not Found (Lines 271-276)**\n\nWhen configured authz cluster doesn't exist:\n- **Condition**: `cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(cluster) == nullptr`\n- **Response Status**: `CheckStatus::Error`\n- **Log**: \"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\"\n- **Statistics**: No cluster-specific stats incremented\n\n#### **Timeout Handling (Lines 279-280)**\n- **Configuration**: `config_->timeout()` in milliseconds\n- **Implementation**: `RequestOptions.setTimeout()` \n- **Result**: When timeout expires, HTTP async client calls `onFailure()` with appropriate reason\n\n---\n\n### 4. RESPONSE CODE DETAILS CONSTANTS (`ext_authz.h`)\n\nLocated in `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h` (Lines 39-46):\n\n```\nAuthzDenied    = \"ext_authz_denied\"       // Request explicitly denied\nAuthzError     = \"ext_authz_error\"        // Service error (timeout, unavailable, etc.)\nAuthzInvalid   = \"ext_authz_invalid\"      // Invalid authz response (bad headers/params)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. STATISTICS COUNTERS\n\nAll statistics prefixed with `ext_authz.<optional_filter_stats_prefix>`:\n\n1. **ok**: Authorization succeeded\n2. **denied**: Request explicitly denied\n3. **error**: Service error occurred\n4. **invalid**: Invalid response received\n5. **disabled**: Filter disabled by runtime\n6. **failure_mode_allowed**: Error occurred but allowed through due to `failure_mode_allow: true`\n7. **ignored_dynamic_metadata**: Dynamic metadata injection disabled\n\nPer-cluster stats (deprecated but still supported):\n- `ext_authz_ok_`\n- `ext_authz_denied_`\n- `ext_authz_error_`\n- `ext_authz_invalid_`\n- `ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_`\n\n---\n\n### 6. CONFIGURATION PARAMETERS\n\n**Failure Mode Control:**\n- `failure_mode_allow`: When true, errors allow request through; when false (default), errors block request\n- `failure_mode_allow_header_add`: When true with failure_mode_allow, adds `x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true` header\n- `status_on_error`: HTTP status code for error responses (default 403 Forbidden, configurable to any valid HTTP code)\n\n**Service Configuration:**\n- **gRPC**: `timeout` in milliseconds (optional, no default = no timeout)\n- **HTTP**: `timeout` in milliseconds (required field, default varies by config parsing)\n\n---\n\n### 7. FILTER FLOW WITH ERRORS\n\n```\ndecodeHeaders()\n  \u251c\u2500 Check if disabled\n  \u2502  \u2514\u2500 If deny_at_disable=true \u2192 sendLocalReply(statusOnError, \"ext_authz_error\")\n  \u251c\u2500 Initiate check() call to authz service\n  \u2514\u2500 Return StopAllIterationAndWatermark\n\n[Async Wait for Response...]\n\nonComplete(response)\n  \u251c\u2500 CheckStatus::OK \u2192 Apply mutations, continueDecoding()\n  \u251c\u2500 CheckStatus::Denied \u2192 sendLocalReply(response->status_code, \"ext_authz_denied\")\n  \u2514\u2500 CheckStatus::Error\n      \u251c\u2500 If failure_mode_allow \u2192 Add header (if configured), continueDecoding()\n      \u2514\u2500 If !failure_mode_allow \u2192 sendLocalReply(statusOnError, \"ext_authz_error\")\n```\n\n---\n\n### 8. UNAVAILABILITY SCENARIOS\n\n| Scenario | gRPC Behavior | HTTP Behavior | Result Status |\n|----------|---------------|---------------|----------------|\n| Service timeout | `DeadlineExceeded` \u2192 `onFailure()` | Timeout \u2192 `onFailure()` | `CheckStatus::Error` |\n| Service crash/down | `Unavailable` \u2192 `onFailure()` | Connection reset \u2192 `onFailure()` | `CheckStatus::Error` |\n| Cluster removed | Network layer | `onFailure()` (no cluster) | `CheckStatus::Error` |\n| Response too large | (gRPC has limits) | `ExceedResponseBufferLimit` \u2192 `onFailure()` | `CheckStatus::Error` |\n| Service returns 5xx | Treated as Denied | HTTP 5xx \u2192 `CheckStatus::Error` | `CheckStatus::Error` |\n| Network error | Various gRPC statuses | Reset \u2192 `onFailure()` | `CheckStatus::Error` |\n\n---\n\n### 9. NETWORK FILTER VARIANT (`network/ext_authz.cc`)\n\nThe network filter follows similar patterns:\n- **onComplete()**: Lines 72-96\n- Same three status cases: OK, Denied, Error\n- Statistics tracked but no response code details (network layer)\n- No header mutation capability\n- Can cancel in-flight checks on connection close (lines 60-69)\n\n---\n\n### 10. KEY DIFFERENCES: gRPC vs HTTP Client\n\n| Aspect | gRPC | HTTP |\n|--------|------|------|\n| Non-OK gRPC status | \u2192 Denied (403) | N/A |\n| HTTP 5xx response | N/A | \u2192 Error |\n| HTTP non-5xx response | N/A | \u2192 Denied (use response code) |\n| Dynamic metadata | From response proto | From headers only |\n| Denied response code | Configurable per response | Uses actual response code |\n| Header mutations on OK | Full support (proto) | Via header matchers |\n| Timeout behavior | `DeadlineExceeded` status | `onFailure()` callback |\n\n---\n\n### 11. CONFIGURATION FLOW\n\n```yaml\next_authz:\n  failure_mode_allow: bool           # Error handling mode\n  failure_mode_allow_header_add: bool # Add header on error\n  status_on_error:\n    code: 403                         # HTTP code for errors\n  \n  grpc_service:\n    timeout: 250ms                    # gRPC timeout\n    \n  http_service:\n    server_uri:\n      timeout: 250ms                  # HTTP timeout\n    authorization_response:\n      allowed_upstream_headers: [...]  # Response header matchers\n      allowed_client_headers: [...]    # For denied responses\n```\n\n---\n\nThis comprehensive analysis covers all failure modes, error handling paths, response codes, statistics tracking, and configuration options for the Envoy ext_authz filter across both HTTP and network variants.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Thoroughly explore the Envoy ext_authz filter implementation to understand all failure modes and error handling. Focus on:\n\n1. In source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc - Look at:\n   - How does onComplete() handle different CheckStatus values (OK, Error, Denied)?\n   - What happens when failure_mode_allow is set?\n   - What happens on timeout?\n   - What happens when the auth service is unavailable?\n   - What response codes are sent back on denial vs error?\n\n2. In source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc:\n   - How does onFailure() handle gRPC failures?\n   - How is the response status determined?\n\n3. In source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.cc:\n   - How does the HTTP client handle failures?\n   - What status codes trigger what behavior?\n\n4. Look for any rate limiting, circuit breaking, or retry logic.\n\n5. What are the ResponseCodeDetails strings used (e.g., \"ext_authz_error\", \"ext_authz_denied\")?\n\nProvide specific function names, line numbers where possible, and the exact error handling logic.",

HttpFilterTestBase : public T {\n    59\u2192public:\n    60\u2192  HttpFilterTestBase() {}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192  void initialize(std::string&& yaml) {\n    63\u2192 ON_CALL(decoder_filter_callbacks_, filterConfigName()).WillByDefault(Return(FilterConfigName));\n    76\u2192    filter_->setDecoderFilterCallbacks(decoder_filter_callbacks_);\n    77\u2192 81\u2192  static envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz\n    82\u2192  getFilterConfig(bool failure_mode_allow, bool http_client, bool emit_filter_state_stats = false,\n    83\u2192                  absl::optional<Envoy::ProtobufWkt::Struct> filter_metadata = absl::nullopt) {\n    84\u2192    const std::string http_config = R\"EOF(\n    85\u2192    failure_mode_allow_header_add: true\n    86\u2192    http_service:\n    87\u2192      server_uri:\n    88\u2192        uri: \"ext_authz:9000\"\n    89\u2192        cluster: \"ext_authz\"\n    90\u2192        timeout: 0.25s\n    91\u2192    )EOF\";\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    const std::string grpc_config = R\"EOF(\n    94\u2192    failure_mode_allow_header_add: true\n    95\u2192    grpc_service:\n    96\u2192 "     1\u2192#include \"envoy/config/bootstrap/v3/bootstrap.pb.h\"\n     2\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/grpc_service.pb.h\"\n     3\u2192#include \"gmock/gmock.h\"\n    17\u2192#include \"gtest/gtest.h\"\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192using envoy::config::bootstrap::v3::Bootstrap;\n    20\u2192using testing::_;\n    21\u2192using testing::Invoke;\n    22\u2192using testing::NiceMock;\n    23\u2192using testing::Return;\n    24\u2192using testing::StrictMock;\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    27\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    28\u2192namespace HttpFilters {\n    29\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192class TestAsyncClientManagerImpl : public Grpc::AsyncClientManagerImpl {\n    32\u2192public:\n    33\u2192  TestAsyncClientManagerImpl(Upstream::ClusterManager& cm, ThreadLocal::Instance& tls,\n    34\u2192                             Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context,\n    35\u2192                             const Grpc::StatNames& stat_names,\n    36\u2192                             const Bootstrap::GrpcAsyncClientManagerConfig& config)\n    37\u2192      : Grpc::AsyncClientManagerImpl(cm, tls, context, stat_names, config) {}\n    38\u2192  absl::StatusOr<Grpc::AsyncClientFactoryPtr>\n    39\u2192  factoryForGrpcService(const envoy::config::core::v3::GrpcService&, Stats::Scope&, bool) override {\n    40\u2192    return std::make_unique<NiceMock<Grpc::MockAsyncClientFactory>>();\n    41\u2192  }\n    42\u2192};\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192class ExtAuthzFilterTest : public Event::TestUsingSimulatedTime,\n    45\u2192                           public Thread::RealThreadsTestHelper,\n    46\u2192                           public testing::Test {\n    47\u2192public:\n    48\u2192  ExtAuthzFilterTest() : RealThreadsTestHelper(5), stat_names_(symbol_table_) {\n    49\u2192    ON_CALL(context_.server_factory_context_, threadLocal())\n    50\u2192        .WillByDefault(testing::ReturnRef(tls()));\n    51\u2192    ON_CALL(context_.server_factory_context_, api()).WillByDefault(testing::ReturnRef(api()));\n    52\u2192    runOnMainBlocking([&]() {\n    53\u2192      async_client_manager_ = std::make_unique<TestAsyncClientManagerImpl>(\n    54\u2192          context_.server_factory_context_.cluster_manager_, tls(),\n    55\u2192          context_.server_factory_context_, stat_names_, Bootstrap::GrpcAsyncClientManagerConfig());\n    56\u2192    });\n    57\u2192  }\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192  ~ExtAuthzFilterTest() override {\n    60\u2192    // Reset the async client manager before shutdown threading.\n    61\u2192    // Because its dtor will try to post to event loop to clear thread local slot.\n    62\u2192    runOnMainBlocking([&]() { async_client_manager_.reset(); });\n    63\u2192    // TODO(chaoqin-li1123): clean this up when we figure out how to free the threading resources in\n    64\u2192    // RealThreadsTestHelper.\n    65\u2192    shutdownThreading();\n    66\u2192    exitThreads();\n    67\u2192  }\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192  Http::FilterFactoryCb createFilterFactory(\n    70\u2192      const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& ext_authz_config) {\n    71\u2192    // Delegate call to mock async client manager to real async client manager.\n    72\u2192    ON_CALL(context_.server_factory_context_.cluster_manager_.async_client_manager_,\n    73\u2192            getOrCreateRawAsyncClientWithHashKey(_, _, _))\n    74\u2192        .WillByDefault(\n    75\u2192            Invoke([&](const Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey& config_with_hash_key,\n    76\u2192                       Stats::Scope& scope, bool skip_cluster_check) {\n    77\u2192              return async_client_manager_->getOrCreateRawAsyncClientWithHashKey(\n    78\u2192                  config_with_hash_key, scope, skip_cluster_check);\n    79\u2192            }));\n    80\u2192    ExtAuthzFilterConfig factory;"

If a request is never sent, stats are not set. In those\n    39\u2192  // tests, we need to be able to override this to false.\n    40\u2192  absl::optional<bool> expect_stats_override;\n    41\u2192  // In timeout tests we expect zero response bytes.\n    42\u2192  bool stats_expect_response_bytes = true;\n    43\u2192};\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192struct WaitForSuccessfulUpstreamResponseOpts {\n    46\u2192  // Fields of type Headers must be set at initialization.\n    47\u2192  const Headers headers_to_add = {};\n    48\u2192  const Headers headers_to_append = {};\n    49\u2192  const Headers headers_to_remove = {};\n    50\u2192  const Http::TestRequestHeaderMapImpl new_headers_from_upstream = {};\n    51\u2192  const Http::TestRequestHeaderMapImpl headers_to_append_multiple = {};\n    52\u2192  bool failure_mode_allowed_header = false;\n    53\u2192};\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192class ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest\n    56\u2192    : public Grpc::BaseGrpcClientIntegrationParamTest,\n    57\u2192      public HttpIntegrationTest,\n    58\u2192      public testing::TestWithParam<\n    59\u2192          std::tuple<std::tuple<Network::Address::IpVersion, Grpc::ClientType>, bool, bool>> {\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192public:\n    62\u2192  ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest()\n    63\u2192      : HttpIntegrationTest(Http::CodecType::HTTP1, ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest::ipVersion()) {}\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192  static std::string testParamsToString(\n    66\u2192      const ::testing::TestParamInfo<\n    67\u2192          std::tuple<std::tuple<Network::Address::IpVersion, Grpc::ClientType>, bool, bool>>& p) {\n    68\u2192    return fmt::format(\n    69\u2192        \"{}_{}_{}_{}\", TestUtility::ipVersionToString(std::get<0>(std::get<0>(p.param))),\n    70\u2192        std::get<1>(std::get<0>(p.param)) == Grpc::ClientType::GoogleGrpc ? \"GoogleGrpc\"\n    71\u2192                                                                          : \"EnvoyGrpc\",\n    72\u2192        std::get<1>(p.param) ? \"RawHeaders\" : \"LegacyHeaders\",\n    73\u2192        std::get<2>(p.param) ? \"EmitFilterStateStats\" : \"DoNotEmitFilterStateStats\");\n    74\u2192  }\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  // BaseGrpcClientIntegrationParamTest\n    77\u2192  Network::Address::IpVersion ipVersion() const override {\n    78\u2192    return std::get<0>(std::get<0>(GetParam()));\n    79\u2192  }\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192  Grpc::ClientType clientType() const override { return std::get<1>(std::get<0>(GetParam())); }\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192  bool encodeRawHeaders() const { return std::get<1>(GetParam()); }\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  bool emitFilterStateStats() const { return std::get<2>(GetParam()); }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192  void createUpstreams() override {\n    88\u2192    HttpIntegrationTest::createUpstreams();\n    89\u2192    addFakeUpstream(Http::CodecType::HTTP2);\n    90\u2192  }\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192  void initializeConfig(GrpcInitializeConfigOpts opts = {}) {\n    93\u2192    config_helper_.addConfigModifier([this,\n    94\u2192                                      opts](envoy::config::bootstrap::v3::Bootstrap& bootstrap) {\n    95\u2192      auto* ext_authz_cluster = bootstrap.mutable_static_resources()->add_clusters();\n    96\u2192      ext_authz_cluster->MergeFrom(bootstrap.static_resources().clusters()[0]);\n    97\u2192      ext_authz_cluster->set_name(\"ext_authz_cluster\");\n    98\u2192      ConfigHelper::setHttp2(*ext_authz_cluster);\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192      TestUtility::loadFromYaml(base_filter_config_, proto_config_);"

136\u2192\n   137\u2192  if (config.has_allowed_headers() &&\n   138\u2192      config.http_service().authorization_request().has_allowed_headers()) {\n   139\u2192    throw EnvoyException(\"Invalid duplicate configuration for allowed_headers.\");\n   140\u2192  }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192  // An unset request_headers_matchers_ means that all client request headers are allowed through\n   143\u2192  // to the authz server; this is to preserve backwards compatibility when introducing\n   144\u2192  // allowlisting of request headers for gRPC authz servers. Pre-existing support is for\n   145\u2192  // HTTP authz servers only and defaults to blocking all but a few headers (i.e. Authorization,\n   146\u2192  // Method, Path and Host).\n   147\u2192  if (config.has_grpc_service() && config.has_allowed_headers()) {\n   148\u2192    allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n   149\u2192        config.allowed_headers(), false, factory_context);\n   150\u2192  } else if (config.has_http_service()) {\n   151\u2192    if (config.http_service().authorization_request().has_allowed_headers()) {\n   152\u2192      allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n   153\u2192          config.http_service().authorization_request().allowed_headers(), true, factory_context);\n   154\u2192    } else {\n   155\u2192      allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n   156\u2192          config.allowed_headers(), true, factory_context);\n   157\u2192    }\n   158\u2192  }\n   159\u2192  if (config.has_disallowed_headers()) {\n   160\u2192    disallowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n   161\u2192        config.disallowed_headers(), false, factory_context);\n   162\u2192  }\n   163\u2192}\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192void FilterConfigPerRoute::merge(const FilterConfigPerRoute& other) {\n   166\u2192  // We only merge context extensions here, and leave boolean flags untouched since those flags are\n   167\u2192  // not used from the merged config.\n   168\u2192  auto begin_it = other.context_extensions_.begin();\n   169\u2192  auto end_it = other.context_extensions_.end();\n   170\u2192  for (auto it = begin_it; it != end_it; ++it) {\n   171\u2192    context_extensions_[it->first] = it->second;\n   172\u2192  }\n   173\u2192}\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192void Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n   176\u2192  if (filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding) {\n   177\u2192    return;\n   178\u2192  }\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192  // Now that we'll definitely be making the request, add filter state stats if configured to do so.\n   181\u2192  const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\n   182\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n   183\u2192  if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value())) {\n   184\u2192    if (!filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\n   185\u2192      filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\n   186\u2192                            std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\n   187\u2192                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\n   188\u2192                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192      // This may return nullptr (if there's a value at this name whose type doesn't match or isn't\n   191\u2192      // mutable, for example), so we must check logging_info_ is not nullptr later.\n   192\u2192      logging_info_ =\n   193\u2192          filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n   194\u2192    }\n   195\u2192    if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n   196\u2192      stats_.filter_state_name_collision_.inc();\n   197\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug,\n   198\u2192                       \"Could not find logging info at {}! (Did another filter already put data \"\n   199\u2192                       \"at this name?)\",\n   200\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n   201\u2192    }\n   202\u2192  }\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192  absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\n   205\u2192  for (const FilterConfigPerRoute& cfg :\n   206\u2192       Http::Utility::getAllPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_)) {\n   207\u2192    if (maybe_merged_per_route_config.has_value()) {\n   208\u2192      maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().merge(cfg);\n   209\u2192    } else {\n   210\u2192      maybe_merged_per_route_config = cfg;\n   211\u2192    }\n   212\u2192  }\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192  Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string> context_extensions;\n   215\u2192  if (maybe_merged_per_route_config) {\n   216\u2192    context_extensions = maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().takeContextExtensions();\n   217\u2192  }\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192  // If metadata_context_namespaces or typed_metadata_context_namespaces is specified,\n   220\u2192  // pass matching filter metadata to the ext_authz service.\n   221\u2192  // If metadata key is set in both the connection and request metadata,\n   222\u2192  // then the value will be the request metadata value.\n   223\u2192  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata metadata_context;\n   224\u2192  fillMetadataContext({&decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata(),\n   225\u2192                       &decoder_callbacks_->connection()->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata()},\n   226\u2192                      config_->metadataContextNamespaces(),\n   227\u2192                      config_->typedMetadataContextNamespaces(), metadata_context);\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192  // Fill route_metadata_context from the selected route's metadata.\n   230\u2192  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata route_metadata_context;\n   231\u2192  if (decoder_callbacks_->route() != nullptr) {\n   232\u2192    fillMetadataContext({&decoder_callbacks_->route()->metadata()},\n   233\u2192                        config_->routeMetadataContextNamespaces(),\n   234\u2192                        config_->routeTypedMetadataContextNamespaces(), route_metadata_context);\n   235\u2192  }\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::createHttpCheck(\n   238\u2192      decoder_callbacks_, headers, std::move(context_extensions), std::move(metadata_context),\n   239\u2192      std::move(route_metadata_context), check_request_, max_request_bytes_, config_->packAsBytes(),\n   240\u2192      config_->headersAsBytes(), config_->includePeerCertificate(), config_->includeTLSSession(),\n   241\u2192      config_->destinationLabels(), config_->allowedHeadersMatcher(),\n   242\u2192      config_->disallowedHeadersMatcher());\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   245\u2192  // Store start time of ext_authz filter call\n   246\u2192  start_time_ = decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime();\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192  state_ = State::Calling;\n   249\u2192  filter_return_ = FilterReturn::StopDecoding; // Don't let the filter chain continue as we are\n   250\u2192                                               // going to invoke check call.\n   251\u2192  cluster_ = decoder_callbacks_->clusterInfo();\n   252\u2192  initiating_call_ = true;\n   253\u2192  client_->check(*this, check_request_, decoder_callbacks_->activeSpan(),\n   254\u2192                 decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo());\n   255\u2192  initiating_call_ = false;\n   256\u2192}\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {\n   259\u2192  Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route = decoder_callbacks_->route();\n   260\u2192  const auto per_route_flags = getPerRouteFlags(route);\n   261\u2192  skip_check_ = per_route_flags.skip_check_;\n   262\u2192  if (skip_check_) {\n   263\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   264\u2192  }\n   265\u2192\n   266\u2192  if (!config_->filterEnabled(decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata())) {\n   267\u2192    stats_.disabled_.inc();\n   268\u2192    if (config_->denyAtDisable()) {\n   269\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter is disabled. Deny the request.\",\n   270\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n   271\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\n   272\u2192          StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\n   273\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n   274\u2192          config_->statusOnError(), EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n   275\u2192          Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzError);\n   276\u2192      return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n   277\u2192    }\n   278\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   279\u2192  }\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192  request_headers_ = &headers;\n   282\u2192  const auto check_settings = per_route_flags.check_settings_;\n   283\u2192  buffer_data_ = (config_->withRequestBody() || check_settings.has_with_request_body()) &&\n   284\u2192                 !check_settings.disable_request_body_buffering() &&\n   285\u2192                 !(end_stream || Http::Utility::isWebSocketUpgradeRequest(headers) ||\n   286\u2192                   Http::Utility::isH2UpgradeRequest(headers));\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192  max_request_bytes_ = config_->maxRequestBytes();\n   289\u2192  if (buffer_data_) {\n   290\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192    allow_partial_message_ = check_settings.has_with_request_body()\n   293\u2192                                 ? check_settings.with_request_body().allow_partial_message()\n   294\u2192                                 : config_->allowPartialMessage();\n   295\u2192    if (check_settings.has_with_request_body()) {\n   296\u2192      max_request_bytes_ = check_settings.with_request_body().max_request_bytes();\n   297\u2192    }\n   298\u2192    if (!allow_partial_message_) {\n   299\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->setDecoderBufferLimit(max_request_bytes_);\n   300\u2192    }\n   301\u2192    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n   302\u2192  }\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192  // Initiate a call to the authorization server since we are not disabled.\n   305\u2192  initiateCall(headers);\n   306\u2192  return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding\n   307\u2192             ? Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark\n   308\u2192             : Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   309\u2192}\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   312\u2192  if (buffer_data_ && !skip_check_) {\n   313\u2192    const bool buffer_is_full = isBufferFull(data.length());\n   314\u2192    if (end_stream || buffer_is_full) {\n   315\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {}\",\n   316\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, buffer_is_full ? \"buffer is full\" : \"stream is ended\");\n   317\u2192      // Make sure data is available in initiateCall.\n   318\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->addDecodedData(data, true);\n   319\u2192      initiateCall(*request_headers_);\n   320\u2192      return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding\n   321\u2192                 ? Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark\n   322\u2192                 : Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\n   323\u2192    } else {\n   324\u2192      return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndBuffer;\n   325\u2192    }\n   326\u2192  }\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\n   329\u2192}\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap&) {\n   332\u2192  if (buffer_data_ && !skip_check_) {\n   333\u2192    if (filter_return_ != FilterReturn::StopDecoding) {\n   334\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request\",\n   335\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n   336\u2192      initiateCall(*request_headers_);\n   337\u2192    }\n   338\u2192    return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding ? Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration\n   339\u2192                                                        : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\n   340\u2192  }\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192  return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\n   343\u2192}\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus Filter::encode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&) {\n   346\u2192  return Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   347\u2192}\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::encodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers, bool) {\n   350\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   351\u2192                   \"ext_authz filter has {} response header(s) to add and {} response header(s) to \"\n   352\u2192                   \"set to the encoded response:\",\n   353\u2192                   *encoder_callbacks_, response_headers_to_add_.size(),\n   354\u2192                   response_headers_to_set_.size());\n   355\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_add_.empty()) {\n   356\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   357\u2192        trace, \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the encoded response:\", *encoder_callbacks_);\n   358\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_add_) {\n   359\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\n   360\u2192      headers.addCopy(key, value);\n   361\u2192    }\n   362\u2192  }\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_set_.empty()) {\n   365\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   366\u2192        trace, \"ext_authz filter set header(s) to the encoded response:\", *encoder_callbacks_);\n   367\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_set_) {\n   368\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\n   369\u2192      headers.setCopy(key, value);\n   370\u2192    }\n   371\u2192  }\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_add_if_absent_.empty()) {\n   374\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_add_if_absent_) {\n   375\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\n   376\u2192      if (auto header_entry = headers.get(key); header_entry.empty()) {\n   377\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the encoded response:\",\n   378\u2192                         *encoder_callbacks_);\n   379\u2192        headers.addCopy(key, value);\n   380\u2192      }\n   381\u2192    }\n   382\u2192  }\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  if (!response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_.empty()) {\n   385\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_) {\n   386\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *encoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   387\u2192      if (auto header_entry = headers.get(key); !header_entry.empty()) {\n   388\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   389\u2192            trace, \"ext_authz filter set header(s) to the encoded response:\", *encoder_callbacks_);\n   390\u2192        headers.setCopy(key, value);\n   391\u2192      }\n   392\u2192    }\n   393\u2192  }\n   394\u2192  return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n   395\u2192}\n   396\u2192\n   397\u2192Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::encodeData(Buffer::Instance&, bool) {\n   398\u2192  return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\n   399\u2192}\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap&) {\n   402\u2192  return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\n   403\u2192}\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192Http::FilterMetadataStatus Filter::encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap&) {\n   406\u2192  return Http::FilterMetadataStatus::Continue;\n   407\u2192}\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192void Filter::setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n   410\u2192  decoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n   411\u2192}\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192void Filter::updateLoggingInfo() {\n   414\u2192  if (!config_->emitFilterStateStats()) {\n   415\u2192    return;\n   416\u2192  }\n   417\u2192\n   418\u2192  if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n   419\u2192    return;\n   420\u2192  }\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192  // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\n   423\u2192  if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n   424\u2192    logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>(\n   425\u2192        decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value()));\n   426\u2192  }\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192  auto const* stream_info = client_->streamInfo();\n   429\u2192  if (stream_info == nullptr) {\n   430\u2192    return;\n   431\u2192  }\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192  const auto& bytes_meter = stream_info->getUpstreamBytesMeter();\n   434\u2192  if (bytes_meter != nullptr) {\n   435\u2192    logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());\n   436\u2192    logging_info_->setBytesReceived(bytes_meter->wireBytesReceived());\n   437\u2192  }\n   438\u2192  if (stream_info->upstreamInfo().has_value()) {\n   439\u2192    logging_info_->setUpstreamHost(stream_info->upstreamInfo()->upstreamHost());\n   440\u2192  }\n   441\u2192  absl::optional<Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr> cluster_info =\n   442\u2192      stream_info->upstreamClusterInfo();\n   443\u2192  if (cluster_info) {\n   444\u2192    logging_info_->setClusterInfo(std::move(*cluster_info));\n   445\u2192  }\n   446\u2192}\n   447\u2192\n   448\u2192void Filter::setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n   449\u2192  encoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n   450\u2192}\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192void Filter::onDestroy() {\n   453\u2192  if (state_ == State::Calling) {\n   454\u2192    state_ = State::Complete;\n   455\u2192    client_->cancel();\n   456\u2192  }\n   457\u2192}\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192CheckResult Filter::validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n   460\u2192    Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation, absl::string_view key,\n   461\u2192    absl::string_view value) const {\n   462\u2192  if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n   463\u2192                                       !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n   464\u2192    return CheckResult::FAIL;\n   465\u2192  }\n   466\u2192  // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder mutation rules.\n   467\u2192  return config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(operation, Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n   468\u2192}\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\n   471\u2192  state_ = State::Complete;\n   472\u2192  using Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus;\n   473\u2192  Stats::StatName empty_stat_name;\n   474\u2192\n   475\u2192  updateLoggingInfo();\n   476\u2192\n   477\u2192  if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\n   478\u2192    if (!config_->enableDynamicMetadataIngestion()) {\n   479\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   480\u2192                       \"Response is trying to inject dynamic metadata, but dynamic metadata \"\n   481\u2192                       \"ingestion is disabled. Ignoring...\",\n   482\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n   483\u2192      stats_.ignored_dynamic_metadata_.inc();\n   484\u2192    } else {\n   485\u2192      // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\n   486\u2192      if (start_time_.has_value() && response->status == CheckStatus::OK) {\n   487\u2192        ProtobufWkt::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\n   488\u2192        auto duration =\n   489\u2192            decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value();\n   490\u2192        ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\n   491\u2192            std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\n   492\u2192        (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\"ext_authz_duration\"] =\n   493\u2192            ext_authz_duration_value;\n   494\u2192      }\n   495\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\",\n   496\u2192                                                          response->dynamic_metadata);\n   497\u2192    }\n   498\u2192  }\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192  switch (response->status) {\n   501\u2192  case CheckStatus::OK: {\n   502\u2192    // Any changes to request headers or query parameters can affect how the request is going to be\n   503\u2192    // routed. If we are changing the headers we also need to clear the route\n   504\u2192    // cache.\n   505\u2192    if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\n   506\u2192        (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\n   507\u2192         !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\n   508\u2192         !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\n   509\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz is clearing route cache\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   510\u2192      decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\n   511\u2192    }\n   512\u2192\n   513\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   514\u2192                     \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   515\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\n   516\u2192      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n   517\u2192          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n   518\u2192      switch (check_result) {\n   519\u2192      case CheckResult::OK:\n   520\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   521\u2192        request_headers_->setCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n   522\u2192        break;\n   523\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n   524\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   525\u2192                         key, value);\n   526\u2192        break;\n   527\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n   528\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   529\u2192                         key, value);\n   530\u2192        rejectResponse();\n   531\u2192        return;\n   532\u2192      }\n   533\u2192    }\n   534\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_add) {\n   535\u2192      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n   536\u2192          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n   537\u2192      switch (check_result) {\n   538\u2192      case CheckResult::OK:\n   539\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   540\u2192        request_headers_->addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n   541\u2192        break;\n   542\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n   543\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   544\u2192                         key, value);\n   545\u2192        break;\n   546\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n   547\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   548\u2192                         key, value);\n   549\u2192        rejectResponse();\n   550\u2192        return;\n   551\u2192      }\n   552\u2192    }\n   553\u2192    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_append) {\n   554\u2192      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n   555\u2192          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::APPEND, key, value);\n   556\u2192      switch (check_result) {\n   557\u2192      case CheckResult::OK: {\n   558\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   559\u2192        Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\n   560\u2192        const auto header_to_modify = request_headers_->get(lowercase_key);\n   561\u2192        // TODO(dio): Add a flag to allow appending non-existent headers, without setting it\n   562\u2192        // first (via `headers_to_add`). For example, given:\n   563\u2192        // 1. Original headers {\"original\": \"true\"}\n   564\u2192        // 2. Response headers from the authorization servers {{\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\":\n   565\u2192        // \"2\"}}\n   566\u2192        //\n   567\u2192        // Currently it is not possible to add {{\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\": \"2\"}} (the\n   568\u2192        // intended combined headers: {{\"original\": \"true\"}, {\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\":\n   569\u2192        // \"2\"}}) to the request to upstream server by only sets `headers_to_append`.\n   570\u2192        if (!header_to_modify.empty()) {\n   571\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   572\u2192          // The current behavior of appending is by combining entries with the same key,\n   573\u2192          // into one entry. The value of that combined entry is separated by \",\".\n   574\u2192          // TODO(dio): Consider to use addCopy instead.\n   575\u2192          request_headers_->appendCopy(lowercase_key, value);\n   576\u2192        }\n   577\u2192        break;\n   578\u2192      }\n   579\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n   580\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   581\u2192                         key, value);\n   582\u2192        break;\n   583\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n   584\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\",\n   585\u2192                         *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   586\u2192        rejectResponse();\n   587\u2192        return;\n   588\u2192      }\n   589\u2192    }\n   590\u2192\n   591\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   592\u2192                     \"ext_authz filter removed header(s) from the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   593\u2192    for (const auto& key : response->headers_to_remove) {\n   594\u2192      // If the response contains an invalid header to remove, it's the same as trying to remove a\n   595\u2192      // header that doesn't exist, so just ignore it.\n   596\u2192      if (config_->validateMutations() && !Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key)) {\n   597\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n   598\u2192        continue;\n   599\u2192      }\n   600\u2192      // We don't allow removing any :-prefixed headers, nor Host, as removing them would make the\n   601\u2192      // request malformed. checkDecoderHeaderMutation also performs this check, however, so only\n   602\u2192      // perform this check explicitly if decoder header mutation rules is empty.\n   603\u2192      if (!config_->hasDecoderHeaderMutationRules() &&\n   604\u2192          !Http::HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(key)) {\n   605\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n   606\u2192        continue;\n   607\u2192      }\n   608\u2192      // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder header mutation rules.\n   609\u2192      Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\n   610\u2192      switch (config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(CheckOperation::REMOVE, lowercase_key,\n   611\u2192                                                  EMPTY_STRING)) {\n   612\u2192      case CheckResult::OK:\n   613\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n   614\u2192        request_headers_->remove(lowercase_key);\n   615\u2192        break;\n   616\u2192      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n   617\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   618\u2192                         key);\n   619\u2192        break;\n   620\u2192      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n   621\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   622\u2192                         key);\n   623\u2192        rejectResponse();\n   624\u2192        return;\n   625\u2192      }\n   626\u2192    }\n   627\u2192\n   628\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_add.empty()) {\n   629\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to add to the response:\",\n   630\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add.size());\n   631\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add) {\n   632\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n   633\u2192                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n   634\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n   635\u2192                           value);\n   636\u2192          rejectResponse();\n   637\u2192          return;\n   638\u2192        }\n   639\u2192      }\n   640\u2192      response_headers_to_add_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add.begin(),\n   641\u2192                                                    response->response_headers_to_add.end()};\n   642\u2192    }\n   643\u2192\n   644\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_set.empty()) {\n   645\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to set to the response:\",\n   646\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_set.size());\n   647\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_set) {\n   648\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n   649\u2192                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n   650\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n   651\u2192                           value);\n   652\u2192          rejectResponse();\n   653\u2192          return;\n   654\u2192        }\n   655\u2192      }\n   656\u2192      response_headers_to_set_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_set.begin(),\n   657\u2192                                                    response->response_headers_to_set.end()};\n   658\u2192    }\n   659\u2192\n   660\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.empty()) {\n   661\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to add to the response:\",\n   662\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.size());\n   663\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent) {\n   664\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n   665\u2192                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n   666\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n   667\u2192                           value);\n   668\u2192          rejectResponse();\n   669\u2192          return;\n   670\u2192        }\n   671\u2192      }\n   672\u2192      response_headers_to_add_if_absent_ =\n   673\u2192          Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.begin(),\n   674\u2192                             response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.end()};\n   675\u2192    }\n   676\u2192\n   677\u2192    if (!response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.empty()) {\n   678\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to set to the response:\",\n   679\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_,\n   680\u2192                       response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.size());\n   681\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists) {\n   682\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n   683\u2192                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n   684\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n   685\u2192                           value);\n   686\u2192          rejectResponse();\n   687\u2192          return;\n   688\u2192        }\n   689\u2192      }\n   690\u2192      response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_ =\n   691\u2192          Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.begin(),\n   692\u2192                             response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.end()};\n   693\u2192    }\n   694\u2192\n   695\u2192    absl::optional<Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti> modified_query_parameters;\n   696\u2192    if (!response->query_parameters_to_set.empty()) {\n   697\u2192      modified_query_parameters = Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti::parseQueryString(\n   698\u2192          request_headers_->Path()->value().getStringView());\n   699\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   700\u2192          trace, \"ext_authz filter set query parameter(s) on the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n   701\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->query_parameters_to_set) {\n   702\u2192        if (config_->validateMutations() &&\n   703\u2192            (!Http::Utility::PercentEncoding::queryParameterIsUrlEncoded(key) ||\n   704\u2192             !Http::Utility::PercentEncoding::queryParameterIsUrlEncoded(value))) {\n   705\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid query parameter {}={}.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n   706\u2192                           key, value);\n   707\u2192          rejectResponse();\n   708\u2192          return;\n   709\u2192        }\n   710\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}={}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n   711\u2192        modified_query_parameters->overwrite(key, value);\n   712\u2192      }\n   713\u2192    }\n   714\u2192\n   715\u2192    if (!response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty()) {\n   716\u2192      if (!modified_query_parameters) {\n   717\u2192        modified_query_parameters = Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti::parseQueryString(\n   718\u2192            request_headers_->Path()->value().getStringView());\n   719\u2192      }\n   720\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter removed query parameter(s) from the request:\",\n   721\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n   722\u2192      for (const auto& key : response->query_parameters_to_remove) {\n   723\u2192        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n   724\u2192        modified_query_parameters->remove(key);\n   725\u2192      }\n   726\u2192    }\n   727\u2192\n   728\u2192    // We modified the query parameters in some way, so regenerate the `path` header and set it\n   729\u2192    // here.\n   730\u2192    if (modified_query_parameters) {\n   731\u2192      const auto new_path =\n   732\u2192          modified_query_parameters->replaceQueryString(request_headers_->Path()->value());\n   733\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   734\u2192          trace, \"ext_authz filter modified query parameter(s), using new path for request: {}\",\n   735\u2192          *decoder_callbacks_, new_path);\n   736\u2192      request_headers_->setPath(new_path);\n   737\u2192    }\n   738\u2192\n   739\u2192    if (cluster_) {\n   740\u2192      config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_ok_);\n   741\u2192    }\n   742\u2192    stats_.ok_.inc();\n   743\u2192    continueDecoding();\n   744\u2192    break;\n   745\u2192  }\n   746\u2192\n   747\u2192  case CheckStatus::Denied: {\n   748\u2192 Response status code: '{}'\",\n   749\u2192                     *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(response->status_code));\n   750\u2192    stats_.denied_.inc();\n   751\u2192\n   752\u2192    if (cluster_) {\n   753\u2192      config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_denied_);\n   754\u2192      if (config_->chargeClusterResponseStats()) {\n   755\u2192 empty_stat_name,\n   758\u2192                                               enumToInt(response->status_code),\n   759\u2192                                               true,\n   760\u2192 empty_stat_name,\n   763\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\n   764\u2192                                               empty_stat_name,\n   765\u2192                                               false};\n   766\u2192        config_->httpContext().codeStats().chargeResponseStat(info, false);\n   767\u2192      }\n   768\u2192    }\n   769\u2192\n   770\u2192    // Check headers are valid.\n   771\u2192    if (config_->validateMutations()) {\n   772\u2192      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\n   773\u2192        if (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n   774\u2192            !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value)) {\n   775\u2192          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n   776\u2192                           value);\n   777\u2192          rejectResponse();\n   778\u2192          return;\n   779\u2192        }\n   780\u2192      }\n   781\u2192    }\n   782\u2192\n   783\u2192    // setResponseFlag must be called before sendLocalReply\n   784\u2192    decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\n   785\u2192        StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\n   786\u2192    decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n   787\u2192        response->status_code, response->body,\n   788\u2192
