                         \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the local response:\", callbacks);\n   792\u2192          // Firstly, remove all headers requested by the ext_authz filter, to ensure that they will\n   793\u2192          // override existing headers.\n   794\u2192          for (const auto& [key, _] : headers) {\n   795\u2192            response_headers.remove(Http::LowerCaseString(key));\n   796\u2192          }\n   797\u2192          // Then set all of the requested headers, allowing the same header to be set multiple\n   798\u2192          // times, e.g. `Set-Cookie`.\n   799\u2192          for (const auto& [key, value] : headers) {\n   800\u2192            ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \" '{}':'{}'\", callbacks, key, value);\n   801\u2192            response_headers.addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n   802\u2192          }\n   803\u2192        },\n   804\u2192        absl::nullopt, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzDenied);\n   805\u2192    break;\n   806\u2192  }\n   807\u2192\n   808\u2192  case CheckStatus::Error: {\n   809\u2192    if (cluster_) {\n   810\u2192      config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_error_);\n   811\u2192    }\n   812\u2192    stats_.error_.inc();\n   813\u2192    if (config_->failureModeAllow()) {\n   814\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\",\n   815\u2192                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n   816\u2192      stats_.failure_mode_allowed_.inc();\n   817\u2192      if (cluster_) {\n   818\u2192        config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_);\n   819\u2192 config_->ext_authz_invalid_);\n   846\u2192  }\n   847\u2192  stats_.invalid_.inc();\n   848\u2192  decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\n   849\u2192 Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzInvalid);\n   853\u2192}\n   854\u2192\n   855\u2192bool Filter::isBufferFull(uint64_t num_bytes_processing) const {\n   856\u2192  if (!allow_partial_message_) {\n   857\u2192    return false;\n   858\u2192  }\n   859\u2192\n   860\u2192  uint64_t num_bytes_buffered = num_bytes_processing;\n   861\u2192  const auto* buffer = decoder_callbacks_->decodingBuffer();\n   862\u2192  if (buffer != nullptr) {\n   863\u2192    num_bytes_buffered += buffer->length();\n   864\u2192  }\n   865\u2192\n   866\u2192  return num_bytes_buffered >= max_request_bytes_;\n   867\u2192}\n   868\u2192\n   869\u2192void Filter::continueDecoding() {\n   870\u2192  // After sending the check request, we don't need to buffer the data anymore.\n   871\u2192  buffer_data_ = false;\n   872\u2192\n   873\u2192  filter_return_ = FilterReturn::ContinueDecoding;\n   874\u2192  if (!initiating_call_) {\n   875\u2192    decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n   876\u2192  }\n   877\u2192}\n   878\u2192\n   879\u2192Filter::PerRouteFlags Filter::getPerRouteFlags(const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route) const {\n   880\u2192  if (route == nullptr) {\n   881\u2192    return PerRouteFlags{\n   882\u2192        true /*skip_check_*/,\n   883\u2192        envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings() /*check_settings_*/};\n   884\u2192  }\n   885\u2192\n   886\u2192  const auto* specific_check_settings =\n   887\u2192 PerRouteFlags{\n   894\u2192      false /*skip_check_*/,\n   895\u2192      envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings() /*check_settings_*/};\n   896\u2192}\n   897\u2192\n include_peer_certificate_;\n   252\u2192  const bool include_tls_session_;\n   253\u2192  const bool charge_cluster_response_stats_;\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192  // The stats for the filter.\n   256\u2192  ExtAuthzFilterStats stats_;\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::MatcherSharedPtr allowed_headers_matcher_;\n   259\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::MatcherSharedPtr disallowed_headers_matcher_;\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192public:\n   262\u2192  // TODO(nezdolik): deprecate cluster scope stats counters in favor of filter scope stats\n   263\u2192  // (ExtAuthzFilterStats stats_).\n   264\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_ok_;\n   265\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_denied_;\n   266\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_error_;\n   267\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_invalid_;\n   268\u2192  const Stats::StatName ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_;\n   269\u2192};\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192using FilterConfigSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<FilterConfig>;\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192/**\n   274\u2192 * Per route settings for ExtAuth. Allows customizing the CheckRequest on a\n   275\u2192 * virtualhost\\route\\weighted cluster level.\n   276\u2192 */\n   277\u2192class FilterConfigPerRoute : public Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfig {\n   278\u2192public:\n   279\u2192  using ContextExtensionsMap = Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>;\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192  FilterConfigPerRoute(\n   282\u2192      const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthzPerRoute& config)\n   283\u2192      : context_extensions_(config.has_check_settings()\n   284\u2192                                ? config.check_settings().context_extensions()\n   285\u2192                                : ContextExtensionsMap()),\n   286\u2192        check_settings_(config.has_check_settings()\n   287\u2192                            ? config.check_settings()\n   288\u2192                            : envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings()),\n   289\u2192        disabled_(config.disabled()) {\n   290\u2192    if (config.has_check_settings() && config.check_settings().disable_request_body_buffering() &&\n   291\u2192        config.check_settings().has_with_request_body()) {\n   292\u2192      ExceptionUtil::throwEnvoyException(\n   293\u2192          \"Invalid configuration for check_settings. Only one of disable_request_body_buffering or \"\n   294\u2192          \"with_request_body can be set.\");\n   295\u2192    }\n   296\u2192  }\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192  void merge(const FilterConfigPerRoute& other);\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192  /**\n   301\u2192   * @return Context extensions to add to the CheckRequest.\n   302\u2192   */\n   303\u2192  const ContextExtensionsMap& contextExtensions() const { return context_extensions_; }\n   304\u2192  // Allow moving the context extensions out of this object.\n   305\u2192  ContextExtensionsMap&& takeContextExtensions() { return std::move(context_extensions_); }\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192  bool disabled() const { return disabled_; }\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192  envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings checkSettings() const {\n   310\u2192    return check_settings_;\n   311\u2192  }\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192private:\n   314\u2192  // We save the context extensions as a protobuf map instead of a std::map as this allows us to\n   315\u2192  // move it to the CheckRequest, thus avoiding a copy that would incur by converting it.\n   316\u2192  ContextExtensionsMap context_extensions_;\n   317\u2192  envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings check_settings_;\n   318\u2192  bool disabled_;\n   319\u2192};\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192/**\n   322\u2192 * HTTP ext_authz filter. Depending on the route configuration, this filter calls the global\n   323\u2192 * ext_authz service before allowing further filter iteration.\n   324\u2192 */\n   325\u2192class Filter : public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::ext_authz>,\n   326\u2192               public Http::StreamFilter,\n   327\u2192               public Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks {\n   328\u2192public:\n   329\u2192  Filter(const FilterConfigSharedPtr& config, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr&& client)\n   330\u2192      : config_(config), client_(std::move(client)), stats_(config->stats()) {}\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192  // Http::StreamFilterBase\n   333\u2192  void onDestroy() override;\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoderFilter\n   336\u2192  Http::FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n   337\u2192                                          bool end_stream) override;\n   338\u2192  Http::FilterDataStatus decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   339\u2192  Http::FilterTrailersStatus decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers) override;\n   340\u2192  void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192  // Http::StreamEncoderFilter\n   343\u2192  Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus encode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) override;\n   344\u2192  Http::FilterHeadersStatus encodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers,\n   345\u2192                                          bool end_stream) override;\n   346\u2192  Http::FilterDataStatus encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   347\u2192  Http::FilterTrailersStatus encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) override;\n   348\u2192  Http::FilterMetadataStatus encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap& trailers) override;\n   349\u2192  void setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192  // ExtAuthz::RequestCallbacks\n   352\u2192  void onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&&) override;\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192private:\n   355\u2192  // Convenience function for the following:\n   356\u2192  // 1. If `validate_mutations` is set to true, validate header key and value.\n   357\u2192  // 2. If `decoder_header_mutation_rules` is set, check that mutation is allowed.\n   358\u2192  Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult\n   359\u2192  validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation,\n   360\u2192                                        absl::string_view key, absl::string_view value) const;\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192  // Called when the filter is configured to reject invalid responses & the authz response contains\n   363\u2192  // invalid header or query parameters. Sends a local response with the configured rejection status\n   364\u2192  // code.\n   365\u2192  void rejectResponse();\n   366\u2192\n   367\u2192  absl::optional<MonotonicTime> start_time_;\n   368\u2192  void addResponseHeaders(Http::HeaderMap& header_map, const Http::HeaderVector& headers);\n   369\u2192  void initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers);\n   370\u2192  void continueDecoding();\n   371\u2192  bool isBufferFull(uint64_t num_bytes_processing) const;\n   372\u2192  void updateLoggingInfo();\n   373\u2192\n   374\u2192  // This holds a set of flags defined in per-route configuration.\n   375\u2192  struct PerRouteFlags {\n   376\u2192    const bool skip_check_;\n   377\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::CheckSettings check_settings_;\n   378\u2192  };\n   379\u2192  PerRouteFlags getPerRouteFlags(const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route) const;\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192  // State of this filter's communication with the external authorization service.\n   382\u2192  // The filter has either not started calling the external service, in the middle of calling\n   383\u2192  // it or has completed.\n   384\u2192  enum class State { NotStarted, Calling, Complete };\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192  // FilterReturn is used to capture what the return code should be to the filter chain.\n   387\u2192  // if this filter is either in the middle of calling the service or the result is denied then\n   388\u2192  // the filter chain should stop. Otherwise, the filter chain can continue to the next filter.\n   389\u2192  enum class FilterReturn { ContinueDecoding, StopDecoding };\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192  Http::HeaderMapPtr getHeaderMap(const Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr& response);\n   392\u2192  FilterConfigSharedPtr config_;\n   393\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr client_;\n   394\u2192  Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks* decoder_callbacks_{};\n   395\u2192  Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks* encoder_callbacks_{};\n   396\u2192  Http::RequestHeaderMap* request_headers_;\n   397\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_add_{};\n   398\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_set_{};\n   399\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent_{};\n   400\u2192  Http::HeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_{};\n   401\u2192  State state_{State::NotStarted};\n   402\u2192  FilterReturn filter_return_{FilterReturn::ContinueDecoding};\n   403\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_;\n   404\u2192  // The stats for the filter.\n   405\u2192  ExtAuthzFilterStats stats_;\n   406\u2192  ExtAuthzLoggingInfo* logging_info_{nullptr};\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192  // This is used to hold the final configs after we merge them with per-route configs.\n   409\u2192  bool allow_partial_message_{};\n   410\u2192  uint32_t max_request_bytes_{};\n   411\u2192\n   412\u2192  // Used to identify if the callback to onComplete() is synchronous (on the stack) or asynchronous.\n   413\u2192  bool initiating_call_{};\n   414\u2192  bool buffer_data_{};\n   415\u2192  bool skip_check_{false};\n   416\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest check_request_{};\n   417\u2192};\n   418\u2192\n   419\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   420\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\n   421\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   422\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   423\u2192"

\"source/common/singleton/const_singleton.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    20\u2192namespace Filters {\n    21\u2192namespace Common {\n    22\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192/**\n    25\u2192 * Constant values used for tracing metadata.\n    26\u2192 */\n    27\u2192struct TracingConstantValues {\n    28\u2192  const std::string TraceStatus = \"ext_authz_status\";\n    29\u2192  const std::string TraceUnauthz = \"ext_authz_unauthorized\";\n    30\u2192  const std::string TraceOk = \"ext_authz_ok\";\n    31\u2192  const std::string HttpStatus = \"ext_authz_http_status\";\n    32\u2192};\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192using TracingConstants = ConstSingleton<TracingConstantValues>;\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192/**\n    37\u2192 * Possible constant response code details values for a check call.\n    38\u2192 */\n    39\u2192struct ResponseCodeDetailsValues {\n    40\u2192  // The ext_authz filter denied the downstream request/connection.\n    41\u2192  const std::string AuthzDenied = \"ext_authz_denied\";\n    42\u2192  // The ext_authz filter encountered a failure, and was configured to fail-closed.\n    43\u2192  const std::string AuthzError = \"ext_authz_error\";\n    44\u2192  // The ext_authz filter invalidated the CheckResponse and dropped the client request.\n    45\u2192  const std::string AuthzInvalid = \"ext_authz_invalid\";\n    46\u2192};\n    47\u2192using ResponseCodeDetails = ConstSingleton<ResponseCodeDetailsValues>;\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192/**\n    50\u2192 * Constant auth related HTTP headers. All lower case. This group of headers can\n    51\u2192 * contain prefix override headers.\n    52\u2192 */\n    53\u2192class HeaderValues {\n    54\u2192public:\n    55\u2192  const char* prefix() const { return ThreadSafeSingleton<Http::PrefixValue>::get().prefix(); }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthPartialBody{absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-partial-body\")};\n    58\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove{\n    59\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-headers-to-remove\")};\n    60\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed{\n    61\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-failure-mode-allowed\")};\n    62\u2192};\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192using Headers = ConstSingleton<HeaderValues>;\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192/**\n    67\u2192 * Possible async results for a check call.\n    68\u2192 */\n    69\u2192enum class CheckStatus {\n    70\u2192  // The request is authorized.\n    71\u2192  OK,\n    72\u2192  // The authz service could not be queried.\n    73\u2192  Error,\n    74\u2192  // The request is denied.\n    75\u2192  Denied\n    76\u2192};\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192using UnsafeHeader = std::pair<std::string, std::string>;\n    79\u2192using UnsafeHeaderVector = std::vector<UnsafeHeader>;\n    80\u2192/**\n    81\u2192 * Authorization response object for a RequestCallback.\n    82\u2192 *\n    83\u2192 * Header mutations and query parameter mutations are not expected to already be validated by the\n    84\u2192 * client before packing them into this struct (hence they are \"unsafe\").\n    85\u2192 */\n    86\u2192struct Response {\n    87\u2192  // Call status.\n    88\u2192  CheckStatus status;\n    89\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, that will be optionally appended\n    90\u2192  // to the request to the upstream server.\n    91\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_append;\n    92\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set\n    93\u2192  // (using \"setCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n    94\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_set;\n    95\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n    96\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n    97\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_add;\n    98\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n    99\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n   100\u2192  // responses.\n   101\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add;\n   102\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n   103\u2192  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses.\n   104\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_set;\n   105\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n   106\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n   107\u2192  // responses only if the headers were not returned from the authz server.\n   108\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent;\n   109\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n   110\u2192  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses\n   111\u2192  // only if the headers were returned from the authz server.\n   112\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists;\n   113\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers consumed by the authorization server, will be removed\n   114\u2192  // from the request to the upstream server.\n   115\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\n   116\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to be set (possibly overwritten) on the\n   117\u2192  // request to the upstream server.\n   118\u2192  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector query_parameters_to_set;\n   119\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to remove from the request to the upstream server.\n   120\u2192  std::vector<std::string> query_parameters_to_remove;\n   121\u2192  // Optional http body used only on denied response.\n   122\u2192  std::string body;\n   123\u2192  // Optional http status used only on denied response.\n   124\u2192  Http::Code status_code{};\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192  // A set of metadata returned by the authorization server, that will be emitted as filter's\n   127\u2192  // dynamic metadata that other filters can leverage.\n   128\u2192  ProtobufWkt::Struct dynamic_metadata;\n   129\u2192};\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192using ResponsePtr = std::unique_ptr<Response>;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192/**\n   134\u2192 * Async callbacks used during check() calls.\n   135\u2192 */\n   136\u2192class RequestCallbacks {\n   137\u2192public:\n   138\u2192  virtual ~RequestCallbacks() = default;\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  /**\n   141\u2192   * Called when a check request is complete. The resulting ResponsePtr is supplied.\n   142\u2192   */\n   143\u2192  virtual void onComplete(ResponsePtr&& response) PURE;\n   144\u2192};\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192class Client {\n   147\u2192public:\n   148\u2192  // Destructor\n   149\u2192  virtual ~Client() = default;\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  /**\n   152\u2192   * Cancel an inflight Check request.\n   153\u2192   */\n   154\u2192  virtual void cancel() PURE;\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  /**\n   157\u2192   * Request a check call to an external authorization service which can use the\n   158\u2192   * passed request parameters to make a permit/deny decision.\n   159\u2192   * @param callback supplies the completion callbacks.\n   160\u2192   *        NOTE: The callback may happen within the calling stack.\n   161\u2192   * @param request is the proto message with the attributes of the specific payload.\n   162\u2192   * @param parent_span source for generating an egress child span as part of the trace.\n   163\u2192   * @param stream_info supplies the client's stream info.\n   164\u2192   */\n   165\u2192  virtual void check(RequestCallbacks& callback,\n   166\u2192                     const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   167\u2192                     Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) PURE;\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  /**\n   170\u2192   * Returns streamInfo of the current request if possible. By default just return a nullptr.\n   171\u2192   */\n   172\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const { return nullptr; }\n   173\u2192};\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192using ClientPtr = std::unique_ptr<Client>;\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   178\u2192} // namespace Common\n   179\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   180\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   181\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   182\u2192"

"     1\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"source/common/grpc/async_client_impl.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"source/common/http/headers.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/network/utility.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/protobuf/protobuf.h\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    14\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    15\u2192namespace Filters {\n    16\u2192namespace Common {\n    17\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192void copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(\n    20\u2192    ResponsePtr& response,\n    21\u2192    const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::core::v3::HeaderValueOption>& headers) {\n    22\u2192  for (const auto& header : headers) {\n    23\u2192    if (header.append().value()) {\n    24\u2192      response->headers_to_append.emplace_back(header.header().key(), header.header().value());\n    25\u2192    } else {\n    26\u2192      response->headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(), header.header().value());\n    27\u2192    }\n    28\u2192  }\n    29\u2192}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192void copyOkResponseMutations(ResponsePtr& response,\n    32\u2192                             const envoy::service::auth::v3::OkHttpResponse& ok_response) {\n    33\u2192  copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(response, ok_response.headers());\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192  for (const auto& header : ok_response.response_headers_to_add()) {\n    36\u2192    if (header.has_append()) {\n    37\u2192      if (header.append().value()) {\n    38\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    39\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    40\u2192      } else {\n    41\u2192        response->response_headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    42\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    43\u2192      }\n    44\u2192    } else {\n    45\u2192      switch (header.append_action()) {\n    46\u2192        PANIC_ON_PROTO_ENUM_SENTINEL_VALUES;\n    47\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::APPEND_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD:\n    48\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    49\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    50\u2192        break;\n    51\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::ADD_IF_ABSENT:\n    52\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    53\u2192                                                                 header.header().value());\n    54\u2192        break;\n    55\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS:\n    56\u2192        response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    57\u2192                                                                       header.header().value());\n    58\u2192        break;\n    59\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD:\n    60\u2192        response->response_headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    61\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    62\u2192        break;\n    63\u2192      }\n    64\u2192    }\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192  response->headers_to_remove = std::vector<std::string>{ok_response.headers_to_remove().begin(),\n    68\u2192                                                         ok_response.headers_to_remove().end()};\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192  for (const auto& query_parameter : ok_response.query_parameters_to_set()) {\n    71\u2192    response->query_parameters_to_set.emplace_back(query_parameter.key(), query_parameter.value());\n    72\u2192  }\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192  response->query_parameters_to_remove =\n    75\u2192      std::vector<std::string>{ok_response.query_parameters_to_remove().begin(),\n    76\u2192                               ok_response.query_parameters_to_remove().end()};\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192GrpcClientImpl::GrpcClientImpl(const Grpc::RawAsyncClientSharedPtr& async_client,\n    80\u2192                               const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& timeout)\n    81\u2192    : async_client_(async_client), timeout_(timeout),\n    82\u2192      service_method_(*Protobuf::DescriptorPool::generated_pool()->FindMethodByName(\n    83\u2192          \"envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization.Check\")) {}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192GrpcClientImpl::~GrpcClientImpl() { ASSERT(!callbacks_); }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::cancel() {\n    88\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ != nullptr);\n    89\u2192  request_->cancel();\n    90\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n    91\u2192}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks,\n    94\u2192                           const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n    95\u2192                           Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) {\n    96\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr);\n    97\u2192  callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n    98\u2192  Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions options;\n    99\u2192  options.setTimeout(timeout_);\n   100\u2192  options.setParentContext(Http::AsyncClient::ParentContext{&stream_info});\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"Sending CheckRequest: {}\", request.DebugString());\n   103\u2192  request_ = async_client_->send(service_method_, request, *this, parent_span, options);\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::onSuccess(std::unique_ptr<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>&& response,\n   107\u2192                               Tracing::Span& span) {\n   108\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"Received CheckResponse: {}\", response->DebugString());\n   109\u2192  ResponsePtr authz_response = std::make_unique<Response>(Response{});\n   110\u2192  if (response->status().code() == Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Ok) {\n   111\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, TracingConstants::get().TraceOk);\n   112\u2192    authz_response->status = CheckStatus::OK;\n   113\u2192    if (response->has_ok_response()) {\n   114\u2192      const auto& ok_response = response->ok_response();\n   115\u2192      copyOkResponseMutations(authz_response, ok_response);\n   116\u2192    }\n   117\u2192  } else {\n   118\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, TracingConstants::get().TraceUnauthz);\n   119\u2192    authz_response->status = CheckStatus::Denied;\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    // The default HTTP status code for denied response is 403 Forbidden.\n   122\u2192    authz_response->status_code = Http::Code::Forbidden;\n   123\u2192    if (response->has_denied_response()) {\n   124\u2192      copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(authz_response, response->denied_response().headers());\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192      const uint32_t status_code = response->denied_response().status().code();\n   127\u2192      if (status_code > 0) {\n   128\u2192        authz_response->status_code = static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code);\n   129\u2192      }\n   130\u2192      authz_response->body = response->denied_response().body();\n   131\u2192    }\n   132\u2192  }\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192  // OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata is deprecated. Until OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata is\n   135\u2192  // removed, it overrides dynamic_metadata field of the outer check response.\n   136\u2192  if (response->has_ok_response() && response->ok_response().has_dynamic_metadata()) {\n   137\u2192    authz_response->dynamic_metadata = response->ok_response().dynamic_metadata();\n   138\u2192  } else {\n   139\u2192    authz_response->dynamic_metadata = response->dynamic_metadata();\n   140\u2192  }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::move(authz_response));\n   143\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   144\u2192}\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::onFailure(Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus status, const std::string&,\n   147\u2192                               Tracing::Span&) {\n   148\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"CheckRequest call failed with status: {}\",\n   149\u2192            Grpc::Utility::grpcStatusToString(status));\n   150\u2192  ASSERT(status != Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Ok);\n   151\u2192  Response response{};\n   152\u2192  response.status = CheckStatus::Error;\n   153\u2192  response.status_code = Http::Code::Forbidden;\n   154\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(response));\n   155\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   156\u2192}\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   159\u2192} // namespace Common\n   160\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   161\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   162\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   163\u2192"

Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\n     4\u2192#include <cstdint>\n     5\u2192#include <memory>\n     6\u2192#include <string>\n     7\u2192#include <vector>\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/grpc/async_client.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/grpc/async_client_manager.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"envoy/http/filter.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"envoy/http/protocol.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"envoy/network/address.h\"\n    17\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n    18\u2192#include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n    19\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n    20\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n    21\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192#include \"source/common/grpc/typed_async_client.h\"\n    24\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h\"\n    25\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    28\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    29\u2192namespace Filters {\n    30\u2192namespace Common {\n    31\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192using ExtAuthzAsyncCallbacks = Grpc::AsyncRequestCallbacks<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>;\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192/*\n    36\u2192 * This client implementation is used when the Ext_Authz filter needs to communicate with an gRPC\n    37\u2192 * authorization server. Unlike the HTTP client, the gRPC allows the server to define response\n    38\u2192 * objects which contain the HTTP attributes to be sent to the upstream or to the downstream client.\n    39\u2192 * The gRPC client does not rewrite path. NOTE: We create gRPC client for each filter stack instead\n    40\u2192 * of a client per thread. That is ok since this is unary RPC and the cost of doing this is minimal.\n    41\u2192 */\n    42\u2192class GrpcClientImpl : public Client,\n    43\u2192                       public ExtAuthzAsyncCallbacks,\n    44\u2192                       public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::ext_authz> {\n    45\u2192public:\n    46\u2192  GrpcClientImpl(const Grpc::RawAsyncClientSharedPtr& async_client,\n    47\u2192                 const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& timeout);\n    48\u2192  ~GrpcClientImpl() override;\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192  // ExtAuthz::Client\n    51\u2192  void cancel() override;\n    52\u2192  void check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks, const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n    53\u2192             Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) override;\n    54\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const override {\n    55\u2192    return request_ ? &request_->streamInfo() : nullptr;\n    56\u2192  }\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192  // Grpc::AsyncRequestCallbacks\n    59\u2192  void onCreateInitialMetadata(Http::RequestHeaderMap&) override {}\n    60\u2192  void onSuccess(std::unique_ptr<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>&& response,\n    61\u2192                 Tracing::Span& span) override;\n    62\u2192  void onFailure(Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus status, const std::string& message,\n    63\u2192                 Tracing::Span& span) override;\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192private:\n    66\u2192  Grpc::AsyncClient<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest, envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>\n    67\u2192      async_client_;\n    68\u2192  Grpc::AsyncRequest* request_{};\n    69\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> timeout_;\n    70\u2192  RequestCallbacks* callbacks_{};\n    71\u2192  const Protobuf::MethodDescriptor& service_method_;\n    72\u2192};\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192using GrpcClientImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<GrpcClientImpl>;\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n    77\u2192} // namespace Common\n    78\u2192} // namespace Filters\n    79\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n    80\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n    81\u2192"

55. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore ext_authz debugging/observability",
  "max_turns": 25,
  "prompt": "Thoroughly explore the Envoy ext_authz filter to understand all debugging and observability features. Look for:\n\n1. **Statistics/Metrics**: Search for stat names in the ext_authz code. What counters, gauges, and histograms are exported? Look in:\n   - source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h (stats struct)\n   - source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h (stats struct)\n   - Check what stat prefix is used\n\n2. **Logging**: Search for ENVOY_LOG, ENVOY_STREAM_LOG, or similar logging macros in ext_authz files. What log levels are used? What information is logged?\n\n3. **Tracing**: Look for tracing-related code. What trace spans or headers are created/propagated? Check TracingConstants in the common ext_authz code.\n\n4. **Dynamic Metadata**: How can authorization responses inject metadata that's visible in access logs?\n\n5. **Response Code Details**: What response code detail strings does ext_authz use? These appear in access logs.\n\n6. **Access Log Integration**: How does ext_authz interact with Envoy's access logging? Look for ExtAuthzLoggingInfo or similar.\n\nProvide specific stat names, log messages, tracing constants, and metadata keys.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "ac1b923",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report.\n\n## Thorough Exploration of Envoy ext_authz Filter Debugging and Observability Features\n\nBased on my comprehensive exploration of the Envoy ext_authz filter codebase, here are all the debugging and observability features:\n\n---\n\n### 1. STATISTICS/METRICS\n\n#### HTTP Filter Stats (ext_authz/http)\nThe HTTP ext_authz filter exports the following counters via the `ExtAuthzFilterStats` struct:\n\n**Counter Stats:**\n- `ok` - Number of successful authorization checks\n- `denied` - Number of denied authorization requests\n- `error` - Number of authorization service errors\n- `disabled` - Number of requests where the filter was disabled\n- `failure_mode_allowed` - Number of errors that were allowed due to failure_mode_allow configuration\n- `invalid` - Number of invalid/malformed authorization responses\n- `ignored_dynamic_metadata` - Number of times dynamic metadata injection was ignored (when dynamic metadata ingestion is disabled)\n- `filter_state_name_collision` - Number of times there was a collision when storing logging info in filter state\n\n**Stat Prefix:**\n- Configured via `stat_prefix` config parameter\n- Format: `ext_authz.<stat_prefix>.<stat_name>` (e.g., `ext_authz.waf.ok` or `ext_authz.ok`)\n- Also generates cluster-scoped stats: `ext_authz_ok_`, `ext_authz_denied_`, `ext_authz_error_`, `ext_authz_invalid_`, `ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_`\n\n#### Network Filter Stats (ext_authz/network)\nThe network ext_authz filter exports both counters and gauges:\n\n**Counter Stats:**\n- `cx_closed` - Number of connections closed due to authorization failure\n- `denied` - Number of denied connections\n- `error` - Number of authorization service errors\n- `failure_mode_allowed` - Number of errors allowed due to failure_mode_allow\n- `ok` - Number of successful checks\n- `total` - Total number of authorization checks\n- `disabled` - Number of connections where filter was disabled\n\n**Gauge Stats:**\n- `active` - Current number of in-flight authorization checks (Accumulate mode)\n\n**Stat Prefix:**\n- Format: `ext_authz.<stat_prefix>.<stat_name>`\n\n---\n\n### 2. LOGGING\n\n#### ENVOY_LOG Statements (Global/No specific request context)\n\n**In ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc:**\n- `ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"Sending CheckRequest: {}\", request.DebugString())` - Logs the full CheckRequest being sent to gRPC service\n- `ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"Received CheckResponse: {}\", response->DebugString())` - Logs the CheckResponse received from gRPC service\n- `ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"CheckRequest call failed with status: {}\", Grpc::Utility::grpcStatusToString(status))` - Logs gRPC call failures\n\n**In ext_authz_http_impl.cc:**\n- `ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\", cluster)` - Logs when configured cluster doesn't exist\n\n#### ENVOY_STREAM_LOG Statements (Per-request context in HTTP filter)\n\n**In ext_authz.cc (HTTP Filter):**\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Could not find logging info at {}! (Did another filter already put data at this name?)\", *decoder_callbacks_, decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())` - Logs filter state name collision\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\", *decoder_callbacks_)` - Logs when initiating authz call\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter is disabled. Deny the request.\", *decoder_callbacks_)` - Logs when filter is disabled and deny_at_disable is true\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\", *decoder_callbacks_)` - Logs when request body buffering starts\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {}\", *decoder_callbacks_, buffer_is_full ? \"buffer is full\" : \"stream is ended\")` - Logs when buffering completes\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\", *decoder_callbacks_)` - Logs authz call initiation\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Response is trying to inject dynamic metadata, but dynamic metadata ingestion is disabled. Ignoring...\", *decoder_callbacks_)` - Logs when dynamic metadata is ignored\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_)` - Logs header additions\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value)` - Logs individual headers being set/added/appended/removed\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter rejected the request. Response status code: '{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(response->status_code))` - Logs authorization denial\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\", *decoder_callbacks_)` - Logs error that was allowed by failure_mode_allow\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error. Response status code: {}\", *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(config_->statusOnError()))` - Logs error that was rejected\n\n- `ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter invalidated the response. Response status code: {}\", *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(status))` - Logs invalid response rejection\n\n- Multiple trace logs for query parameter mutations, header validation, and response header processing\n\n**Log Levels:**\n- `trace` - Most detailed logging (header-by-header details, CheckRequest/CheckResponse bodies)\n- `debug` - Medium verbosity (buffering state, key operational transitions)\n- No `warn` or `error` level logging in ext_authz filter itself\n\n---\n\n### 3. TRACING\n\n#### Tracing Constants\nDefined in `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h`:\n\n**TracingConstantValues struct:**\n```cpp\nstruct TracingConstantValues {\n  const std::string TraceStatus = \"ext_authz_status\";\n  const std::string TraceUnauthz = \"ext_authz_unauthorized\";\n  const std::string TraceOk = \"ext_authz_ok\";\n  const std::string HttpStatus = \"ext_authz_http_status\";\n};\n```\n\n#### Trace Spans and Tags\n\n**In gRPC Implementation (ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc):**\n- When response status is OK: `span.setTag(\"ext_authz_status\", \"ext_authz_ok\")`\n- When response status is not OK (denied/error): `span.setTag(\"ext_authz_status\", \"ext_authz_unauthorized\")`\n\n**In HTTP Implementation (ext_authz_http_impl.cc):**\n- `span.setTag(\"ext_authz_http_status\", Http::CodeUtility::toString(status_code))` - HTTP status code as string\n- `span.setTag(\"ext_authz_status\", \"ext_authz_ok\")` - When HTTP 200 OK\n- `span.setTag(\"ext_authz_status\", \"ext_authz_unauthorized\")` - For any non-200 status\n\n**Tracing Name:**\n- HTTP: Configured as `\"async <cluster_name> egress\"` (e.g., \"async ext_authz_cluster egress\")\n- Network: Uses NullSpan (no tracing in network filter)\n\n**Parent Span Propagation:**\n- Both HTTP and Network filters receive parent span from stream context via `decoder_callbacks_->activeSpan()` or `Tracing::NullSpan::instance()`\n- Child spans are created with the authorization service call as child of the main request span\n\n---\n\n### 4. DYNAMIC METADATA\n\n#### Metadata Injection from Authorization Responses\n\n**Source Fields:**\n- `response->dynamic_metadata` - Struct of key-value pairs returned by the authorization service\n- For gRPC: Can come from either `CheckResponse.dynamic_metadata` or (deprecated) `OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata`\n- For HTTP: Extracted from response headers via `dynamic_metadata_from_headers` configuration\n\n#### Injected Metadata Key\n- Namespace: `\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\"` (HTTP filter)\n- Namespace: `NetworkFilterNames::get().ExtAuthorization` which is `\"envoy.filters.network.ext_authz\"` (Network filter)\n\n#### Special Metadata Fields Added by Filter\n\n**Duration Field:**\n- Key: `\"ext_authz_duration\"`\n- Value: Duration in milliseconds (as numeric value) of the authorization check\n- Added automatically when `start_time_` is available and status is OK\n- Injected for both HTTP and Network filters\n\n#### Configuration Control\n- `enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion` (default: true) - Can disable dynamic metadata injection entirely\n- When disabled, the stat `ignored_dynamic_metadata_` is incremented\n\n#### Access via Filter State\nThe HTTP filter stores metadata in filter state via `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` object in filter state with key = filter config name\n\n---\n\n### 5. RESPONSE CODE DETAILS\n\nResponse code detail strings set in `response_code_details` field of stream info:\n\nDefined in `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h`:\n\n```cpp\nstruct ResponseCodeDetailsValues {\n  const std::string AuthzDenied = \"ext_authz_denied\";      // ext_authz filter denied the request\n  const std::string AuthzError = \"ext_authz_error\";        // ext_authz failure, fail-closed\n  const std::string AuthzInvalid = \"ext_authz_invalid\";    // ext_authz invalidated CheckResponse\n};\n```\n\n**Usage:**\n- Set via `decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(detail_string)`\n- Also sets response flag: `StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService` for all denials/errors\n- For Network filter, also closes connection with reason: `\"ext_authz_close\"`\n\n---\n\n### 6. ACCESS LOG INTEGRATION\n\n#### ExtAuthzLoggingInfo Filter State Object\n\n**Location:** `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h`\n\n**Stored in Filter State:**\n- Key: `decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()` - The filter's unique config name\n- Type: `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` - Custom filter state object\n- Lifecycle: Request-scoped (LifeSpan::Request)\n- Mutability: Mutable (StateType::Mutable)\n\n**Data Fields in ExtAuthzLoggingInfo:**\n1. `filter_metadata_` (Optional Struct) - The configured filter metadata from config\n2. `latency_` (Optional duration in microseconds) - Authorization check latency\n3. `bytes_sent_` (Optional uint64_t) - Bytes sent to authz service (gRPC only)\n4. `bytes_received_` (Optional uint64_t) - Bytes received from authz service (gRPC only)\n5. `cluster_info_` (Upstream cluster info) - Info about the upstream authz service cluster\n6. `upstream_host_` (Upstream host info) - Info about the specific upstream host used\n\n**Populated When:**\n- `emitFilterStateStats()` config option is true, OR\n- `filterMetadata()` config option is set\n- Collision detection: If another filter already has data at this name, `filter_state_name_collision_` stat is incremented\n\n**Accessor Methods:**\n- `filterMetadata()` - Get configured filter metadata\n- `latency()` - Get authorization check latency\n- `bytesSent()` - Get bytes sent (gRPC only)\n- `bytesReceived()` - Get bytes received (gRPC only)\n- `clusterInfo()` - Get upstream cluster info\n- `upstreamHost()` - Get upstream host info\n\n**Access in Access Logs:**\nAccess logs can read this via filter state if the logging filter traverses filter state or via custom access log formatters that read filter state.\n\n#### Dynamic Metadata for Access Logs\n- The authorization response metadata is stored in stream info's dynamic metadata under `\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\"` namespace\n- Includes `ext_authz_duration` field with millisecond precision\n- Can be accessed in access logs via dynamic metadata formatters\n\n#### Integration Points\n- HTTP filter sets metadata before continuing filter chain\n- Metadata is available to all downstream filters and access loggers\n- Network filter sets metadata under `NetworkFilterNames::get().ExtAuthorization` (`\"envoy.filters.network.ext_authz\"`)\n\n---\n\n### 7. KEY CONFIGURATION OPTIONS FOR OBSERVABILITY\n\n**For Statistics:**\n- `stat_prefix` - Custom prefix for stats (e.g., \"waf\")\n- `charge_cluster_response_stats` - Whether to charge response stats to upstream cluster (default: true)\n\n**For Logging:**\n- Logger ID: `Logger::Id::ext_authz` - Can filter logs by this ID\n- Set log level to \"trace\" or \"debug\" for ext_authz observability\n\n**For Tracing:**\n- Automatic child span creation with configured tracer\n- Trace tags populated in span for status information\n- Parent span propagation from request\n\n**For Metadata/Access Logs:**\n- `emit_filter_state_stats` - Enable populating ExtAuthzLoggingInfo in filter state\n- `filter_metadata` - Static metadata to include in logging info\n- `enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion` - Allow authz service to inject dynamic metadata\n\n---\n\n### Summary Table of Observable Signals\n\n| Signal Type | Name | Values/Examples | Scope |\n|------------|------|--------|-------|\n| Counter | `ext_authz.ok` | 0, 1, 2, ... | Filter-level |\n| Counter | `ext_authz.denied` | 0, 1, 2, ... | Filter-level |\n| Counter | `ext_authz.error` | 0, 1, 2, ... | Filter-level |\n| Counter | `ext_authz.disabled` | 0, 1, 2, ... | Filter-level |\n| Counter | `ext_authz.failure_mode_allowed` | 0, 1, 2, ... | Filter-level |\n| Counter | `ext_authz.invalid` | 0, 1, 2, ... | Filter-level |\n| Gauge | `ext_authz.active` (network only) | 0, 1, 2, ... | Connection-level |\n| Log | ENVOY_STREAM_LOG (trace) | Header details, mutation details | Request-level |\n| Log | ENVOY_LOG (trace/debug) | gRPC debug strings, cluster errors | Global |\n| Trace Tag | `ext_authz_status` | \"ext_authz_ok\" or \"ext_authz_unauthorized\" | Span-level |\n| Trace Tag | `ext_authz_http_status` | \"200\", \"403\", etc. (HTTP only) | Span-level |\n| Dynamic Metadata | `ext_authz_duration` | milliseconds as number | Request-level |\n| Response Code Detail | Status | \"ext_authz_denied\", \"ext_authz_error\", \"ext_authz_invalid\" | Request-level |\n| Filter State | ExtAuthzLoggingInfo | Latency, bytes, cluster info | Request-level |",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Thoroughly explore the Envoy ext_authz filter to understand all debugging and observability features. Look for:\n\n1. **Statistics/Metrics**: Search for stat names in the ext_authz code. What counters, gauges, and histograms are exported? Look in:\n   - source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h (stats struct)\n   - source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h (stats struct)\n   - Check what stat prefix is used\n\n2. **Logging**: Search for ENVOY_LOG, ENVOY_STREAM_LOG, or similar logging macros in ext_authz files. What log levels are used? What information is logged?\n\n3. **Tracing**: Look for tracing-related code. What trace spans or headers are created/propagated? Check TracingConstants in the common ext_authz code.\n\n4. **Dynamic Metadata**: How can authorization responses inject metadata that's visible in access logs?\n\n5. **Response Code Details**: What response code detail strings does ext_authz use? These appear in access logs.\n\n6. **Access Log Integration**: How does ext_authz interact with Envoy's access logging? Look for ExtAuthzLoggingInfo or similar.\n\nProvide specific stat names, log messages, tracing constants, and metadata keys.",

response headers, except Host, should be added to\n   159\u2192  // the client response.\n   160\u2192  if (matchers.empty()) {\n   161\u2192    envoy::type::matcher::v3::StringMatcher matcher;\n 181\u2192            matcher, context));\n   182\u2192  }\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192  return std::make_shared<HeaderKeyMatcher>(std::move(matchers));\n   185\u2192}\n   186\u2192\n   193\u2192RawHttpClientImpl::RawHttpClientImpl(Upstream::ClusterManager& cm, ClientConfigSharedPtr config)\n   194\u2192    : cm_(cm), config_(config) {}\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192RawHttpClientImpl::~RawHttpClientImpl() { ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr); }\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::cancel() {\n   199\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ != nullptr);\n   200\u2192  request_->cancel();\n   201\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   202\u2192}\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192// Client\n   205\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks,\n   206\u2192                              const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   207\u2192                              Tracing::Span& parent_span,\n   208\u2192                              const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) {\n   209\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr);\n   210\u2192  callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192  Http::RequestHeaderMapPtr headers;\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192  const auto& http_request = request.attributes().request().http();\n   215\u2192  const auto& http_request_body =\n   216\u2192      http_request.body().empty() ? http_request.raw_body() : http_request.body();\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192  uint64_t request_length = http_request_body.size();\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192  if (request_length > 0) {\n   221\u2192    headers = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(\n   222\u2192        {{Http::Headers::get().ContentLength, std::to_string(request_length)}});\n   223\u2192  } else {\n   224\u2192    headers = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::RequestHeaderMapImpl>(lengthZeroHeader());\n   225\u2192  }\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192  if (config_->encodeRawHeaders()) {\n   228\u2192    for (const auto& header : http_request.header_map().headers()) {\n   229\u2192      const Http::LowerCaseString key{header.key()};\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192      // Skip setting content-length header since it is already configured at initialization.\n   232\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().ContentLength) {\n   233\u2192        continue;\n   234\u2192      }\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().Path && !config_->pathPrefix().empty()) {\n   237\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, absl::StrCat(config_->pathPrefix(), header.raw_value()));\n   238\u2192      } else {\n   239\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, header.raw_value());\n   240\u2192      }\n   241\u2192    }\n   242\u2192  } else {\n   243\u2192    for (const auto& header : http_request.headers()) {\n   244\u2192      const Http::LowerCaseString key{header.first};\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192      // Skip setting content-length header since it is already configured at initialization.\n   247\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().ContentLength) {\n   248\u2192        continue;\n   249\u2192      }\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192      if (key == Http::Headers::get().Path && !config_->pathPrefix().empty()) {\n   252\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, absl::StrCat(config_->pathPrefix(), header.second));\n   253\u2192      } else {\n   254\u2192        headers->addCopy(key, header.second);\n   255\u2192      }\n   256\u2192    }\n   257\u2192  }\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192  config_->requestHeaderParser().evaluateHeaders(*headers, stream_info);\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192  Http::RequestMessagePtr message =\n   262\u2192      std::make_unique<Envoy::Http::RequestMessageImpl>(std::move(headers));\n   263\u2192  if (request_length > 0) {\n   264\u2192    message->body().add(http_request_body);\n   265\u2192  }\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192  const std::string& cluster = config_->cluster();\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192  // It's possible that the cluster specified in the filter configuration no longer exists due to a\n   270\u2192  // CDS removal.\n   271\u2192  const auto thread_local_cluster = cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(cluster);\n   272\u2192  if (thread_local_cluster == nullptr) {\n   273\u2192    // TODO(dio): Add stats related to this.\n   274\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\", cluster);\n   275\u2192    callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse()));\n   276\u2192    callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   277\u2192  } else {\n   278\u2192    // Do not enforce a sampling decision on this span; instead keep the parent's sampling status.\n   279\u2192    auto options = Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions()\n   280\u2192                       .setTimeout(config_->timeout())\n   281\u2192                       .setParentSpan(parent_span)\n   282\u2192                       .setChildSpanName(config_->tracingName())\n   283\u2192                       .setSampled(absl::nullopt);\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192    options.setSendXff(false);\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192    request_ = thread_local_cluster->httpAsyncClient().send(std::move(message), *this, options);\n   288\u2192  }\n   289\u2192}\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onSuccess(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&,\n   292\u2192                                  Http::ResponseMessagePtr&& message) {\n   293\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(toResponse(std::move(message)));\n   294\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   295\u2192}\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onFailure(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&,\n   298\u2192                                  Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason reason) {\n   299\u2192  // TODO(botengyao): handle different failure reasons.\n   300\u2192  ASSERT(reason == Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::Reset ||\n   301\u2192         reason == Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::ExceedResponseBufferLimit);\n   302\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse()));\n   303\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   304\u2192}\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onBeforeFinalizeUpstreamSpan(\n   307\u2192    Tracing::Span& span, const Http::ResponseHeaderMap* response_headers) {\n   308\u2192  if (response_headers != nullptr) {\n   309\u2192    const uint64_t status_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*response_headers);\n   310\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().HttpStatus,\n   311\u2192                Http::CodeUtility::toString(static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code)));\n   312\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)\n   313\u2192                                                         ? TracingConstants::get().TraceOk\n   314\u2192                                                         : TracingConstants::get().TraceUnauthz);\n   315\u2192  }\n   316\u2192}\n   317\u2192\n   318\u2192ResponsePtr RawHttpClientImpl::toResponse(Http::ResponseMessagePtr message) {\n   319\u2192  const uint64_t status_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(message->headers());\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192  // Set an error status if the call to the authorization server returns any of the 5xx HTTP error\n   322\u2192  // codes. A Forbidden response is sent to the client if the filter has not been configured with\n   323\u2192  // failure_mode_allow.\n   324\u2192  if (Http::CodeUtility::is5xx(status_code)) {\n   325\u2192    return std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse());\n   326\u2192  }\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  // Extract headers-to-remove from the storage header coming from the\n   329\u2192  // authorization server.\n   330\u2192  const auto& storage_header_name = Headers::get().EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove;\n   331\u2192  // If we are going to construct an Ok response we need to save the\n   332\u2192  // headers_to_remove in a variable first.\n   333\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\n   334\u2192  if (status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)) {\n   335\u2192    const auto& get_result = message->headers().get(storage_header_name);\n   336\u2192    for (size_t i = 0; i < get_result.size(); ++i) {\n   337\u2192      const Http::HeaderEntry* entry = get_result[i];\n   338\u2192      if (entry != nullptr) {\n   339\u2192        absl::string_view storage_header_value = entry->value().getStringView();\n   340\u2192        std::vector<absl::string_view> header_names = StringUtil::splitToken(\n   341\u2192            storage_header_value, \",\", /*keep_empty_string=*/false, /*trim_whitespace=*/true);\n   342\u2192        headers_to_remove.reserve(headers_to_remove.size() + header_names.size());\n   343\u2192        for (const auto& header_name : header_names) {\n   344\u2192          headers_to_remove.emplace_back(header_name);\n   345\u2192        }\n   346\u2192      }\n   347\u2192    }\n   348\u2192  }\n   349\u2192  // Now remove the storage header from the authz server response headers before\n   350\u2192  // we reuse them to construct an Ok/Denied authorization response below.\n   351\u2192  message->headers().remove(storage_header_name);\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192  // Create an Ok authorization response.\n   354\u2192  if (status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)) {\n   355\u2192    SuccessResponse ok{message->headers(),\n   356\u2192                       config_->upstreamHeaderMatchers(),\n   357\u2192                       config_->upstreamHeaderToAppendMatchers(),\n   358\u2192                       config_->clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers(),\n   359\u2192                       config_->dynamicMetadataMatchers(),\n   360\u2192                       Response{CheckStatus::OK,\n   361\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   362\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   363\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   364\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   365\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   366\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   367\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   368\u2192                                std::move(headers_to_remove),\n   369\u2192                                Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector{},\n   370\u2192                                {},\n   371\u2192                                EMPTY_STRING,\n   372\u2192                                Http::Code::OK,\n   373\u2192                                ProtobufWkt::Struct{}}};\n   374\u2192    return std::move(ok.response_);\n   375\u2192  }\n   376\u2192\n   377\u2192  // Create a Denied authorization response.\n   378\u2192  SuccessResponse denied{message->headers(),\n   379\u2192                         config_->clientHeaderMatchers(),\n   380\u2192                         config_->upstreamHeaderToAppendMatchers(),\n   381\u2192                         config_->clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers(),\n   382\u2192                         config_->dynamicMetadataMatchers(),\n   383\u2192                         Response{CheckStatus::Denied,\n   384\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   385\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   386\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   387\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   388\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   389\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   390\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   391\u2192                                  {{}},\n   392\u2192                                  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector{},\n   393\u2192                                  {},\n   394\u2192                                  message->bodyAsString(),\n   395\u2192                                  static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code),\n   396\u2192                                  ProtobufWkt::Struct{}}};\n   397\u2192  return std::move(denied.response_);\n   398\u2192}\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   401\u2192} // namespace Common\n   402\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   403\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   404\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   405\u2192"
58. Read @ -

"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\"\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/type/matcher/v3/string.pb.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/common/logger.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/common/matchers.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"source/common/router/header_parser.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    17\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    18\u2192namespace Filters {\n    19\u2192namespace Common {\n    20\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192/**\n    23\u2192 * HTTP client configuration for the HTTP authorization (ext_authz) filter.\n    24\u2192 */\n    25\u2192class ClientConfig {\n    26\u2192public:\n    27\u2192  ClientConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\n    28\u2192               uint32_t timeout, absl::string_view path_prefix,\n    29\u2192               Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192  /**\n    32\u2192   * Returns the name of the authorization cluster.\n    33\u2192   */\n    34\u2192  const std::string& cluster() { return cluster_name_; }\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192  /**\n    37\u2192   * Returns the authorization request path prefix.\n    38\u2192   */\n    39\u2192  const std::string& pathPrefix() { return path_prefix_; }\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192  /**\n    42\u2192   * Returns authorization request timeout.\n    43\u2192   */\n    44\u2192  const std::chrono::milliseconds& timeout() const { return timeout_; }\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192  /**\n    47\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\n    48\u2192   * should be send back to the client.\n    49\u2192   */\n    50\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& clientHeaderMatchers() const { return client_header_matchers_; }\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192  /**\n    53\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\n    54\u2192   * should be send back to the client on a successful (i.e. non-denied) response.\n    55\u2192   */\n    56\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers() const {\n    57\u2192    return client_header_on_success_matchers_;\n    58\u2192  }\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192  /**\n    61\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the headers to emit as dynamic metadata.\n    62\u2192   */\n    63\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& dynamicMetadataMatchers() const { return to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_; }\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192  /**\n    66\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\n    67\u2192   * should be send to an the upstream server.\n    68\u2192   */\n    69\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& upstreamHeaderMatchers() const { return upstream_header_matchers_; }\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192  /**\n    72\u2192   * Returns a list of matchers used for selecting the authorization response headers that\n    73\u2192   * should be sent to the upstream server. The same header keys will be appended, instead of\n    74\u2192   * be replaced.\n    75\u2192   */\n    76\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& upstreamHeaderToAppendMatchers() const {\n    77\u2192    return upstream_header_to_append_matchers_;\n    78\u2192  }\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  /**\n    81\u2192   * Returns the name used for tracing.\n    82\u2192   */\n    83\u2192  const std::string& tracingName() { return tracing_name_; }\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  /**\n    86\u2192   * Returns the configured request header parser.\n    87\u2192   */\n    88\u2192  const Router::HeaderParser& requestHeaderParser() const { return *request_headers_parser_; }\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  /**\n    91\u2192   * Returns whether or not to encode raw headers (i.e. use headers_map instead of headers field).\n    92\u2192   */\n    93\u2192  bool encodeRawHeaders() const { return encode_raw_headers_; }\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192private:\n    96\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr toClientMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n    97\u2192                                           Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n    98\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr\n    99\u2192  toClientMatchersOnSuccess(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   100\u2192                            Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n   101\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr\n   102\u2192  toDynamicMetadataMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   103\u2192                            Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n   104\u2192  static MatcherSharedPtr\n   105\u2192  toUpstreamMatchers(const envoy::type::matcher::v3::ListStringMatcher& list,\n   106\u2192                     Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context);\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr request_header_matchers_;\n   109\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr client_header_matchers_;\n   110\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr client_header_on_success_matchers_;\n   111\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_;\n   112\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr upstream_header_matchers_;\n   113\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr upstream_header_to_append_matchers_;\n   114\u2192  const std::string cluster_name_;\n   115\u2192  const std::chrono::milliseconds timeout_;\n   116\u2192  const std::string path_prefix_;\n   117\u2192  const std::string tracing_name_;\n   118\u2192  Router::HeaderParserPtr request_headers_parser_;\n   119\u2192  const bool encode_raw_headers_;\n   120\u2192};\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192using ClientConfigSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<ClientConfig>;\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192/**\n   125\u2192 * This client implementation is used when the Ext_Authz filter needs to communicate with an\n   126\u2192 * HTTP authorization server. Unlike the gRPC client that allows the server to define the\n   127\u2192 * response object, in the HTTP client, all headers and body provided in the response are\n   128\u2192 * dispatched to the downstream, and some headers to the upstream. The HTTP client also allows\n   129\u2192 * setting a path prefix witch is not available for gRPC.\n   130\u2192 */\n   131\u2192class RawHttpClientImpl : public Client,\n   132\u2192                          public Http::AsyncClient::Callbacks,\n   133\u2192                          Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n   134\u2192public:\n   135\u2192  explicit RawHttpClientImpl(Upstream::ClusterManager& cm, ClientConfigSharedPtr config);\n   136\u2192  ~RawHttpClientImpl() override;\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192  // ExtAuthz::Client\n   139\u2192  void cancel() override;\n   140\u2192  void check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks, const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   141\u2192             Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) override;\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192  // Http::AsyncClient::Callbacks\n   144\u2192  void onSuccess(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&, Http::ResponseMessagePtr&& message) override;\n   145\u2192  void onFailure(const Http::AsyncClient::Request&,\n   146\u2192                 Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason reason) override;\n   147\u2192  void onBeforeFinalizeUpstreamSpan(Tracing::Span& span,\n   148\u2192                                    const Http::ResponseHeaderMap* response_headers) override;\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192private:\n   151\u2192  ResponsePtr toResponse(Http::ResponseMessagePtr message);\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192  Upstream::ClusterManager& cm_;\n   154\u2192  ClientConfigSharedPtr config_;\n   155\u2192  Http::AsyncClient::Request* request_{};\n   156\u2192  RequestCallbacks* callbacks_{};\n   157\u2192};\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   160\u2192} // namespace Common\n   161\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   162\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   163\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   164\u2192"

"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"envoy/network/address.h\"\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192#include \"source/common/buffer/buffer_impl.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192#include \"test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz.pb.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"test/mocks/http/mocks.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"test/mocks/network/mocks.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"test/mocks/server/server_factory_context.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"test/mocks/stats/mocks.h\"\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192#include \"gmock/gmock.h\"\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    18\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    19\u2192namespace HttpFilters {\n    20\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192class ReusableFilterFactory {\n    23\u2192public:\n    24\u2192  ReusableFilterFactory();\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192  // Update metadata_ and create a filter using the config and client.\n    27\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Filter> newFilter(FilterConfigSharedPtr config,\n    28\u2192                                    Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr&& client,\n    29\u2192                                    const envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata& metadata);\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192private:\n    32\u2192  // Do not use ON_CALL outside of constructor on these mocks. Each ON_CALL has a memory cost and\n    33\u2192  // will cause OOMs if the fuzzer runs long enough.\n    34\u2192  NiceMock<Http::MockStreamDecoderFilterCallbacks> decoder_callbacks_;\n    35\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Buffer::OwnedImpl> decoding_buffer_;\n    36\u2192  NiceMock<Http::MockStreamEncoderFilterCallbacks> encoder_callbacks_;\n    37\u2192  Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr addr_;\n    38\u2192  NiceMock<Envoy::Network::MockConnection> connection_;\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192  // Returned by decoder_callbacks.stream_info_.dynamicMetadata(). Updated by calling newFilter.\n    41\u2192  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata metadata_;\n    42\u2192};\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192class ReusableFuzzerUtil {\n    45\u2192public:\n    46\u2192  // Validate input, then create a filter using the input.config() & the provided client.\n    47\u2192  absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<Filter>>\n    48\u2192  setup(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::ExtAuthzTestCaseBase& input,\n    49\u2192        Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr client);\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192private:\n    52\u2192  NiceMock<Stats::MockIsolatedStatsStore> stats_store_;\n    53\u2192  NiceMock<Server::Configuration::MockServerFactoryContext> factory_context_;\n    54\u2192  ReusableFilterFactory filter_factory_;\n    55\u2192};\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n    58\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\n    59\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n    60\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n    61\u2192"
