\"envoy/common/pure.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/http/codes.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/stream_info/stream_info.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192#include \"source/common/http/headers.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"source/common/singleton/const_singleton.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    20\u2192namespace Filters {\n    21\u2192namespace Common {\n    22\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192/**\n    25\u2192 * Constant values used for tracing metadata.\n    26\u2192 */\n    27\u2192struct TracingConstantValues {\n    28\u2192  const std::string TraceStatus = \"ext_authz_status\";\n    29\u2192  const std::string TraceUnauthz = \"ext_authz_unauthorized\";\n    30\u2192  const std::string TraceOk = \"ext_authz_ok\";\n    31\u2192  const std::string HttpStatus = \"ext_authz_http_status\";\n    32\u2192};\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192using TracingConstants = ConstSingleton<TracingConstantValues>;\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192/**\n    37\u2192 * Possible constant response code details values for a check call.\n    38\u2192 */\n    39\u2192struct ResponseCodeDetailsValues {\n    40\u2192  // The ext_authz filter denied the downstream request/connection.\n    41\u2192  const std::string AuthzDenied = \"ext_authz_denied\";\n    42\u2192  // The ext_authz filter encountered a failure, and was configured to fail-closed.\n    43\u2192  const std::string AuthzError = \"ext_authz_error\";\n    44\u2192  // The ext_authz filter invalidated the CheckResponse and dropped the client request.\n    45\u2192  const std::string AuthzInvalid = \"ext_authz_invalid\";\n    46\u2192};\n    47\u2192using ResponseCodeDetails = ConstSingleton<ResponseCodeDetailsValues>;\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192/**\n    50\u2192 * Constant auth related HTTP headers. All lower case. This group of headers can\n    51\u2192 * contain prefix override headers.\n    52\u2192 */\n    53\u2192class HeaderValues {\n    54\u2192public:\n    55\u2192  const char* prefix() const { return ThreadSafeSingleton<Http::PrefixValue>::get().prefix(); }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthPartialBody{absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-partial-body\")};\n    58\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove{\n    59\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-headers-to-remove\")};\n    60\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed{\n    61\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-failure-mode-allowed\")};\n    62\u2192};\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192using Headers = ConstSingleton<HeaderValues>;\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192/**\n    67\u2192 * Possible async results for a check call.\n    68\u2192 */\n    69\u2192enum class CheckStatus {\n    70\u2192  // The request is authorized.\n    71\u2192  OK,\n    72\u2192  // The authz service could not be queried.\n    73\u2192  Error,\n    74\u2192  // The request is denied.\n    75\u2192  Denied\n    76\u2192};\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192using UnsafeHeader = std::pair<std::string, std::string>;\n    79\u2192using UnsafeHeaderVector = std::vector<UnsafeHeader>;\n    80\u2192/**\n    81\u2192 * Authorization response object for a RequestCallback.\n    82\u2192 *\n    83\u2192 * Header mutations and query parameter mutations are not expected to already be validated by the\n    84\u2192 * client before packing them into this struct (hence they are \"unsafe\").\n    85\u2192 */\n    86\u2192struct Response {\n    87\u2192  // Call status.\n    88\u2192  CheckStatus status;\n    89\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, that will be optionally appended\n    90\u2192  // to the request to the upstream server.\n    91\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_append;\n    92\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set\n    93\u2192  // (using \"setCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n    94\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_set;\n    95\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n    96\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n    97\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_add;\n    98\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n    99\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n   100\u2192  // responses.\n   101\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add;\n   102\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n   103\u2192  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses.\n   104\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_set;\n   105\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n   106\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n   107\u2192  // responses only if the headers were not returned from the authz server.\n   108\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent;\n   109\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n   110\u2192  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses\n   111\u2192  // only if the headers were returned from the authz server.\n   112\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists;\n   113\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers consumed by the authorization server, will be removed\n   114\u2192  // from the request to the upstream server.\n   115\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\n   116\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to be set (possibly overwritten) on the\n   117\u2192  // request to the upstream server.\n   118\u2192  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector query_parameters_to_set;\n   119\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to remove from the request to the upstream server.\n   120\u2192  std::vector<std::string> query_parameters_to_remove;\n   121\u2192  // Optional http body used only on denied response.\n   122\u2192  std::string body;\n   123\u2192  // Optional http status used only on denied response.\n   124\u2192  Http::Code status_code{};\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192  // A set of metadata returned by the authorization server, that will be emitted as filter's\n   127\u2192  // dynamic metadata that other filters can leverage.\n   128\u2192  ProtobufWkt::Struct dynamic_metadata;\n   129\u2192};\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192using ResponsePtr = std::unique_ptr<Response>;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192/**\n   134\u2192 * Async callbacks used during check() calls.\n   135\u2192 */\n   136\u2192class RequestCallbacks {\n   137\u2192public:\n   138\u2192  virtual ~RequestCallbacks() = default;\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  /**\n   141\u2192   * Called when a check request is complete. The resulting ResponsePtr is supplied.\n   142\u2192   */\n   143\u2192  virtual void onComplete(ResponsePtr&& response) PURE;\n   144\u2192};\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192class Client {\n   147\u2192public:\n   148\u2192  // Destructor\n   149\u2192  virtual ~Client() = default;\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  /**\n   152\u2192   * Cancel an inflight Check request.\n   153\u2192   */\n   154\u2192  virtual void cancel() PURE;\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  /**\n   157\u2192   * Request a check call to an external authorization service which can use the\n   158\u2192   * passed request parameters to make a permit/deny decision.\n   159\u2192   * @param callback supplies the completion callbacks.\n   160\u2192   *        NOTE: The callback may happen within the calling stack.\n   161\u2192   * @param request is the proto message with the attributes of the specific payload.\n   162\u2192   * @param parent_span source for generating an egress child span as part of the trace.\n   163\u2192   * @param stream_info supplies the client's stream info.\n   164\u2192   */\n   165\u2192  virtual void check(RequestCallbacks& callback,\n   166\u2192                     const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   167\u2192                     Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) PURE;\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  /**\n   170\u2192   * Returns streamInfo of the current request if possible. By default just return a nullptr.\n   171\u2192   */\n   172\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const { return nullptr; }\n   173\u2192};\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192using ClientPtr = std::unique_ptr<Client>;\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   178\u2192} // namespace Common\n   179\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   180\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   181\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   182\u2192"

HttpFilterTestBase : public T {\n    59\u2192public:\n    60\u2192  HttpFilterTestBase() {}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192  void initialize(std::string&& yaml) {\n    63\u2192 \"source/common/protobuf/protobuf.h\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    14\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    15\u2192namespace Filters {\n    16\u2192namespace Common {\n    17\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192void copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(\n    20\u2192    ResponsePtr& response,\n    21\u2192    const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::core::v3::HeaderValueOption>& headers) {\n    22\u2192  for (const auto& header : headers) {\n    23\u2192    if (header.append().value()) {\n    24\u2192      response->headers_to_append.emplace_back(header.header().key(), header.header().value());\n    25\u2192    } else {\n    26\u2192      response->headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(), header.header().value());\n    27\u2192    }\n    28\u2192  }\n    29\u2192}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192void copyOkResponseMutations(ResponsePtr& response,\n    32\u2192                             const envoy::service::auth::v3::OkHttpResponse& ok_response) {\n    33\u2192  copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(response, ok_response.headers());\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192  for (const auto& header : ok_response.response_headers_to_add()) {\n    36\u2192    if (header.has_append()) {\n    37\u2192      if (header.append().value()) {\n    38\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    39\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    40\u2192      } else {\n    41\u2192        response->response_headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    42\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    43\u2192      }\n    44\u2192    } else {\n    45\u2192      switch (header.append_action()) {\n    46\u2192        PANIC_ON_PROTO_ENUM_SENTINEL_VALUES;\n    47\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::APPEND_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD:\n    48\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    49\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    50\u2192        break;\n    51\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::ADD_IF_ABSENT:\n    52\u2192        response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    53\u2192                                                                 header.header().value());\n    54\u2192        break;\n    55\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS:\n    56\u2192        response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    57\u2192                                                                       header.header().value());\n    58\u2192        break;\n    59\u2192      case Router::HeaderValueOption::OVERWRITE_IF_EXISTS_OR_ADD:\n    60\u2192        response->response_headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.header().key(),\n    61\u2192                                                       header.header().value());\n    62\u2192        break;\n    63\u2192      }\n    64\u2192    }\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192  response->headers_to_remove = std::vector<std::string>{ok_response.headers_to_remove().begin(),\n    68\u2192                                                         ok_response.headers_to_remove().end()};\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192  for (const auto& query_parameter : ok_response.query_parameters_to_set()) {\n    71\u2192    response->query_parameters_to_set.emplace_back(query_parameter.key(), query_parameter.value());\n    72\u2192  }\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192  response->query_parameters_to_remove =\n    75\u2192      std::vector<std::string>{ok_response.query_parameters_to_remove().begin(),\n    76\u2192                               ok_response.query_parameters_to_remove().end()};\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192GrpcClientImpl::GrpcClientImpl(const Grpc::RawAsyncClientSharedPtr& async_client,\n    80\u2192                               const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>& timeout)\n    81\u2192    : async_client_(async_client), timeout_(timeout),\n    82\u2192      service_method_(*Protobuf::DescriptorPool::generated_pool()->FindMethodByName(\n    83\u2192          \"envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization.Check\")) {}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192GrpcClientImpl::~GrpcClientImpl() { ASSERT(!callbacks_); }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::cancel() {\n    88\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ != nullptr);\n    89\u2192  request_->cancel();\n    90\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n    91\u2192}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::check(RequestCallbacks& callbacks,\n    94\u2192                           const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n    95\u2192                           Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) {\n    96\u2192  ASSERT(callbacks_ == nullptr);\n    97\u2192  callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n    98\u2192  Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions options;\n    99\u2192  options.setTimeout(timeout_);\n   100\u2192  options.setParentContext(Http::AsyncClient::ParentContext{&stream_info});\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"Sending CheckRequest: {}\", request.DebugString());\n   103\u2192  request_ = async_client_->send(service_method_, request, *this, parent_span, options);\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::onSuccess(std::unique_ptr<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>&& response,\n   107\u2192                               Tracing::Span& span) {\n   108\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"Received CheckResponse: {}\", response->DebugString());\n   109\u2192  ResponsePtr authz_response = std::make_unique<Response>(Response{});\n   110\u2192  if (response->status().code() == Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Ok) {\n   111\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, TracingConstants::get().TraceOk);\n   112\u2192    authz_response->status = CheckStatus::OK;\n   113\u2192    if (response->has_ok_response()) {\n   114\u2192      const auto& ok_response = response->ok_response();\n   115\u2192      copyOkResponseMutations(authz_response, ok_response);\n   116\u2192    }\n   117\u2192  } else {\n   118\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, TracingConstants::get().TraceUnauthz);\n   119\u2192    authz_response->status = CheckStatus::Denied;\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    // The default HTTP status code for denied response is 403 Forbidden.\n   122\u2192    authz_response->status_code = Http::Code::Forbidden;\n   123\u2192    if (response->has_denied_response()) {\n   124\u2192      copyHeaderFieldIntoResponse(authz_response, response->denied_response().headers());\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192      const uint32_t status_code = response->denied_response().status().code();\n   127\u2192      if (status_code > 0) {\n   128\u2192        authz_response->status_code = static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code);\n   129\u2192      }\n   130\u2192      authz_response->body = response->denied_response().body();\n   131\u2192    }\n   132\u2192  }\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192  // OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata is deprecated. Until OkHttpResponse.dynamic_metadata is\n   135\u2192  // removed, it overrides dynamic_metadata field of the outer check response.\n   136\u2192  if (response->has_ok_response() && response->ok_response().has_dynamic_metadata()) {\n   137\u2192    authz_response->dynamic_metadata = response->ok_response().dynamic_metadata();\n   138\u2192  } else {\n   139\u2192    authz_response->dynamic_metadata = response->dynamic_metadata();\n   140\u2192  }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::move(authz_response));\n   143\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   144\u2192}\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192void GrpcClientImpl::onFailure(Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus status, const std::string&,\n   147\u2192                               Tracing::Span&) {\n   148\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"CheckRequest call failed with status: {}\",\n   149\u2192            Grpc::Utility::grpcStatusToString(status));\n   150\u2192  ASSERT(status != Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Ok);"

"   300\u2192  ASSERT(reason == Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::Reset ||\n   301\u2192         reason == Http::AsyncClient::FailureReason::ExceedResponseBufferLimit);\n   302\u2192  callbacks_->onComplete(std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse()));\n   303\u2192  callbacks_ = nullptr;\n   304\u2192}\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192void RawHttpClientImpl::onBeforeFinalizeUpstreamSpan(\n   307\u2192    Tracing::Span& span, const Http::ResponseHeaderMap* response_headers) {\n   308\u2192  if (response_headers != nullptr) {\n   309\u2192    const uint64_t status_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*response_headers);\n   310\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().HttpStatus,\n   311\u2192                Http::CodeUtility::toString(static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code)));\n   312\u2192    span.setTag(TracingConstants::get().TraceStatus, status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)\n   313\u2192                                                         ? TracingConstants::get().TraceOk\n   314\u2192                                                         : TracingConstants::get().TraceUnauthz);\n   315\u2192  }\n   316\u2192}\n   317\u2192\n   318\u2192ResponsePtr RawHttpClientImpl::toResponse(Http::ResponseMessagePtr message) {\n   319\u2192  const uint64_t status_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(message->headers());\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192  // Set an error status if the call to the authorization server returns any of the 5xx HTTP error\n   322\u2192  // codes. A Forbidden response is sent to the client if the filter has not been configured with\n   323\u2192  // failure_mode_allow.\n   324\u2192  if (Http::CodeUtility::is5xx(status_code)) {\n   325\u2192    return std::make_unique<Response>(errorResponse());\n   326\u2192  }\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  // Extract headers-to-remove from the storage header coming from the\n   329\u2192  // authorization server.\n   330\u2192  const auto& storage_header_name = Headers::get().EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove;\n   331\u2192  // If we are going to construct an Ok response we need to save the\n   332\u2192  // headers_to_remove in a variable first.\n   333\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\n   334\u2192  if (status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)) {\n   335\u2192    const auto& get_result = message->headers().get(storage_header_name);\n   336\u2192    for (size_t i = 0; i < get_result.size(); ++i) {\n   337\u2192      const Http::HeaderEntry* entry = get_result[i];\n   338\u2192      if (entry != nullptr) {\n   339\u2192        absl::string_view storage_header_value = entry->value().getStringView();\n   340\u2192        std::vector<absl::string_view> header_names = StringUtil::splitToken(\n   341\u2192            storage_header_value, \",\", /*keep_empty_string=*/false, /*trim_whitespace=*/true);\n   342\u2192        headers_to_remove.reserve(headers_to_remove.size() + header_names.size());\n   343\u2192        for (const auto& header_name : header_names) {\n   344\u2192          headers_to_remove.emplace_back(header_name);\n   345\u2192        }\n   346\u2192      }\n   347\u2192    }\n   348\u2192  }\n   349\u2192  // Now remove the storage header from the authz server response headers before\n   350\u2192  // we reuse them to construct an Ok/Denied authorization response below.\n   351\u2192  message->headers().remove(storage_header_name);\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192  // Create an Ok authorization response.\n   354\u2192  if (status_code == enumToInt(Http::Code::OK)) {\n   355\u2192    SuccessResponse ok{message->headers(),\n   356\u2192                       config_->upstreamHeaderMatchers(),\n   357\u2192                       config_->upstreamHeaderToAppendMatchers(),\n   358\u2192                       config_->clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers(),\n   359\u2192                       config_->dynamicMetadataMatchers(),\n   360\u2192                       Response{CheckStatus::OK,\n   361\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   362\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   363\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   364\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   365\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   366\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   367\u2192                                UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   368\u2192                                std::move(headers_to_remove),\n   369\u2192                                Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector{},\n   370\u2192                                {},\n   371\u2192                                EMPTY_STRING,\n   372\u2192                                Http::Code::OK,\n   373\u2192                                ProtobufWkt::Struct{}}};\n   374\u2192    return std::move(ok.response_);\n   375\u2192  }\n   376\u2192\n   377\u2192  // Create a Denied authorization response.\n   378\u2192  SuccessResponse denied{message->headers(),\n   379\u2192                         config_->clientHeaderMatchers(),\n   380\u2192                         config_->upstreamHeaderToAppendMatchers(),\n   381\u2192                         config_->clientHeaderOnSuccessMatchers(),\n   382\u2192                         config_->dynamicMetadataMatchers(),\n   383\u2192                         Response{CheckStatus::Denied,\n   384\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   385\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   386\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   387\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   388\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   389\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   390\u2192                                  UnsafeHeaderVector{},\n   391\u2192                                  {{}},\n   392\u2192                                  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector{},\n   393\u2192                                  {},\n   394\u2192                                  message->bodyAsString(),\n   395\u2192                                  static_cast<Http::Code>(status_code),\n   396\u2192                                  ProtobufWkt::Struct{}}};\n   397\u2192  return std::move(denied.response_);\n   398\u2192}\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   401\u2192} // namespace Common\n   402\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   403\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   404\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   405\u2192"

{
  "command": "head -50 /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_fuzz_test.cc",
  "description": "Check HTTP fuzz test setup"
}
Output
"#include <memory>\n#include <string>\n\n#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.validate.h\"\n#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.h\"\n\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/test_common.h\"\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/http/common/fuzz/http_filter_fuzzer.h\"\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz.pb.h\"\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz.pb.validate.h\"\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_lib.h\"\n#include \"test/fuzz/fuzz_runner.h\"\n#include \"test/mocks/server/server_factory_context.h\"\n#include \"test/mocks/upstream/cluster_manager.h\"\n\n#include \"gmock/gmock.h\"\n\nusing Envoy::Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::TestCommon;\nusing envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::ExtAuthzTestCaseHttp;\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Extensions {\nnamespace HttpFilters {\nnamespace ExtAuthz {\nnamespace {\n\nstd::string resultCaseToHttpStatus(const ExtAuthzTestCaseHttp::AuthResult result) {\n  switch (result) {\n    PANIC_ON_PROTO_ENUM_SENTINEL_VALUES;\n  case ExtAuthzTestCaseHttp::OK:\n    return \"200\";\n  case ExtAuthzTestCaseHttp::ERROR:\n    return \"500\";\n  case ExtAuthzTestCaseHttp::DENIED:\n    return \"403\";\n  }\n  PANIC_DUE_TO_CORRUPT_ENUM;\n}\n\nclass ReusableHttpClientFactory {\npublic:\n  ReusableHttpClientFactory() : http_sync_request_(&internal_http_mock_client_) {\n    cm_.initializeThreadLocalClusters({\"ext_authz\"});\n    ON_CALL(cm_.thread_local_cluster_, httpAsyncClient())\n        .WillByDefault(ReturnRef(internal_http_mock_client_));\n\n    ON_CALL(internal_http_mock_client_, send_(_, _, _))"

}

"#include <memory>\n\n#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.validate.h\"\n#include \"envoy/grpc/status.h\"\n#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/test_common.h\"\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/http/common/fuzz/http_filter_fuzzer.h\"\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz.pb.h\"\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz.pb.validate.h\"\n#include \"test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_lib.h\"\n#include \"test/fuzz/fuzz_runner.h\"\n#include \"test/mocks/grpc/mocks.h\"\n\n#include \"gmock/gmock.h\"\n\nusing Envoy::Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::TestCommon;\nusing envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::ExtAuthzTestCaseGrpc;\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Extensions {\nnamespace HttpFilters {\nnamespace ExtAuthz {\nnamespace {\n\nclass ReusableGrpcClientFactory {\npublic:\n  ReusableGrpcClientFactory()\n      : internal_grpc_mock_client_(std::make_shared<NiceMock<Grpc::MockAsyncClient>>()) {\n    ON_CALL(*internal_grpc_mock_client_, sendRaw(_, _, _, _, _, _))\n        .WillByDefault(\n            Invoke([&](absl::string_view, absl::string_view, Buffer::InstancePtr&& serialized_req,\n                       Grpc::RawAsyncRequestCallbacks&, Tracing::Span&,\n                       const Http::AsyncClient::RequestOptions&) -> Grpc::AsyncRequest* {\n              envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest check_request;\n              EXPECT_TRUE(check_request.ParseFromString(serialized_req->toString()))\n                  << \"Could not parse serialized check request\";\n\n              ENVOY_LOG_MISC(trace, \"Check Request attributes:\\n{}\",\n                             check_request.attributes().DebugString());\n\n              if (failure_reason_) {\n                grpc_client_->onFailure(Envoy::Grpc::Status::WellKnownGrpcStatus::Unknown,\n                                        *failure_reason_, mock_span_);\n              } else {\n                grpc_client_->onSuccess(std::move(response_), mock_span_);"

append_matchers_(append_matchers),\n    59\u2192        response_matchers_(response_matchers),\n    60\u2192        to_dynamic_metadata_matchers_(dynamic_metadata_matchers),\n    61\u2192        response_(std::make_unique<Response>(response)) {\n    62\u2192    headers_.iterate([this](const Http::HeaderEntry& header) -> Http::HeaderMap::Iterate {\n    63\u2192      // UpstreamHeaderMatcher\n    64\u2192      if (matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\n    65\u2192        response_->headers_to_set.emplace_back(header.key().getStringView(),\n    66\u2192                                               header.value().getStringView());\n    67\u2192      }\n    68\u2192      if (append_matchers_->matches(header.key().getStringView())) {\n    69\u2192        // If there is an existing matching key in the current headers, the new entry will be\n    70\u2192        // appended with the same key. request to upstream server will have two entries for \"key\": {\"key\": \"value1\", \"key\":\n    73\u2192        // \"value2\"}.\n    74\u2192        response_->headers_to_add.emplace_back(header.key().getStringView(),\n    75\u2192                                               header.value().getStringView());\n    76\u2192      }\n    77\u2192 90\u2192  }\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192  const Http::HeaderMap& headers_;\n    93\u2192  // All matchers below are used on headers_.\n    94\u2192  const MatcherSharedPtr& matchers_;\n    95\u2192 602\u2192// Test the client when the request is canceled.\n   603\u2192TEST_F(ExtAuthzHttpClientTest, CancelledAuthorizationRequest) {\n   604\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest request;\n 608\u2192\n   609\u2192  EXPECT_CALL(async_request_, cancel());\n   610\u2192  client_->cancel();\n   611\u2192}\n   612\u2192\n   613\u2192// Test the client when the configured cluster is getThreadLocalCluster(Eq(\"ext_authz\"))).WillOnce(Return(nullptr));\n   618\u2192  EXPECT_CALL(cm_.thread_local_cluster_, httpAsyncClient()).Times(0);\n   619\u2192  EXPECT_CALL(request_callbacks_,\n envoy.filters.network.name.\n    12\u2192 */\n    13\u2192class NetworkFilterNameValues {\n    14\u2192public:\n    15\u2192  // Client ssl auth filter\n    16\u2192  const std::string ClientSslAuth = filter\n    37\u2192  const std::string MySQLProxy = \"envoy.filters.network.mysql_proxy\";\n    38\u2192  // Postgres proxy filter\n    39\u2192  const std::string PostgresProxy = \"envoy.filters.network.postgres_proxy\";\n    40\u2192  // Rate limit filter\n    41\u2192  const std::string RateLimit = \"envoy.filters.network.ratelimit\";\n    42\u2192  // Redis proxy filter\n {
  "file_path": "/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz.proto"

Output
"     1\u2192syntax = \"proto3\";\n     2\u2192package envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz;\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192import \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.proto\";\n     5\u2192import \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto\";\n     6\u2192import \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.proto\";\n     7\u2192import \"test/fuzz/common.proto\";\n     8\u2192import \"validate/validate.proto\";\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192// We only fuzz a single request per iteration.\n    11\u2192message ExtAuthzTestCaseBase {\n    12\u2192  envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz config = 1\n    13\u2192      [(validate.rules).message = {required: true}];\n    14\u2192  // HTTP request data.\n    15\u2192  test.fuzz.HttpData request_data = 2 [(validate.rules).message = {required: true}];\n    16\u2192  // Filter metadata.\n    17\u2192  envoy.config.core.v3.Metadata filter_metadata = 4;\n    18\u2192}\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192message ExtAuthzTestCaseGrpc {\n    21\u2192  ExtAuthzTestCaseBase base = 1 [(validate.rules).message = {required: true}];\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192  oneof response_or_failure_reason {\n    24\u2192    // Full auth check result. Note it is not validated to simulate an untrusted authz server (i.e.\n    25\u2192    // it can contain garbage mutations).\n    26\u2192    envoy.service.auth.v3.CheckResponse response = 2 [(validate.rules).message.skip = true];\n    27\u2192    // If this is set onFailure will be called instead of onSuccess.\n    28\u2192    string failure_reason = 3;\n    29\u2192  }\n    30\u2192}\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192message ExtAuthzTestCaseHttp {\n    33\u2192  ExtAuthzTestCaseBase base = 1 [(validate.rules).message = {required: true}];\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192  enum AuthResult {\n    36\u2192    // Possible results for a check call. Taken from\n    37\u2192    // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/945b5833f094dee31d2971cee8d40553bb0fe714/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h#L65\n    38\u2192    OK = 0;\n    39\u2192    DENIED = 1;\n    40\u2192    ERROR = 2;\n    41\u2192  }\n    42\u2192  // Set default auth check result.\n    43\u2192  AuthResult result = 3 [(validate.rules).enum.defined_only = true];\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192"

"     1\u2192syntax = \"proto3\";\n     2\u2192package envoy.extensions.filters.network.ext_authz;\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192import \"envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto\";\n     5\u2192import \"google/protobuf/empty.proto\";\n     6\u2192import \"validate/validate.proto\";\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192message Result {\n     9\u2192  oneof result_selector {\n    10\u2192    option (validate.required) = true;\n    11\u2192    // Authorization check status\n    12\u2192    google.protobuf.Empty check_status_error = 1;\n    13\u2192    google.protobuf.Empty check_status_denied = 2;\n    14\u2192    google.protobuf.Empty check_status_ok = 3;\n    15\u2192  }\n    16\u2192}\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192message OnData {\n    19\u2192  bytes data = 1;\n    20\u2192  bool end_stream = 2;\n    21\u2192  // optional: to set the default authorization check result for this and the following onData()\n    22\u2192  Result result = 3;\n    23\u2192}\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192message Action {\n    26\u2192  oneof action_selector {\n    27\u2192    option (validate.required) = true;\n    28\u2192    // Call onNewConnection().\n    29\u2192    google.protobuf.Empty on_new_connection = 1;\n    30\u2192    // Call onData().\n    31\u2192    OnData on_data = 2;\n    32\u2192    // Connection close\n    33\u2192    google.protobuf.Empty remote_close = 3;\n    34\u2192    google.protobuf.Empty local_close = 4;\n    35\u2192  }\n    36\u2192}\n    37\u2192message ExtAuthzTestCase {\n    38\u2192  envoy.extensions.filters.network.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz config = 1\n    39\u2192      [(validate.rules).message = {required: true}];\n    40\u2192  repeated Action actions = 2;\n    41\u2192}\n    42\u2192"

"   280\u2192  client_->onSuccess(std::move(check_response), span_);\n   281\u2192}\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192// Test the client when an unknown error occurs.\n   284\u2192TEST_F(ExtAuthzGrpcClientTest, UnknownError) {\n   285\u2192  initialize();\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest request;\n   288\u2192  expectCallSend(request);\n   289\u2192  client_->check(request_callbacks_, request, Tracing::NullSpan::instance(), stream_info_);\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192  EXPECT_CALL(request_callbacks_,\n   292\u2192              onComplete_(WhenDynamicCastTo<ResponsePtr&>(AuthzErrorResponse(CheckStatus::Error))));\n   293\u2192  client_->onFailure(Grpc::Status::Unknown, \"\", span_);\n   294\u2192}\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192// Test the client when the request is canceled.\n   297\u2192TEST_F(ExtAuthzGrpcClientTest, CancelledAuthorizationRequest) {\n   298\u2192  initialize();\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest request;\n   301\u2192  EXPECT_CALL(*async_client_, sendRaw(_, _, _, _, _, _)).WillOnce(Return(&async_request_));\n   302\u2192  client_->check(request_callbacks_, request, Tracing::NullSpan::instance(), stream_info_);\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192  EXPECT_CALL(async_request_, cancel());\n   305\u2192  client_->cancel();\n   306\u2192}\n   307\u2192\n   308\u2192// Test the client when the request times out.\n   309\u2192TEST_F(ExtAuthzGrpcClientTest, AuthorizationRequestTimeout) {\n   310\u2192  initialize();\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192  envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest request;\n   313\u2192  expectCallSend(request);\n   314\u2192  client_->check(request_callbacks_, request, Tracing::NullSpan::instance(), stream_info_);\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192  EXPECT_CALL(request_callbacks_,\n   317\u2192              onComplete_(WhenDynamicCastTo<ResponsePtr&>(AuthzErrorResponse(CheckStatus::Error))));\n   318\u2192  client_->onFailure(Grpc::Status::DeadlineExceeded, \"\", span_);\n   319\u2192}\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192// Test the client when an OK response is received with dynamic metadata in that OK response.\n   322\u2192TEST_F(ExtAuthzGrpcClientTest, AuthorizationOkWithDynamicMetadata) {\n   323\u2192  initialize();\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192  auto check_response = std::make_unique<envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckResponse>();\n   326\u2192  auto status = check_response->mutable_status();\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192  ProtobufWkt::Struct expected_dynamic_metadata;\n   329\u2192  auto* metadata_fields = expected_dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields();"

\"test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/logging_test_filter.pb.validate.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"test/test_common/utility.h\"\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192#include \"gtest/gtest.h\"\n    16\u2192\n retrieve the logging info on encodeComplete.\n    23\u2192class LoggingTestFilter : public Http::PassThroughFilter {\n    24\u2192public:\n    25\u2192  LoggingTestFilter(const std::string& logging_id, const std::string& cluster_name,\n    26\u2192                    bool expect_stats, bool expect_envoy_grpc_specific_stats,\n    27\u2192                    bool expect_response_bytes, const ProtobufWkt::Struct& filter_metadata)\n    28\u2192      : logging_id_(logging_id), expected_cluster_name_(cluster_name), expect_stats_(expect_stats),\n    29\u2192        expect_envoy_grpc_specific_stats_(expect_envoy_grpc_specific_stats),\n    30\u2192        expect_response_bytes_(expect_response_bytes), filter_metadata_(filter_metadata) {}\n    31\u2192  void encodeComplete() override {\n    32\u2192    ASSERT(decoder_callbacks_ != nullptr);\n    33\u2192    const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\n    34\u2192        decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192    ASSERT_EQ(filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthz::ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(logging_id_), expect_stats_);\n    37\u2192    if (!expect_stats_) {\n    38\u2192      return;\n    39\u2192    }\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    const ExtAuthz::ExtAuthzLoggingInfo* ext_authz_logging_info =\n    42\u2192        filter_state->getDataReadOnly<ExtAuthz::ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(logging_id_);\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192    ASSERT_EQ(ext_authz_logging_info->filterMetadata().has_value(), filter_metadata_.has_value());\n    45\u2192    if (filter_metadata_.has_value()) {\n    46\u2192      // TODO: Is there a better way to do deep comparison of protos?\n    47\u2192      EXPECT_EQ(ext_authz_logging_info->filterMetadata()->DebugString(),\n    48\u2192                filter_metadata_->DebugString());\n    49\u2192    }\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    ASSERT_TRUE(ext_authz_logging_info->latency().has_value());\n    52\u2192    EXPECT_GT(ext_authz_logging_info->latency()->count(), 0);\n    53\u2192    if (expect_envoy_grpc_specific_stats_) {\n    54\u2192      // If the stats exist a request should always have been sent.\n    55\u2192      EXPECT_GT(ext_authz_logging_info->bytesSent(), 0);\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192      // A response may or may not have been received depending on the test.\n    58\u2192      if (expect_response_bytes_) {\n    59\u2192        EXPECT_GT(ext_authz_logging_info->bytesReceived(), 0);\n    60\u2192      } else {\n    61\u2192        EXPECT_EQ(ext_authz_logging_info->bytesReceived(), 0);\n    62\u2192      }\n    63\u2192      ASSERT_NE(ext_authz_logging_info->upstreamHost(), nullptr);\n    64\u2192      EXPECT_EQ(ext_authz_logging_info->upstreamHost()->cluster().name(), expected_cluster_name_);\n    65\u2192    }\n    66\u2192  }\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192private:\n    69\u2192  const std::string logging_id_;\n    70\u2192  const std::string expected_cluster_name_;\n    71\u2192  const bool expect_stats_;\n    72\u2192  const bool expect_envoy_grpc_specific_stats_;\n    73\u2192  const bool expect_response_bytes_;\n    74\u2192  const absl::optional<ProtobufWkt::Struct> filter_metadata_;\n    75\u2192};\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192class LoggingTestFilterFactory : public Extensions::HttpFilters::Common::FactoryBase<\n    78\u2192                                     test::integration::filters::LoggingTestFilterConfig> {\n    79\u2192public:\n    80\u2192  LoggingTestFilterFactory() : FactoryBase(\"logging-test-filter\"){};\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192  Http::FilterFactoryCb createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped(\n    83\u2192      const test::integration::filters::LoggingTestFilterConfig& proto_config, const std::string&,\n    84\u2192      Server::Configuration::FactoryContext&) override {\n    85\u2192    return [=](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) -> void {\n    86\u2192      callbacks.addStreamFilter(std::make_shared<LoggingTestFilter>(\n    87\u2192          proto_config.logging_id(), proto_config.upstream_cluster_name(),\n    88\u2192          proto_config.expect_stats(), proto_config.expect_envoy_grpc_specific_stats(),\n    89\u2192          proto_config.expect_response_bytes(), proto_config.filter_metadata()));\n    90\u2192    };\n    91\u2192  }\n    92\u2192};\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192// Perform static registration\n    95\u2192static Registry::RegisterFactory<LoggingTestFilterFactory,\n    96\u2192                                 Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory>\n    97\u2192    register_;\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192} // namespace ExternalProcessing\n   100\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\n   101\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   102\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   103\u2192"

11\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/network/well_known_names.h\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    14\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    15\u2192namespace NetworkFilters {\n    16\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192InstanceStats Config::generateStats(const std::string& name, Stats::Scope& scope) {\n    19\u2192  const std::string final_prefix = fmt::format(\"ext_authz.{}.\", name);\n    20\u2192  return {ALL_TCP_EXT_AUTHZ_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix),\n    21\u2192                                  POOL_GAUGE_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\n    22\u2192}\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192void Filter::callCheck() {\n    25\u2192  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::createTcpCheck(\n    26\u2192      filter_callbacks_, check_request_, config_->includePeerCertificate(),\n    27\u2192      config_->includeTLSSession(), config_->destinationLabels());\n    28\u2192  // Store start time of ext_authz filter call\n    29\u2192  start_time_ = filter_callbacks_->connection().dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime();\n    30\u2192  status_ = Status::Calling;\n    31\u2192  config_->stats().active_.inc();\n    32\u2192  config_->stats().total_.inc();\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192  calling_check_ = true;\n    35\u2192  client_->check(*this, check_request_, Tracing::NullSpan::instance(),\n    36\u2192                 filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo());\n    37\u2192  calling_check_ = false;\n    38\u2192}\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192Network::FilterStatus Filter::onData(Buffer::Instance&, bool /* end_stream */) {\n    41\u2192  if (!filterEnabled(filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().dynamicMetadata())) {\n    42\u2192    config_->stats().disabled_.inc();\n    43\u2192    return Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\n    44\u2192  }\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192  if (status_ == Status::NotStarted) {\n    47\u2192    // By waiting to invoke the check at onData() the call to authorization service will have\n    48\u2192    // sufficient information to fill out the checkRequest_.\n    49\u2192    callCheck();\n    50\u2192  }\n    51\u2192  return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::Stop ? Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration\n    52\u2192                                              : Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\n    53\u2192}\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192Network::FilterStatus Filter::onNewConnection() {\n    56\u2192  // Wait till onData() happens.\n    57\u2192  return Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\n    58\u2192}\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192void Filter::onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) {\n    61\u2192  if (event == Network::ConnectionEvent::RemoteClose ||\n    62\u2192      event == Network::ConnectionEvent::LocalClose) {\n    63\u2192    if (status_ == Status::Calling) {\n    64\u2192      // Make sure that any pending request in the client is cancelled. This will be NOP if the\n    65\u2192      // request already completed.\n    66\u2192      client_->cancel();\n    67\u2192      config_->stats().active_.dec();\n    68\u2192    }\n    69\u2192  }\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\n    73\u2192  status_ = Status::Complete;\n    74\u2192  config_->stats().active_.dec();\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  switch (response->status) {\n    77\u2192  case Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::OK:\n    78\u2192    config_->stats().ok_.inc();\n    79\u2192    // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\n    80\u2192    if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n    81\u2192      ProtobufWkt::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\n    82\u2192      auto duration = filter_callbacks_->connection().dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() -\n    83\u2192                      start_time_.value();\n    84\u2192      ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\n    85\u2192          std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\n    86\u2192      (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\"ext_authz_duration\"] =\n    87\u2192          ext_authz_duration_value;\n    88\u2192    }\n    89\u2192    break;\n    90\u2192  case Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Error:\n    91\u2192    config_->stats().error_.inc();\n    92\u2192    break;\n    93\u2192  case Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Denied:\n    94\u2192    config_->stats().denied_.inc();\n    95\u2192    break;\n    96\u2192  }\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    filter_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata("

126. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore ext_authz dependencies",
  "max_turns": 20,
  "prompt": "Explore the Envoy ext_authz filter BUILD files to understand its upstream dependencies and downstream consumers. I need:\n\n1. Look at source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD - what deps does it list?\n2. Look at source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/BUILD - what deps does it list?\n3. Look at source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD - what deps does it list?\n4. Search for references to ext_authz in other parts of the codebase - who imports or depends on ext_authz? Check:\n   - source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl or similar extension registration files\n   - Any all_extensions or filter chain configs that reference ext_authz\n   - grep for \"ext_authz\" in source/extensions/ outside the ext_authz directories themselves\n\n5. Check how ext_authz is registered as a named filter - look for the filter name registration (e.g., \"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\")\n\nBe thorough and provide the specific dependency names from BUILD files.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "aa556f9",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report with all the information gathered.\n\n## Comprehensive Analysis of Envoy ext_authz Filter BUILD Files and Dependencies\n\nI've thoroughly explored the Envoy ext_authz filter architecture. Here's what I found:\n\n### 1. HTTP ext_authz Filter Dependencies\n**File:** `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD`\n\nThe HTTP filter has two main targets:\n\n**`ext_authz` library target** depends on:\n- `//envoy/http:codes_interface`\n- `//envoy/stats:stats_macros`\n- `//source/common/buffer:buffer_lib`\n- `//source/common/common:assert_lib`\n- `//source/common/common:empty_string`\n- `//source/common/common:enum_to_int`\n- `//source/common/common:matchers_lib`\n- `//source/common/common:minimal_logger_lib`\n- `//source/common/http:codes_lib`\n- `//source/common/http:utility_lib`\n- `//source/common/router:config_lib`\n- `//source/common/runtime:runtime_protos_lib`\n- `//source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_lib` (CRITICAL SHARED DEPENDENCY)\n- `//source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_lib` (CRITICAL SHARED DEPENDENCY)\n- `//source/extensions/filters/common/mutation_rules:mutation_rules_lib`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n\n**`config` extension target** depends on:\n- `:ext_authz` (local library)\n- `//envoy/registry`\n- `//envoy/stats:stats_macros`\n- `//source/common/config:utility_lib`\n- `//source/common/protobuf:utility_lib`\n- `//source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_lib`\n- `//source/extensions/filters/http/common:factory_base_lib`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n\n---\n\n### 2. Network ext_authz Filter Dependencies\n**File:** `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/BUILD`\n\nThe network filter has two main targets:\n\n**`ext_authz` library target** depends on:\n- `//envoy/network:connection_interface`\n- `//envoy/network:filter_interface`\n- `//envoy/runtime:runtime_interface`\n- `//envoy/stats:stats_macros`\n- `//envoy/upstream:cluster_manager_interface`\n- `//source/common/common:assert_lib`\n- `//source/common/common:matchers_lib`\n- `//source/common/tracing:http_tracer_lib`\n- `//source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_lib` (CRITICAL SHARED DEPENDENCY)\n- `//source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_interface` (CRITICAL SHARED DEPENDENCY)\n- `//source/extensions/filters/network:well_known_names`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n\n**`config` extension target** depends on:\n- `//envoy/registry`\n- `//source/common/config:utility_lib`\n- `//source/common/protobuf:utility_lib`\n- `//source/extensions/filters/network:well_known_names`\n- `//source/extensions/filters/network/common:factory_base_lib`\n- `//source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz` (internal reference)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n\n---\n\n### 3. Common ext_authz Shared Library Dependencies\n**File:** `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD`\n\nThis is the critical shared component with four targets:\n\n**`ext_authz_interface` library** (core interface):\n- `//envoy/http:codes_interface`\n- `//envoy/stream_info:stream_info_interface`\n- `//source/common/http:headers_lib`\n- `//source/common/tracing:http_tracer_lib`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n\n**`ext_authz_grpc_lib` library** (gRPC client implementation):\n- `:check_request_utils_lib`\n- `:ext_authz_interface`\n- `//envoy/grpc:async_client_interface`\n- `//envoy/grpc:async_client_manager_interface`\n- `//envoy/http:filter_interface`\n- `//envoy/http:header_map_interface`\n- `//envoy/http:protocol_interface`\n- `//envoy/network:address_interface`\n- `//envoy/network:connection_interface`\n- `//envoy/network:filter_interface`\n- `//envoy/upstream:cluster_manager_interface`\n- `//source/common/common:assert_lib`\n- `//source/common/grpc:async_client_lib`\n- `//source/common/grpc:typed_async_client_lib`\n- `//source/common/http:headers_lib`\n- `//source/common/http:utility_lib`\n- `//source/common/network:utility_lib`\n- `//source/common/protobuf`\n- `//source/common/tracing:http_tracer_lib`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n\n**`ext_authz_http_lib` library** (HTTP client implementation):\n- `:check_request_utils_lib`\n- `:ext_authz_interface`\n- `//envoy/upstream:cluster_manager_interface`\n- `//source/common/common:matchers_lib`\n- `//source/common/common:minimal_logger_lib`\n- `//source/common/http:async_client_lib`\n- `//source/common/http:codes_lib`\n- `//source/common/tracing:http_tracer_lib`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/type/matcher/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n\n**`check_request_utils_lib` library** (shared utilities):\n- `:ext_authz_interface`\n- `//envoy/grpc:async_client_interface`\n- `//envoy/grpc:async_client_manager_interface`\n- `//envoy/http:filter_interface`\n- `//envoy/upstream:cluster_manager_interface`\n- `//source/common/grpc:async_client_lib`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto`\n\n---\n\n### 4. Extension Registration and Filter Names\n**File:** `/workspace/source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl`\n\nThe ext_authz filters are registered as:\n\n**HTTP Filter:**\n- Filter name: `\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\"`\n- Config target: `\"//source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:config\"`\n\n**Network Filter:**\n- Filter name: `\"envoy.filters.network.ext_authz\"`\n- Config target: `\"//source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz:config\"`\n\n---\n\n### 5. Well-Known Names Registration\n**Files:**\n- `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/well_known_names.h` (line 74)\n- `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/well_known_names.h` (line 47)\n\n**HTTP Filter Registration:**\n```cpp\nconst std::string ExtAuthorization = \"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\";\n```\n\n**Network Filter Registration:**\n```cpp\nconst std::string ExtAuthorization = \"envoy.filters.network.ext_authz\";\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. Downstream Consumers and Tests\n**Test files that depend on ext_authz:**\n- `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD`\n- `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/BUILD`\n- `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD`\n- `/workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/common/fuzz/BUILD`\n\nThe ext_authz filters are marked as stable with:\n- **Security posture:** `robust_to_untrusted_downstream`\n- **Status:** `stable`\n- **Categories:** `envoy.filters.http` and `envoy.filters.network`\n\n---\n\n### 7. Key Architectural Insights\n\n**Shared Components:**\nThe ext_authz filters use a shared implementation layer at `/workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/` that provides:\n- Common interface definition (`ext_authz_interface`)\n- gRPC client implementation (`ext_authz_grpc_lib`)\n- HTTP client implementation (`ext_authz_http_lib`)\n- Check request utilities (`check_request_utils_lib`)\n\n**Both HTTP and network filters consume:**\n- `ext_authz_grpc_lib` - for gRPC-based authorization\n- `ext_authz_interface` (network) or `ext_authz_http_lib` (HTTP) - for respective implementations\n\n**Filter Factory Pattern:**\n- Uses `FactoryBase` from `/source/extensions/filters/http/common:factory_base_lib`\n- Uses `NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory` for HTTP\n- Uses `NamedNetworkFilterConfigFactory` for network\n- Registration name: `\"envoy.ext_authz\"` for legacy compatibility (shown in config.cc line 77 and config.cc line 50)\n\nThis architecture allows code reuse between the HTTP and network filter implementations while maintaining separation between the filter-specific logic and the shared authorization check logic.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],

Look at source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD - what deps does it list?\n2. Look at source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/BUILD - what deps does it list?\n3. Look at source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/BUILD - what deps does it list?\n4. Search for references to ext_authz in other parts of the codebase - who imports or depends on ext_authz? Check:\n   - source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl or similar extension registration files\n   - Any all_extensions or filter chain configs that reference ext_authz\n   - grep for \"ext_authz\" in source/extensions/ outside the ext_authz directories themselves\n\n5. Check how ext_authz is registered as a named filter - look for the filter name registration (e.g., \"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\")\n\nBe thorough and provide the specific dependency names from BUILD files.",

\"//source/extensions/filters/network/generic_proxy/codecs/http1:config\",\n   538\u2192}\n   539\u2192\n   540\u2192# These can be changed to [\"//visibility:public\"], for  downstream builds which\n   541\u2192# need to directly reference Envoy extensions.\n   542\u2192EXTENSION_CONFIG_VISIBILITY = [\"//:extension_config\", \"//:contrib_library\", \"//:mobile_library\"]\n   543\u2192EXTENSION_PACKAGE_VISIBILITY = [\"//:extension_library\", \"//:contrib_library\", \"//:mobile_library\"]\n   544\u2192CONTRIB_EXTENSION_PACKAGE_VISIBILITY = [\"//:contrib_library\"]\n   545\u2192MOBILE_PACKAGE_VISIBILITY = [\"//:mobile_library\"]\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192# Set this variable to true to disable alwayslink for envoy_cc_library.\n   548\u2192# TODO(alyssawilk) audit uses of this in source/ and migrate all libraries to extensions.\n   549\u2192LEGACY_ALWAYSLINK = 1\n   550\u2192"
133. Grep @ -
Input
{
  "output_mode": "content",
  "path": "/workspace/source/extensions",
  "pattern": "envoy\\.filters\\.(http|network)\\.ext_authz"
}
Output
"source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl:152:    \"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\":                     \"//source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:config\",\nsource/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl:214:    \"envoy.filters.network.ext_authz\":                            \"//source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz:config\",\nsource/extensions/filters/network/well_known_names.h:47:  const std::string ExtAuthorization = \"envoy.filters.network.ext_authz\";\nsource/extensions/filters/http/well_known_names.h:74:  const std::string ExtAuthorization = \"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\";\nsource/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config.h:22:  ExtAuthzFilterConfig() : FactoryBase(\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\") {}\nsource/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc:495:      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\",\nsource/extensions/extensions_metadata.yaml:339:envoy.filters.http.ext_authz:\nsource/extensions/extensions_metadata.yaml:658:envoy.filters.network.ext_authz:"
134. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/well_known_names.h"
}
Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"source/common/config/well_known_names.h\"\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192namespace Envoy {\n     6\u2192namespace Extensions {\n     7\u2192namespace HttpFilters {\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192/**\n    10\u2192 * Well-known http filter names.\n    11\u2192 * NOTE: New filters should use the well known name: envoy.filters.http.name.\n    12\u2192 */\n    13\u2192class HttpFilterNameValues {\n    14\u2192public:\n    15\u2192  // Buffer filter\n    16\u2192  const std::string Buffer = \"envoy.filters.http.buffer\";\n    17\u2192  // Bandwidth limit filter\n    18\u2192  const std::string BandwidthLimit = \"envoy.filters.http.bandwidth_limit\";\n    19\u2192  // Basic Auth filter\n    20\u2192  const std::string BasicAuth = \"envoy.filters.http.basic_auth\";\n    21\u2192  // Cache filter\n    22\u2192  const std::string Cache = \"envoy.filters.http.cache\";\n    23\u2192  // CDN Loop filter\n    24\u2192  const std::string CdnLoop = \"envoy.filters.http.cdn_loop\";\n    25\u2192  // Compressor filter\n    26\u2192  const std::string Compressor = \"envoy.filters.http.compressor\";\n    27\u2192  // CORS filter\n    28\u2192  const std::string Cors = \"envoy.filters.http.cors\";\n    29\u2192  // Composite filter\n    30\u2192  const std::string Composite = \"envoy.filters.http.composite\";\n    31\u2192  // Credential Injector filter\n    32\u2192  const std::string CredentialInjector = \"envoy.filters.http.credential_injector\";\n    33\u2192  // CSRF filter\n    34\u2192  const std::string Csrf = \"envoy.filters.http.csrf\";\n    35\u2192  // Custom Response filter\n    36\u2192  const std::string CustomResponse = \"envoy.filters.http.custom_response\";\n    37\u2192  // Decompressor filter\n    38\u2192  const std::string Decompressor = \"envoy.filters.http.decompressor\";\n    39\u2192  // Dynamo filter\n    40\u2192  const std::string Dynamo = \"envoy.filters.http.dynamo\";\n    41\u2192  // Fault filter\n    42\u2192  const std::string Fault = \"envoy.filters.http.fault\";\n    43\u2192  // GCP authentication filter\n    44\u2192  const std::string GcpAuthn = \"envoy.filters.http.gcp_authn\";\n    45\u2192  // GRPC http1 bridge filter\n    46\u2192  const std::string GrpcHttp1Bridge = \"envoy.filters.http.grpc_http1_bridge\";\n    47\u2192  // GRPC json transcoder filter\n    48\u2192  const std::string GrpcJsonTranscoder = \"envoy.filters.http.grpc_json_transcoder\";\n    49\u2192  // GRPC web filter\n    50\u2192  const std::string GrpcWeb = \"envoy.filters.http.grpc_web\";\n    51\u2192  // GRPC Field Extraction filter\n    52\u2192  const std::string GrpcFieldExtraction = \"envoy.filters.http.grpc_field_extraction\";\n    53\u2192  // Proto Message Extraction filter\n    54\u2192  const std::string ProtoMessageExtraction = \"envoy.filters.http.proto_message_extraction\";\n    55\u2192  // GRPC http1 reverse bridge filter\n    56\u2192  const std::string GrpcHttp1ReverseBridge = \"envoy.filters.http.grpc_http1_reverse_bridge\";\n    57\u2192  // GRPC telemetry\n    58\u2192  const std::string GrpcStats = \"envoy.filters.http.grpc_stats\";\n    59\u2192  // IP tagging filter\n    60\u2192  const std::string IpTagging = \"envoy.filters.http.ip_tagging\";\n    61\u2192  // Rate limit filter\n    62\u2192  const std::string RateLimit = \"envoy.filters.http.ratelimit\";\n    63\u2192  // Router filter\n    64\u2192  const std::string Router = \"envoy.filters.http.router\";\n    65\u2192  // Health checking filter\n    66\u2192  const std::string HealthCheck = \"envoy.filters.http.health_check\";\n    67\u2192  // Lua filter\n    68\u2192  const std::string Lua = \"envoy.filters.http.lua\";\n    69\u2192  // On-demand RDS updates filter\n    70\u2192  const std::string OnDemand = \"envoy.filters.http.on_demand\";\n    71\u2192  // Squash filter\n    72\u2192  const std::string Squash = \"envoy.filters.http.squash\";\n    73\u2192  // External Authorization filter\n    74\u2192  const std::string ExtAuthorization = \"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\";\n    75\u2192  // RBAC HTTP Authorization filter\n    76\u2192  const std::string Rbac = \"envoy.filters.http.rbac\";\n    77\u2192  // JWT authentication filter\n    78\u2192  const std::string JwtAuthn = 88\u2192  const std::string AdaptiveConcurrency = \"envoy.filters.http.adaptive_concurrency\";\n    89\u2192  // Admission control filter\n    90\u2192  const std::string AdmissionControl = \"envoy.filters.http.admission_control\";\n    91\u2192  // Original Src Filter\n    92\u2192  const std::string OriginalSrc = \"envoy.filters.http.original_src\";\n    93\u2192  // Dynamic forward proxy filter\n    94\u2192  const std::string DynamicForwardProxy = \"envoy.filters.http.dynamic_forward_proxy\";\n    95\u2192  // Alternate Protocols Cache filter\n    96\u2192  const std::string \"envoy.filters.http.ext_proc\";\n   109\u2192  // Set metadata filter\n   110\u2192  const std::string SetMetadata = \"envoy.filters.http.set_metadata\";\n   111\u2192};\n   112\u2192\n \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    20\u2192namespace HttpFilters {\n    21\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192Http::FilterFactoryCb ExtAuthzFilterConfig::createFilterFactoryFromProtoWithServerContextTyped(\n    24\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& proto_config,\n    25\u2192    const std::string& stats_prefix, Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& server_context) {\n    26\u2192  const auto filter_config = std::make_shared<FilterConfig>(proto_config, server_context.scope(),\n    27\u2192                                                            stats_prefix, server_context);\n    28\u2192  // The callback is created in main thread and executed in worker thread, variables except factory\n    29\u2192  // context must be captured by value into the callback.\n    30\u2192  Http::FilterFactoryCb callback;\n    31\u2192  if (proto_config.has_http_service()) {\n    32\u2192    // Raw HTTP client.\n    33\u2192    const uint32_t timeout_ms = PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.http_service().server_uri(),\n    34\u2192                                                           timeout, DefaultTimeout);\n    35\u2192    const auto client_config =\n    36\u2192        std::make_shared<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientConfig>(\n    37\u2192            proto_config, timeout_ms, proto_config.http_service().path_prefix(), server_context);\n    38\u2192    callback = [filter_config = std::move(filter_config), client_config,\n    39\u2192                &server_context](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) {\n    40\u2192      auto client = std::make_unique<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RawHttpClientImpl>(\n    41\u2192          server_context.clusterManager(), client_config);\n    42\u2192      callbacks.addStreamFilter(std::make_shared<Filter>(filter_config, std::move(client)));\n    43\u2192    };\n    44\u2192  } else {\n    45\u2192    // gRPC client.\n    46\u2192    const uint32_t timeout_ms =\n    47\u2192        PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.grpc_service(), timeout, DefaultTimeout);\n    48\u2192    THROW_IF_NOT_OK(Config::Utility::checkTransportVersion(proto_config));\n    49\u2192    Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey config_with_hash_key =\n    50\u2192        Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey(proto_config.grpc_service());\n    51\u2192    callback = [&server_context, filter_config = std::move(filter_config), timeout_ms,\n    52\u2192                config_with_hash_key](Http::FilterChainFactoryCallbacks& callbacks) {\n    53\u2192      auto client_or_error = server_context.clusterManager()\n    54\u2192                                 .grpcAsyncClientManager()\n    55\u2192                                 .getOrCreateRawAsyncClientWithHashKey(\n    56\u2192                                     config_with_hash_key, server_context.scope(), true);\n    57\u2192      THROW_IF_NOT_OK_REF(client_or_error.status());\n    58\u2192      auto client = std::make_unique<Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::GrpcClientImpl>(\n    59\u2192          client_or_error.value(), std::chrono::milliseconds(timeout_ms));\n    60\u2192      callbacks.addStreamFilter(std::make_shared<Filter>(filter_config, std::move(client)));\n    61\u2192    };\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192  return callback;\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfigConstSharedPtr\n    67\u2192ExtAuthzFilterConfig::createRouteSpecificFilterConfigTyped(\n    68\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthzPerRoute& proto_config,\n    69\u2192    Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext&, ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor&) {\n    70\u2192  return std::make_shared<FilterConfigPerRoute>(proto_config);\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192/**\n    74\u2192 * Static registration for the external authorization filter. @see RegisterFactory.\n    75\u2192 */\n    76\u2192LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY(ExtAuthzFilterConfig, Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory,\n    77\u2192                        \"envoy.ext_authz\");\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n    80\u2192} // namespace HttpFilters\n    81\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n    82\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n    83\u2192"

\"envoy/config/core/v3/grpc_service.pb.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.pb.validate.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/registry/registry.h\"\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192#include \"source/common/config/utility.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"source/common/protobuf/utility.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    20\u2192namespace NetworkFilters {\n    21\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192Network::FilterFactoryCb ExtAuthzConfigFactory::createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped(\n    24\u2192    const envoy::extensions::filters::network::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& proto_config,\n    25\u2192    Server::Configuration::FactoryContext& context) {\n    26\u2192  ConfigSharedPtr ext_authz_config =\n    27\u2192      std::make_shared<Config>(proto_config, context.scope(), context.serverFactoryContext());\n    28\u2192  const uint32_t timeout_ms = PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(proto_config.grpc_service(), timeout, 200);\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192  THROW_IF_NOT_OK(Envoy::Config::Utility::checkTransportVersion(proto_config));\n    31\u2192  return [grpc_service = proto_config.grpc_service(), &context, ext_authz_config,\n    32\u2192          timeout_ms](Network::FilterManager& filter_manager) -> void {\n    33\u2192    auto factory_or_error = context.serverFactoryContext()\n    34\u2192                                .clusterManager()\n    35\u2192                                .grpcAsyncClientManager()\n    36\u2192                                .factoryForGrpcService(grpc_service, context.scope(), true);\n    37\u2192    THROW_IF_NOT_OK_REF(factory_or_error.status());\n    38\u2192    auto client = std::make_unique<Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::GrpcClientImpl>(\n    39\u2192        factory_or_error.value()->createUncachedRawAsyncClient(),\n    40\u2192        std::chrono::milliseconds(timeout_ms));\n    41\u2192    filter_manager.addReadFilter(Network::ReadFilterSharedPtr{\n    42\u2192        std::make_shared<Filter>(ext_authz_config, std::move(client))});\n    43\u2192  };\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192/**\n    47\u2192 * Static registration for the external authorization filter. @see RegisterFactory.\n    48\u2192 */\n    49\u2192LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY(ExtAuthzConfigFactory,\n    50\u2192                        Server::Configuration::NamedNetworkFilterConfigFactory, \"envoy.ext_authz\");\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n    53\u2192} // namespace NetworkFilters\n    54\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n    55\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n    56\u2192"
