"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\n     4\u2192#include <memory>\n     5\u2192#include <string>\n     6\u2192#include <vector>\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/common/pure.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/http/codes.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.pb.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/stream_info/stream_info.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192#include \"source/common/http/headers.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"source/common/singleton/const_singleton.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Extensions {\n    20\u2192namespace Filters {\n    21\u2192namespace Common {\n    22\u2192namespace ExtAuthz {\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192/**\n    25\u2192 * Constant values used for tracing metadata.\n    26\u2192 */\n    27\u2192struct TracingConstantValues {\n    28\u2192  const std::string TraceStatus = \"ext_authz_status\";\n    29\u2192  const std::string TraceUnauthz = \"ext_authz_unauthorized\";\n    30\u2192  const std::string TraceOk = \"ext_authz_ok\";\n    31\u2192  const std::string HttpStatus = \"ext_authz_http_status\";\n    32\u2192};\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192using TracingConstants = ConstSingleton<TracingConstantValues>;\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192/**\n    37\u2192 * Possible constant response code details values for a check call.\n    38\u2192 */\n    39\u2192struct ResponseCodeDetailsValues {\n    40\u2192  // The ext_authz filter denied the downstream request/connection.\n    41\u2192  const std::string AuthzDenied = \"ext_authz_denied\";\n    42\u2192  // The ext_authz filter encountered a failure, and was configured to fail-closed.\n    43\u2192  const std::string AuthzError = \"ext_authz_error\";\n    44\u2192  // The ext_authz filter invalidated the CheckResponse and dropped the client request.\n    45\u2192  const std::string AuthzInvalid = \"ext_authz_invalid\";\n    46\u2192};\n    47\u2192using ResponseCodeDetails = ConstSingleton<ResponseCodeDetailsValues>;\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192/**\n    50\u2192 * Constant auth related HTTP headers. All lower case. This group of headers can\n    51\u2192 * contain prefix override headers.\n    52\u2192 */\n    53\u2192class HeaderValues {\n    54\u2192public:\n    55\u2192  const char* prefix() const { return ThreadSafeSingleton<Http::PrefixValue>::get().prefix(); }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthPartialBody{absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-partial-body\")};\n    58\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthHeadersToRemove{\n    59\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-headers-to-remove\")};\n    60\u2192  const Http::LowerCaseString EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed{\n    61\u2192      absl::StrCat(prefix(), \"-auth-failure-mode-allowed\")};\n    62\u2192};\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192using Headers = ConstSingleton<HeaderValues>;\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192/**\n    67\u2192 * Possible async results for a check call.\n    68\u2192 */\n    69\u2192enum class CheckStatus {\n    70\u2192  // The request is authorized.\n    71\u2192  OK,\n    72\u2192  // The authz service could not be queried.\n    73\u2192  Error,\n    74\u2192  // The request is denied.\n    75\u2192  Denied\n    76\u2192};\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192using UnsafeHeader = std::pair<std::string, std::string>;\n    79\u2192using UnsafeHeaderVector = std::vector<UnsafeHeader>;\n    80\u2192/**\n    81\u2192 * Authorization response object for a RequestCallback.\n    82\u2192 *\n    83\u2192 * Header mutations and query parameter mutations are not expected to already be validated by the\n    84\u2192 * client before packing them into this struct (hence they are \"unsafe\").\n    85\u2192 */\n    86\u2192struct Response {\n    87\u2192  // Call status.\n    88\u2192  CheckStatus status;\n    89\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, that will be optionally appended\n    90\u2192  // to the request to the upstream server.\n    91\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_append;\n    92\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set\n    93\u2192  // (using \"setCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n    94\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_set;\n    95\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n    96\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n    97\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_add;\n    98\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n    99\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n   100\u2192  // responses.\n   101\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add;\n   102\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n   103\u2192  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses.\n   104\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_set;\n   105\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n   106\u2192  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n   107\u2192  // responses only if the headers were not returned from the authz server.\n   108\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent;\n   109\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n   110\u2192  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses\n   111\u2192  // only if the headers were returned from the authz server.\n   112\u2192  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists;\n   113\u2192  // A set of HTTP headers consumed by the authorization server, will be removed\n   114\u2192  // from the request to the upstream server.\n   115\u2192  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\n   116\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to be set (possibly overwritten) on the\n   117\u2192  // request to the upstream server.\n   118\u2192  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector query_parameters_to_set;\n   119\u2192  // A set of query string parameters to remove from the request to the upstream server.\n   120\u2192  std::vector<std::string> query_parameters_to_remove;\n   121\u2192  // Optional http body used only on denied response.\n   122\u2192  std::string body;\n   123\u2192  // Optional http status used only on denied response.\n   124\u2192  Http::Code status_code{};\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192  // A set of metadata returned by the authorization server, that will be emitted as filter's\n   127\u2192  // dynamic metadata that other filters can leverage.\n   128\u2192  ProtobufWkt::Struct dynamic_metadata;\n   129\u2192};\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192using ResponsePtr = std::unique_ptr<Response>;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192/**\n   134\u2192 * Async callbacks used during check() calls.\n   135\u2192 */\n   136\u2192class RequestCallbacks {\n   137\u2192public:\n   138\u2192  virtual ~RequestCallbacks() = default;\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  /**\n   141\u2192   * Called when a check request is complete. The resulting ResponsePtr is supplied.\n   142\u2192   */\n   143\u2192  virtual void onComplete(ResponsePtr&& response) PURE;\n   144\u2192};\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192class Client {\n   147\u2192public:\n   148\u2192  // Destructor\n   149\u2192  virtual ~Client() = default;\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  /**\n   152\u2192   * Cancel an inflight Check request.\n   153\u2192   */\n   154\u2192  virtual void cancel() PURE;\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  /**\n   157\u2192   * Request a check call to an external authorization service which can use the\n   158\u2192   * passed request parameters to make a permit/deny decision.\n   159\u2192   * @param callback supplies the completion callbacks.\n   160\u2192   *        NOTE: The callback may happen within the calling stack.\n   161\u2192   * @param request is the proto message with the attributes of the specific payload.\n   162\u2192   * @param parent_span source for generating an egress child span as part of the trace.\n   163\u2192   * @param stream_info supplies the client's stream info.\n   164\u2192   */\n   165\u2192  virtual void check(RequestCallbacks& callback,\n   166\u2192                     const envoy::service::auth::v3::CheckRequest& request,\n   167\u2192                     Tracing::Span& parent_span, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) PURE;\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  /**\n   170\u2192   * Returns streamInfo of the current request if possible. By default just return a nullptr.\n   171\u2192   */\n   172\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo const* streamInfo() const { return nullptr; }\n   173\u2192};\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192using ClientPtr = std::unique_ptr<Client>;\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192} // namespace ExtAuthz\n   178\u2192} // namespace Common\n   179\u2192} // namespace Filters\n   180\u2192} // namespace Extensions\n   181\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   182\u2192"

147. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# ext_authz Filter Handoff Document\n\n## 1. Purpose\n\nThe ext_authz filter is an external authorization filter for Envoy proxy that delegates authorization decisions to an external service. Rather than embedding authorization logic directly in Envoy, it sends check requests to a separate authorization server and acts on the response (allow, deny, or error).\n\n**What problems does it solve?**\n\n- **Centralized authorization**: Decouples authorization logic from the proxy, allowing a single authorization service to enforce policies across an entire fleet of Envoy proxies.\n- **Fine-grained access control**: The authorization service receives rich request context (headers, path, client certificate, peer info, request body) and can make nuanced authorization decisions.\n- **Dynamic policy updates**: Authorization policies can be updated in the external service without redeploying Envoy.\n- **Protocol flexibility**: Supports both gRPC and HTTP protocols for communicating with the authorization service, enabling integration with a wide variety of auth backends (OPA, custom services, cloud IAM, etc.).\n\n**When would someone use it?**\n\n- Enforcing authentication/authorization at the edge or service mesh layer.\n- Implementing zero-trust networking where every request must be explicitly authorized.\n- Integrating with external policy engines (e.g., Open Policy Agent).\n- Adding authorization to services that lack their own auth implementation.\n\nThe filter operates at two layers:\n- **HTTP filter** (`envoy.filters.http.ext_authz`): Operates on HTTP requests with full access to headers, path, method, body, and can mutate request/response headers and query parameters based on the authorization response.\n- **Network filter** (`envoy.filters.network.ext_authz`): Operates at the TCP connection level, authorizing connections based on network attributes (source/destination addresses, TLS info) before any HTTP processing.\n\n## 2. Dependencies\n\n### Upstream Dependencies\n\n**Core Envoy Libraries:**\n- `source/common/http:codes_lib` / `envoy/http:codes_interface` - HTTP status code handling\n- `source/common/http:utility_lib` - HTTP utility functions\n- `source/common/http:headers_lib` - Header map manipulation\n- `source/common/http:async_client_lib` - HTTP async client for HTTP-mode authorization\n- `source/common/buffer:buffer_lib` - Request body buffering\n- `source/common/grpc:async_client_lib` / `source/common/grpc:typed_async_client_lib` - gRPC async client for gRPC-mode authorization\n- `source/common/router:config_lib` - Route configuration access\n- `source/common/runtime:runtime_protos_lib` - Runtime feature flags\n- `source/common/common:matchers_lib` - String/header matchers for filtering\n- `source/common/tracing:http_tracer_lib` - Distributed tracing integration\n- `source/common/network:utility_lib` - Network address utilities\n- `source/common/config:utility_lib` - Configuration parsing utilities\n- `source/common/protobuf:utility_lib` / `source/common/protobuf` - Protobuf support\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/mutation_rules:mutation_rules_lib` - Header mutation validation rules\n\n**Envoy Interfaces:**\n- `envoy/grpc:async_client_interface` / `envoy/grpc:async_client_manager_interface` - gRPC client abstractions\n- `envoy/http:filter_interface` - HTTP stream filter interface (`Http::StreamFilter`)\n- `envoy/http:header_map_interface` / `envoy/http:protocol_interface` - Header and protocol interfaces\n- `envoy/network:connection_interface` / `envoy/network:filter_interface` - Network filter interfaces (`Network::ReadFilter`)\n- `envoy/upstream:cluster_manager_interface` - Cluster manager for locating authz service clusters\n- `envoy/stats:stats_macros` - Statistics counter/gauge macros\n- `envoy/stream_info:stream_info_interface` - Per-stream metadata and info\n- `envoy/registry` - Extension registry for filter factory registration\n\n**Protobuf API Definitions:**\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - HTTP filter configuration proto\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Network filter configuration proto\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Authorization service protocol (CheckRequest/CheckResponse)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Core configuration types (GrpcService, HttpUri, etc.)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/type/matcher/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Matcher types for header/string matching\n\n**Internal Shared Libraries:**\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_interface` - Common `Client` interface, `Response` struct, `CheckStatus` enum\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_lib` - `GrpcClientImpl` for gRPC-based authz checks\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_lib` - `RawHttpClientImpl` for HTTP-based authz checks\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:check_request_utils_lib` - `CheckRequestUtils` for building CheckRequest protos\n\n### Downstream Consumers\n\n**Filter Registration:**\nThe ext_authz filters are registered as named extensions in Envoy's filter chain system:\n- HTTP filter name: `envoy.filters.http.ext_authz` (registered in `source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl`)\n- Network filter name: `envoy.filters.network.ext_authz` (registered in `source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl`)\n\n**How it is configured in Envoy's filter chain:**\n- The HTTP variant is added to `http_filters` in an `HttpConnectionManager` listener filter configuration.\n- The Network variant is added to `filters` in a listener's `filter_chains` configuration.\n- Both support per-route configuration overrides via `ExtAuthzPerRoute` (HTTP) to disable or customize the filter on specific routes.\n\n**Security posture:** `robust_to_untrusted_downstream` (stable status).\n\n**Integration points:**\n- Any Envoy deployment can use ext_authz by configuring it in the filter chain and pointing it at an authorization service that implements the `envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization` gRPC service or accepts HTTP check requests.\n- Downstream filters and access loggers can read dynamic metadata injected by ext_authz under the `envoy.filters.http.ext_authz` namespace.\n\n## 3. Relevant Components\n\n### HTTP Filter (`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/`)\n\n- **source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h**: Defines the core HTTP filter class `Filter` (extends `Http::StreamFilter`), configuration class `FilterConfig`, per-route configuration `FilterConfigPerRoute`, statistics struct `ExtAuthzFilterStats`, and `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` for per-stream access log metadata. Contains the filter's state machine and callback interfaces.\n- **source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc**: Main HTTP filter implementation. Handles `decodeHeaders()` (initiates authz check), `decodeData()` (buffers request body), request body buffering, `onComplete()` callback (processes authorization response with OK/Denied/Error paths), header/query-parameter mutation, dynamic metadata injection, and `sendLocalReply()` for denied/error responses.\n- **source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config.h**: Factory class `ExtAuthzFilterConfig` for creating HTTP ext_authz filter instances from protobuf configuration.\n- **source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config.cc**: Configuration parsing and factory implementation. Creates either a gRPC or HTTP client based on configuration, sets up timeouts, and handles per-route configuration factories.\n\n### Network Filter (`source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/`)\n\n- **source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h**: Defines the network filter class `Filter` (extends `Network::ReadFilter`), configuration class `Config`, and `InstanceStats` struct. Handles TCP-level authorization.\n- **source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc**: Network filter implementation. Handles `onNewConnection()` and `onData()` to trigger authorization checks, `onComplete()` callback for auth responses, connection close on denial/error, and dynamic metadata injection at the network layer.\n- **source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config.h**: Factory class `ExtAuthzConfigFactory` for network filter instances.\n- **source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config.cc**: Network filter configuration parsing and gRPC client creation.\n\n### Common/Shared Library (`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/`)\n\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h**: Defines the abstract `Client` interface (with `check()` and `cancel()` methods), `RequestCallbacks` interface, `Response` struct, `CheckStatus` enum (`OK`, `Denied`, `Error`), `ResponseCodeDetails` constants (`ext_authz_denied`, `ext_authz_error`, `ext_authz_invalid`), `TracingConstants`, and `HeaderValues` (e.g., `x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed`).\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h**: `GrpcClientImpl` class implementing the gRPC authorization client. Uses Envoy's typed async gRPC client to send `CheckRequest` messages to the `envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization` service.\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc**: gRPC client implementation. Builds and sends CheckRequest, parses CheckResponse on `onSuccess()` (extracting header mutations, query parameter changes, dynamic metadata), handles `onFailure()` for gRPC errors. Helper functions for copying response mutations (`copyHeaderMutations`, `copyQueryParameterMutations`).\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.h**: `RawHttpClientImpl` class implementing the HTTP authorization client, and `ClientConfig` for HTTP-specific configuration (allowed headers, path prefix, matchers).\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.cc**: HTTP client implementation. Constructs HTTP requests to the authz service, sends via async HTTP client, parses responses in `toResponse()` (200 = OK, 5xx = Error, anything else = Denied), applies header matchers, extracts dynamic metadata from response headers.\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h**: `CheckRequestUtils` static utility class with `createHttpCheck()` and `createTcpCheck()` for building `CheckRequest` protobuf messages from request/connection context. Also defines `Matcher` interface and implementations (`HeaderKeyMatcher`, `NotHeaderKeyMatcher`) for header filtering.\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.cc**: Implementation of check request construction. Populates `AttributeContext` with peer info (`setAttrContextPeer()`), HTTP request details (`setHttpRequest()`), TLS session info (`setTLSSession()`), request body, and filtered headers.\n\n### Protobuf Definitions\n\n- **api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto**: HTTP filter configuration: `ExtAuthz` message (main config with ~30 fields), `ExtAuthzPerRoute`, `CheckSettings`, `BufferSettings`, `HttpService`, `AuthorizationRequest`, `AuthorizationResponse`.\n- **api/envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto**: Network filter configuration proto.\n- **api/envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.proto**: Authorization gRPC service definition (`Authorization` service with `Check` RPC), `CheckRequest`, `CheckResponse`, `OkHttpResponse`, `DeniedHttpResponse`.\n- **api/envoy/service/auth/v3/attribute_context.proto**: `AttributeContext` message with nested `Peer`, `Request`, `HttpRequest`, and `TLSSession` types that carry full request context to the authorization service.\n\n## 4. Failure Modes\n\n### Authorization Service Unavailable\n\nWhen the external authorization service is unreachable (network failure, DNS resolution failure, service crash):\n- **gRPC mode**: The gRPC async client fires `onFailure()` with a status like `Unavailable`, resulting in `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- **HTTP mode**: The HTTP async client fires `onFailure()` with a `Reset` reason, resulting in `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- **Behavior depends on `failure_mode_allow` configuration**:\n  - `failure_mode_allow: false` (default, fail-closed): Request is rejected with the configured `status_on_error` HTTP code (default 403). Response code detail: `ext_authz_error`. Stream flag: `UnauthorizedExternalService`.\n  - `failure_mode_allow: true` (fail-open): Request is allowed through. The `failure_mode_allowed` stat is incremented. If `failure_mode_allow_header_add` is true, the header `x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true` is added to the upstream request so the backend knows authorization was not enforced.\n\n### Authorization Service Timeout\n\nWhen the authz service does not respond within the configured timeout:\n- **gRPC mode**: Results in a `DeadlineExceeded` gRPC status, which triggers `onFailure()` with `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- **HTTP mode**: The HTTP async client timeout triggers `onFailure()`, resulting in `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- The same `failure_mode_allow` logic applies as for service unavailability.\n\n### Authorization Explicitly Denied\n\nWhen the authorization service returns a denial:\n- **gRPC mode**: Any non-OK gRPC status in the `CheckResponse` results in `CheckStatus::Denied`. The response status code defaults to 403 but can be overridden by the `denied_response.status` field in the `CheckResponse`.\n- **HTTP mode**: Any non-200, non-5xx response from the authz service is treated as `CheckStatus::Denied`. The actual HTTP status code from the authz response is used as the denial status code.\n- The filter sends a local reply with the denial status code, the response body from the authz service (if any), and response headers specified in the authz response. Response code detail: `ext_authz_denied`. Stream flag: `UnauthorizedExternalService`.\n\n### Invalid Authorization Response\n\nWhen the authorization service returns a response with invalid header mutations or query parameter mutations (and `validate_mutations` is enabled):\n- The filter rejects the request with HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.\n- Response code detail: `ext_authz_invalid`.\n- The `invalid` stat counter is incremented.\n\n### HTTP Authorization Service Returns 5xx\n\nWhen the HTTP-based authorization service returns a 5xx status code:\n- Treated as `CheckStatus::Error` (not as a denial).\n- Same `failure_mode_allow` logic applies.\n\n### Authorization Cluster Not Found\n\nWhen the configured upstream cluster for the HTTP authz service does not exist:\n- The HTTP client logs `\"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\"` at debug level.\n- Results in `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- Same `failure_mode_allow` logic applies.\n\n### Filter Disabled with deny_at_disable\n\nWhen the filter is disabled (via runtime flag or metadata matcher) and `deny_at_disable` is configured:\n- The request is rejected with `status_on_error` (default 403).\n- Response code detail: `ext_authz_error`.\n- The `disabled` stat counter is incremented.\n\n### Network Filter Failure\n\nFor the network (TCP) filter:\n- On `CheckStatus::Denied` or `CheckStatus::Error` (with `failure_mode_allow: false`): The connection is closed.\n- On `CheckStatus::Error` with `failure_mode_allow: true`: Data continues to flow.\n\n### Request Body Too Large\n\nWhen the request body exceeds `max_request_bytes` in `BufferSettings`:\n- If `allow_partial_message` is true, the check is sent with a truncated body and a flag indicating the body is partial.\n- If `allow_partial_message` is false, the check is sent once the buffer limit is hit, potentially with an incomplete body.\n\n## 5. Testing\n\n### Unit Tests\n\n**HTTP Filter Unit Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc**: 80+ test cases covering the full HTTP filter behavior. Test fixture: `HttpFilterTest` / `HttpFilterTestParam`. Covers: configuration merging, stats collection, error handling (fail-close, fail-open, custom status codes), request data buffering, header-only requests, WebSocket/HTTP2 upgrades, route-based configuration, header manipulation (add/append/remove/set), dynamic metadata ingestion, query parameter mutations, response header handling, filter enable/disable, deny-at-disable, and header mutation rule validation.\n\n**HTTP Filter Config Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config_test.cc**: Factory and configuration validation tests. Test classes: `ExtAuthzFilterTest`, `ExtAuthzFilterHttpTest`, `ExtAuthzFilterGrpcTest`. Covers HTTP service factory, gRPC service factory, Envoy gRPC and Google gRPC client creation.\n\n**Network Filter Unit Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc**: 13 tests covering network filter behavior. Test fixture: `ExtAuthzFilterTest`. Covers: bad configuration, OK/Denied/Error responses with onData, fail-open/fail-close, connection closure handling, immediate responses, and filter enable/disable with metadata.\n\n**Network Filter Config Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config_test.cc**: Configuration validation tests for the network filter.\n\n**Common Library Unit Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl_test.cc**: 17 tests for the gRPC client. Test fixture: `ExtAuthzGrpcClientTest`. Covers: authorization OK, all attributes, invalid headers handling, denial with various statuses, error handling, cancellation, timeout, dynamic metadata, query parameters, and append actions.\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl_test.cc**: 20+ tests for the HTTP client. Test fixture: `ExtAuthzHttpClientTest`. Covers: client configuration, path prefix handling, path rewrite, raw body handling, header manipulation on OK/denied, dynamic metadata, 5xx error handling, request cancellation, and no-cluster scenarios.\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils_test.cc**: 24 tests for CheckRequest construction. Test fixture: `CheckRequestUtilsTest`. Covers: TCP/HTTP request building, peer certificate handling, TLS session info, header allowlists/denylists, partial/full body inclusion, headers-as-bytes encoding, and attribute context population.\n\n### Integration Tests\n\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc**: ~1800 lines of end-to-end integration tests with real gRPC and HTTP authorization servers. Test classes:\n  - `ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest`: 29 parameterized tests covering HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 downstream requests, large bodies (2MB+), header mutations to upstream, allow/deny at disable, metadata matchers, buffering, timeout handling (fail-close/fail-open), response retries on 5xx, response mutation validation, and failure_mode_allow with non-UTF8 data.\n  - `ExtAuthzHttpIntegrationTest`: Tests for direct response, redirect response, case-sensitive matching, forwarded-for headers, body handling, error responses with buffer limits, timeout handling.\n  - `ExtAuthzLocalReplyIntegrationTest`: 2 tests for local reply behavior.\n- **Test data**: `test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.yaml` provides integration test configuration.\n\n### Fuzz Tests\n\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_fuzz_test.cc**: Fuzz test for gRPC-based HTTP filter. Corpus: `ext_authz_grpc_corpus/` (11 seed files including bad_config, custom_status, denied_response, error_fail_close, invalid_headers_to_remove, metadata_context, etc.).\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_fuzz_test.cc**: Fuzz test for HTTP-based HTTP filter. Corpus: `ext_authz_http_corpus/` (9 seed files).\n- **test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_test.cc**: Fuzz test for network filter. Corpus: `ext_authz_corpus/` (3 seed files including two crash reproductions).\n- **Fuzz support libraries**: `test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_lib.h/.cc` and fuzz proto definitions (`ext_authz_fuzz.proto` for both HTTP and network).\n\n### Test Utilities\n\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/mocks.h / mocks.cc**: `MockClient` and `MockRequestCallbacks` for stubbing the authorization client in unit tests.\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/test_common.h / test_common.cc**: `TestCommon` utility class with helper methods (`makeMessageResponse()`, `makeCheckResponse()`, `makeAuthzResponse()`, `makeHeaderValueOption()`) and custom gtest matchers (`AuthzErrorResponse`, `AuthzDeniedResponse`, `AuthzOkResponse`, `ContainsPairAsHeader`).\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/logging_test_filter.cc/.proto**: Test filter used in integration tests to verify `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` filter state statistics.\n\n### How to Run Tests\n\nTests are built and run via Bazel. Scoped examples:\n```bash\n# HTTP filter unit tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_test\n\n# HTTP filter config tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:config_test\n\n# HTTP filter integration tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_integration_test\n\n# Network filter unit tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz:ext_authz_test\n\n# Common gRPC client tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_impl_test\n\n# Common HTTP client tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_impl_test\n\n# Check request utils tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:check_request_utils_test\n```\n\n## 6. Debugging\n\n### Statistics (Metrics)\n\nAll ext_authz stats are prefixed with `ext_authz.<stat_prefix>.` where `stat_prefix` is configured in the filter config.\n\n**HTTP filter counters:**\n| Stat Name | Meaning |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.ok` | Requests authorized successfully |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.denied` | Requests explicitly denied by authz service |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.error` | Authorization service errors (timeout, unavailable, 5xx) |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.disabled` | Requests where the filter was disabled |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.failure_mode_allowed` | Errors allowed through due to `failure_mode_allow: true` |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.invalid` | Invalid authorization responses (bad header mutations) |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.ignored_dynamic_metadata` | Dynamic metadata ignored (ingestion disabled) |\n\n**Network filter counters:**\n| Stat Name | Meaning |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.total` | Total authorization checks |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.ok` | Authorized connections |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.denied` | Denied connections |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.error` | Authorization errors |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.disabled` | Disabled filter invocations |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.cx_closed` | Connections closed due to authz failure |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.failure_mode_allowed` | Errors allowed through |\n\n**Network filter gauge:**\n| Stat Name | Meaning |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.active` | Currently in-flight authorization checks |\n\n**Cluster-scoped stats** (when `charge_cluster_response_stats` is enabled): `ext_authz_ok_`, `ext_authz_denied_`, `ext_authz_error_`, `ext_authz_invalid_`, `ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_` are charged to the upstream authz cluster stats.\n\n**What to check first:** A sudden spike in `error` or `failure_mode_allowed` indicates the authorization service is unhealthy. A spike in `denied` may indicate a policy change or misconfiguration. The `invalid` counter indicates the authz service is returning malformed responses.\n\n### Logging\n\nThe filter uses Envoy's standard logging framework. Set the log level for `ext_authz` logger to see detailed output.\n\n**Key log messages (by level):**\n\n- **trace**: `\"Sending CheckRequest: {}\"` - Full serialized CheckRequest proto (in gRPC impl). `\"Received CheckResponse: {}\"` - Full serialized CheckResponse proto. Per-header mutation details (each header being set/added/appended/removed). `\"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\"` - Auth check initiation. `\"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\"` - Fail-open triggered. `\"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error\"` - Fail-closed triggered.\n- **debug**: `\"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\"` - Authz cluster missing. `\"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\"` / `\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {buffer is full | stream is ended}\"` - Body buffering lifecycle.\n\n**How to enable:** Set `--log-level ext_authz:trace` on the Envoy command line, or use the admin `/logging?ext_authz=trace` endpoint at runtime.\n\n### Distributed Tracing\n\nThe filter integrates with Envoy's tracing subsystem and adds tags to trace spans:\n\n| Trace Tag | Values | Meaning |\n|-----------|--------|---------|\n| `ext_authz_status` | `ext_authz_ok` or `ext_authz_unauthorized` | Authorization result |\n| `ext_authz_http_status` | HTTP status code string (e.g., `\"200\"`, `\"403\"`) | HTTP authz service response code (HTTP client only) |\n\nThe authorization check itself appears as a child span named `\"async <cluster_name> egress\"` under the parent request span. This lets you see authz latency in your tracing backend.\n\n### Response Code Details (Access Logs)\n\nThe filter sets `response_code_details` in stream info, which appears in access logs:\n\n| Detail String | Meaning |\n|---------------|---------|\n| `ext_authz_denied` | Request explicitly denied by authorization service |\n| `ext_authz_error` | Authorization service error (fail-closed) |\n| `ext_authz_invalid` | Invalid authorization response (bad mutations) |\n\nAdditionally, the `UnauthorizedExternalService` response flag is set for all denied/error responses, visible in access logs as the `%RESPONSE_FLAGS%` field (flag: `UAEX`).\n\n### Dynamic Metadata\n\nThe authz service can inject dynamic metadata into the request's stream info under the namespace `envoy.filters.http.ext_authz` (HTTP) or `envoy.filters.network.ext_authz` (network). This metadata is accessible in access log format strings via `%DYNAMIC_METADATA(envoy.filters.http.ext_authz:key)%`.\n\nThe filter automatically adds `ext_authz_duration` (in milliseconds) to dynamic metadata when the authorization check succeeds, providing a built-in latency measurement.\n\n### Filter State (ExtAuthzLoggingInfo)\n\nWhen `emit_filter_state_stats` is enabled, the HTTP filter stores an `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` object in filter state containing:\n- `latency()` - Authorization check latency (microseconds)\n- `bytesSent()` - Bytes sent to authz service (gRPC only)\n- `bytesReceived()` - Bytes received from authz service (gRPC only)\n- `clusterInfo()` - Upstream authz cluster information\n- `upstreamHost()` - Specific upstream host used for the authz check\n\nThis data can be accessed by custom access log formatters or downstream filters for advanced observability.\n\n### Debugging Checklist\n\n1. **Check stats**: Look at `ext_authz.<prefix>.error` and `ext_authz.<prefix>.failure_mode_allowed` for service health issues.\n2. **Check access logs**: Look for `ext_authz_denied`, `ext_authz_error`, or `ext_authz_invalid` in `%RESPONSE_CODE_DETAILS%` and `UAEX` in `%RESPONSE_FLAGS%`.\n3. **Enable trace logging**: Set `ext_authz:trace` to see full CheckRequest/CheckResponse payloads.\n4. **Check tracing**: Look for `ext_authz_status` span tags and the authz child span latency.\n5. **Verify cluster health**: Check the authz service cluster stats and health check status via Envoy admin (`/clusters`).\n6. **Check dynamic metadata**: Use `%DYNAMIC_METADATA(envoy.filters.http.ext_authz:ext_authz_duration)%` in access logs to monitor authz latency.\n7. **Verify configuration**: Ensure the authz cluster exists, timeouts are reasonable, and `failure_mode_allow` is set appropriately for your use case.\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/onboarding.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# ext_authz Filter Handoff Document\n\n## 1. Purpose\n\nThe ext_authz filter is an external authorization filter for Envoy proxy that delegates authorization decisions to an external service. Rather than embedding authorization logic directly in Envoy, it sends check requests to a separate authorization server and acts on the response (allow, deny, or error).\n\n**What problems does it solve?**\n\n- **Centralized authorization**: Decouples authorization logic from the proxy, allowing a single authorization service to enforce policies across an entire fleet of Envoy proxies.\n- **Fine-grained access control**: The authorization service receives rich request context (headers, path, client certificate, peer info, request body) and can make nuanced authorization decisions.\n- **Dynamic policy updates**: Authorization policies can be updated in the external service without redeploying Envoy.\n- **Protocol flexibility**: Supports both gRPC and HTTP protocols for communicating with the authorization service, enabling integration with a wide variety of auth backends (OPA, custom services, cloud IAM, etc.).\n\n**When would someone use it?**\n\n- Enforcing authentication/authorization at the edge or service mesh layer.\n- Implementing zero-trust networking where every request must be explicitly authorized.\n- Integrating with external policy engines (e.g., Open Policy Agent).\n- Adding authorization to services that lack their own auth implementation.\n\nThe filter operates at two layers:\n- **HTTP filter** (`envoy.filters.http.ext_authz`): Operates on HTTP requests with full access to headers, path, method, body, and can mutate request/response headers and query parameters based on the authorization response.\n- **Network filter** (`envoy.filters.network.ext_authz`): Operates at the TCP connection level, authorizing connections based on network attributes (source/destination addresses, TLS info) before any HTTP processing.\n\n## 2. Dependencies\n\n### Upstream Dependencies\n\n**Core Envoy Libraries:**\n- `source/common/http:codes_lib` / `envoy/http:codes_interface` - HTTP status code handling\n- `source/common/http:utility_lib` - HTTP utility functions\n- `source/common/http:headers_lib` - Header map manipulation\n- `source/common/http:async_client_lib` - HTTP async client for HTTP-mode authorization\n- `source/common/buffer:buffer_lib` - Request body buffering\n- `source/common/grpc:async_client_lib` / `source/common/grpc:typed_async_client_lib` - gRPC async client for gRPC-mode authorization\n- `source/common/router:config_lib` - Route configuration access\n- `source/common/runtime:runtime_protos_lib` - Runtime feature flags\n- `source/common/common:matchers_lib` - String/header matchers for filtering\n- `source/common/tracing:http_tracer_lib` - Distributed tracing integration\n- `source/common/network:utility_lib` - Network address utilities\n- `source/common/config:utility_lib` - Configuration parsing utilities\n- `source/common/protobuf:utility_lib` / `source/common/protobuf` - Protobuf support\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/mutation_rules:mutation_rules_lib` - Header mutation validation rules\n\n**Envoy Interfaces:**\n- `envoy/grpc:async_client_interface` / `envoy/grpc:async_client_manager_interface` - gRPC client abstractions\n- `envoy/http:filter_interface` - HTTP stream filter interface (`Http::StreamFilter`)\n- `envoy/http:header_map_interface` / `envoy/http:protocol_interface` - Header and protocol interfaces\n- `envoy/network:connection_interface` / `envoy/network:filter_interface` - Network filter interfaces (`Network::ReadFilter`)\n- `envoy/upstream:cluster_manager_interface` - Cluster manager for locating authz service clusters\n- `envoy/stats:stats_macros` - Statistics counter/gauge macros\n- `envoy/stream_info:stream_info_interface` - Per-stream metadata and info\n- `envoy/registry` - Extension registry for filter factory registration\n\n**Protobuf API Definitions:**\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - HTTP filter configuration proto\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Network filter configuration proto\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/service/auth/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Authorization service protocol (CheckRequest/CheckResponse)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/config/core/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Core configuration types (GrpcService, HttpUri, etc.)\n- `@envoy_api//envoy/type/matcher/v3:pkg_cc_proto` - Matcher types for header/string matching\n\n**Internal Shared Libraries:**\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_interface` - Common `Client` interface, `Response` struct, `CheckStatus` enum\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_lib` - `GrpcClientImpl` for gRPC-based authz checks\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_lib` - `RawHttpClientImpl` for HTTP-based authz checks\n- `source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:check_request_utils_lib` - `CheckRequestUtils` for building CheckRequest protos\n\n### Downstream Consumers\n\n**Filter Registration:**\nThe ext_authz filters are registered as named extensions in Envoy's filter chain system:\n- HTTP filter name: `envoy.filters.http.ext_authz` (registered in `source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl`)\n- Network filter name: `envoy.filters.network.ext_authz` (registered in `source/extensions/extensions_build_config.bzl`)\n\n**How it is configured in Envoy's filter chain:**\n- The HTTP variant is added to `http_filters` in an `HttpConnectionManager` listener filter configuration.\n- The Network variant is added to `filters` in a listener's `filter_chains` configuration.\n- Both support per-route configuration overrides via `ExtAuthzPerRoute` (HTTP) to disable or customize the filter on specific routes.\n\n**Security posture:** `robust_to_untrusted_downstream` (stable status).\n\n**Integration points:**\n- Any Envoy deployment can use ext_authz by configuring it in the filter chain and pointing it at an authorization service that implements the `envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization` gRPC service or accepts HTTP check requests.\n- Downstream filters and access loggers can read dynamic metadata injected by ext_authz under the `envoy.filters.http.ext_authz` namespace.\n\n## 3. Relevant Components\n\n### HTTP Filter (`source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/`)\n\n- **source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h**: Defines the core HTTP filter class `Filter` (extends `Http::StreamFilter`), configuration class `FilterConfig`, per-route configuration `FilterConfigPerRoute`, statistics struct `ExtAuthzFilterStats`, and `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` for per-stream access log metadata. Contains the filter's state machine and callback interfaces.\n- **source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc**: Main HTTP filter implementation. Handles `decodeHeaders()` (initiates authz check), `decodeData()` (buffers request body), request body buffering, `onComplete()` callback (processes authorization response with OK/Denied/Error paths), header/query-parameter mutation, dynamic metadata injection, and `sendLocalReply()` for denied/error responses.\n- **source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config.h**: Factory class `ExtAuthzFilterConfig` for creating HTTP ext_authz filter instances from protobuf configuration.\n- **source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config.cc**: Configuration parsing and factory implementation. Creates either a gRPC or HTTP client based on configuration, sets up timeouts, and handles per-route configuration factories.\n\n### Network Filter (`source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/`)\n\n- **source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h**: Defines the network filter class `Filter` (extends `Network::ReadFilter`), configuration class `Config`, and `InstanceStats` struct. Handles TCP-level authorization.\n- **source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc**: Network filter implementation. Handles `onNewConnection()` and `onData()` to trigger authorization checks, `onComplete()` callback for auth responses, connection close on denial/error, and dynamic metadata injection at the network layer.\n- **source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config.h**: Factory class `ExtAuthzConfigFactory` for network filter instances.\n- **source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config.cc**: Network filter configuration parsing and gRPC client creation.\n\n### Common/Shared Library (`source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/`)\n\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h**: Defines the abstract `Client` interface (with `check()` and `cancel()` methods), `RequestCallbacks` interface, `Response` struct, `CheckStatus` enum (`OK`, `Denied`, `Error`), `ResponseCodeDetails` constants (`ext_authz_denied`, `ext_authz_error`, `ext_authz_invalid`), `TracingConstants`, and `HeaderValues` (e.g., `x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed`).\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.h**: `GrpcClientImpl` class implementing the gRPC authorization client. Uses Envoy's typed async gRPC client to send `CheckRequest` messages to the `envoy.service.auth.v3.Authorization` service.\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl.cc**: gRPC client implementation. Builds and sends CheckRequest, parses CheckResponse on `onSuccess()` (extracting header mutations, query parameter changes, dynamic metadata), handles `onFailure()` for gRPC errors. Helper functions for copying response mutations (`copyHeaderMutations`, `copyQueryParameterMutations`).\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.h**: `RawHttpClientImpl` class implementing the HTTP authorization client, and `ClientConfig` for HTTP-specific configuration (allowed headers, path prefix, matchers).\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.cc**: HTTP client implementation. Constructs HTTP requests to the authz service, sends via async HTTP client, parses responses in `toResponse()` (200 = OK, 5xx = Error, anything else = Denied), applies header matchers, extracts dynamic metadata from response headers.\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.h**: `CheckRequestUtils` static utility class with `createHttpCheck()` and `createTcpCheck()` for building `CheckRequest` protobuf messages from request/connection context. Also defines `Matcher` interface and implementations (`HeaderKeyMatcher`, `NotHeaderKeyMatcher`) for header filtering.\n- **source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils.cc**: Implementation of check request construction. Populates `AttributeContext` with peer info (`setAttrContextPeer()`), HTTP request details (`setHttpRequest()`), TLS session info (`setTLSSession()`), request body, and filtered headers.\n\n### Protobuf Definitions\n\n- **api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto**: HTTP filter configuration: `ExtAuthz` message (main config with ~30 fields), `ExtAuthzPerRoute`, `CheckSettings`, `BufferSettings`, `HttpService`, `AuthorizationRequest`, `AuthorizationResponse`.\n- **api/envoy/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/v3/ext_authz.proto**: Network filter configuration proto.\n- **api/envoy/service/auth/v3/external_auth.proto**: Authorization gRPC service definition (`Authorization` service with `Check` RPC), `CheckRequest`, `CheckResponse`, `OkHttpResponse`, `DeniedHttpResponse`.\n- **api/envoy/service/auth/v3/attribute_context.proto**: `AttributeContext` message with nested `Peer`, `Request`, `HttpRequest`, and `TLSSession` types that carry full request context to the authorization service.\n\n## 4. Failure Modes\n\n### Authorization Service Unavailable\n\nWhen the external authorization service is unreachable (network failure, DNS resolution failure, service crash):\n- **gRPC mode**: The gRPC async client fires `onFailure()` with a status like `Unavailable`, resulting in `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- **HTTP mode**: The HTTP async client fires `onFailure()` with a `Reset` reason, resulting in `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- **Behavior depends on `failure_mode_allow` configuration**:\n  - `failure_mode_allow: false` (default, fail-closed): Request is rejected with the configured `status_on_error` HTTP code (default 403). Response code detail: `ext_authz_error`. Stream flag: `UnauthorizedExternalService`.\n  - `failure_mode_allow: true` (fail-open): Request is allowed through. The `failure_mode_allowed` stat is incremented. If `failure_mode_allow_header_add` is true, the header `x-envoy-auth-failure-mode-allowed: true` is added to the upstream request so the backend knows authorization was not enforced.\n\n### Authorization Service Timeout\n\nWhen the authz service does not respond within the configured timeout:\n- **gRPC mode**: Results in a `DeadlineExceeded` gRPC status, which triggers `onFailure()` with `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- **HTTP mode**: The HTTP async client timeout triggers `onFailure()`, resulting in `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- The same `failure_mode_allow` logic applies as for service unavailability.\n\n### Authorization Explicitly Denied\n\nWhen the authorization service returns a denial:\n- **gRPC mode**: Any non-OK gRPC status in the `CheckResponse` results in `CheckStatus::Denied`. The response status code defaults to 403 but can be overridden by the `denied_response.status` field in the `CheckResponse`.\n- **HTTP mode**: Any non-200, non-5xx response from the authz service is treated as `CheckStatus::Denied`. The actual HTTP status code from the authz response is used as the denial status code.\n- The filter sends a local reply with the denial status code, the response body from the authz service (if any), and response headers specified in the authz response. Response code detail: `ext_authz_denied`. Stream flag: `UnauthorizedExternalService`.\n\n### Invalid Authorization Response\n\nWhen the authorization service returns a response with invalid header mutations or query parameter mutations (and `validate_mutations` is enabled):\n- The filter rejects the request with HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.\n- Response code detail: `ext_authz_invalid`.\n- The `invalid` stat counter is incremented.\n\n### HTTP Authorization Service Returns 5xx\n\nWhen the HTTP-based authorization service returns a 5xx status code:\n- Treated as `CheckStatus::Error` (not as a denial).\n- Same `failure_mode_allow` logic applies.\n\n### Authorization Cluster Not Found\n\nWhen the configured upstream cluster for the HTTP authz service does not exist:\n- The HTTP client logs `\"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\"` at debug level.\n- Results in `CheckStatus::Error`.\n- Same `failure_mode_allow` logic applies.\n\n### Filter Disabled with deny_at_disable\n\nWhen the filter is disabled (via runtime flag or metadata matcher) and `deny_at_disable` is configured:\n- The request is rejected with `status_on_error` (default 403).\n- Response code detail: `ext_authz_error`.\n- The `disabled` stat counter is incremented.\n\n### Network Filter Failure\n\nFor the network (TCP) filter:\n- On `CheckStatus::Denied` or `CheckStatus::Error` (with `failure_mode_allow: false`): The connection is closed.\n- On `CheckStatus::Error` with `failure_mode_allow: true`: Data continues to flow.\n\n### Request Body Too Large\n\nWhen the request body exceeds `max_request_bytes` in `BufferSettings`:\n- If `allow_partial_message` is true, the check is sent with a truncated body and a flag indicating the body is partial.\n- If `allow_partial_message` is false, the check is sent once the buffer limit is hit, potentially with an incomplete body.\n\n## 5. Testing\n\n### Unit Tests\n\n**HTTP Filter Unit Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc**: 80+ test cases covering the full HTTP filter behavior. Test fixture: `HttpFilterTest` / `HttpFilterTestParam`. Covers: configuration merging, stats collection, error handling (fail-close, fail-open, custom status codes), request data buffering, header-only requests, WebSocket/HTTP2 upgrades, route-based configuration, header manipulation (add/append/remove/set), dynamic metadata ingestion, query parameter mutations, response header handling, filter enable/disable, deny-at-disable, and header mutation rule validation.\n\n**HTTP Filter Config Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config_test.cc**: Factory and configuration validation tests. Test classes: `ExtAuthzFilterTest`, `ExtAuthzFilterHttpTest`, `ExtAuthzFilterGrpcTest`. Covers HTTP service factory, gRPC service factory, Envoy gRPC and Google gRPC client creation.\n\n**Network Filter Unit Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc**: 13 tests covering network filter behavior. Test fixture: `ExtAuthzFilterTest`. Covers: bad configuration, OK/Denied/Error responses with onData, fail-open/fail-close, connection closure handling, immediate responses, and filter enable/disable with metadata.\n\n**Network Filter Config Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/config_test.cc**: Configuration validation tests for the network filter.\n\n**Common Library Unit Tests:**\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl_test.cc**: 17 tests for the gRPC client. Test fixture: `ExtAuthzGrpcClientTest`. Covers: authorization OK, all attributes, invalid headers handling, denial with various statuses, error handling, cancellation, timeout, dynamic metadata, query parameters, and append actions.\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl_test.cc**: 20+ tests for the HTTP client. Test fixture: `ExtAuthzHttpClientTest`. Covers: client configuration, path prefix handling, path rewrite, raw body handling, header manipulation on OK/denied, dynamic metadata, 5xx error handling, request cancellation, and no-cluster scenarios.\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/check_request_utils_test.cc**: 24 tests for CheckRequest construction. Test fixture: `CheckRequestUtilsTest`. Covers: TCP/HTTP request building, peer certificate handling, TLS session info, header allowlists/denylists, partial/full body inclusion, headers-as-bytes encoding, and attribute context population.\n\n### Integration Tests\n\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc**: ~1800 lines of end-to-end integration tests with real gRPC and HTTP authorization servers. Test classes:\n  - `ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest`: 29 parameterized tests covering HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 downstream requests, large bodies (2MB+), header mutations to upstream, allow/deny at disable, metadata matchers, buffering, timeout handling (fail-close/fail-open), response retries on 5xx, response mutation validation, and failure_mode_allow with non-UTF8 data.\n  - `ExtAuthzHttpIntegrationTest`: Tests for direct response, redirect response, case-sensitive matching, forwarded-for headers, body handling, error responses with buffer limits, timeout handling.\n  - `ExtAuthzLocalReplyIntegrationTest`: 2 tests for local reply behavior.\n- **Test data**: `test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.yaml` provides integration test configuration.\n\n### Fuzz Tests\n\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_fuzz_test.cc**: Fuzz test for gRPC-based HTTP filter. Corpus: `ext_authz_grpc_corpus/` (11 seed files including bad_config, custom_status, denied_response, error_fail_close, invalid_headers_to_remove, metadata_context, etc.).\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_fuzz_test.cc**: Fuzz test for HTTP-based HTTP filter. Corpus: `ext_authz_http_corpus/` (9 seed files).\n- **test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_test.cc**: Fuzz test for network filter. Corpus: `ext_authz_corpus/` (3 seed files including two crash reproductions).\n- **Fuzz support libraries**: `test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_fuzz_lib.h/.cc` and fuzz proto definitions (`ext_authz_fuzz.proto` for both HTTP and network).\n\n### Test Utilities\n\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/mocks.h / mocks.cc**: `MockClient` and `MockRequestCallbacks` for stubbing the authorization client in unit tests.\n- **test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/test_common.h / test_common.cc**: `TestCommon` utility class with helper methods (`makeMessageResponse()`, `makeCheckResponse()`, `makeAuthzResponse()`, `makeHeaderValueOption()`) and custom gtest matchers (`AuthzErrorResponse`, `AuthzDeniedResponse`, `AuthzOkResponse`, `ContainsPairAsHeader`).\n- **test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/logging_test_filter.cc/.proto**: Test filter used in integration tests to verify `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` filter state statistics.\n\n### How to Run Tests\n\nTests are built and run via Bazel. Scoped examples:\n```bash\n# HTTP filter unit tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_test\n\n# HTTP filter config tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:config_test\n\n# HTTP filter integration tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz:ext_authz_integration_test\n\n# Network filter unit tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz:ext_authz_test\n\n# Common gRPC client tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_grpc_impl_test\n\n# Common HTTP client tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:ext_authz_http_impl_test\n\n# Check request utils tests\nbazel test //test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz:check_request_utils_test\n```\n\n## 6. Debugging\n\n### Statistics (Metrics)\n\nAll ext_authz stats are prefixed with `ext_authz.<stat_prefix>.` where `stat_prefix` is configured in the filter config.\n\n**HTTP filter counters:**\n| Stat Name | Meaning |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.ok` | Requests authorized successfully |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.denied` | Requests explicitly denied by authz service |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.error` | Authorization service errors (timeout, unavailable, 5xx) |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.disabled` | Requests where the filter was disabled |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.failure_mode_allowed` | Errors allowed through due to `failure_mode_allow: true` |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.invalid` | Invalid authorization responses (bad header mutations) |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.ignored_dynamic_metadata` | Dynamic metadata ignored (ingestion disabled) |\n\n**Network filter counters:**\n| Stat Name | Meaning |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.total` | Total authorization checks |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.ok` | Authorized connections |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.denied` | Denied connections |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.error` | Authorization errors |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.disabled` | Disabled filter invocations |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.cx_closed` | Connections closed due to authz failure |\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.failure_mode_allowed` | Errors allowed through |\n\n**Network filter gauge:**\n| Stat Name | Meaning |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `ext_authz.<prefix>.active` | Currently in-flight authorization checks |\n\n**Cluster-scoped stats** (when `charge_cluster_response_stats` is enabled): `ext_authz_ok_`, `ext_authz_denied_`, `ext_authz_error_`, `ext_authz_invalid_`, `ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_` are charged to the upstream authz cluster stats.\n\n**What to check first:** A sudden spike in `error` or `failure_mode_allowed` indicates the authorization service is unhealthy. A spike in `denied` may indicate a policy change or misconfiguration. The `invalid` counter indicates the authz service is returning malformed responses.\n\n### Logging\n\nThe filter uses Envoy's standard logging framework. Set the log level for `ext_authz` logger to see detailed output.\n\n**Key log messages (by level):**\n\n- **trace**: `\"Sending CheckRequest: {}\"` - Full serialized CheckRequest proto (in gRPC impl). `\"Received CheckResponse: {}\"` - Full serialized CheckResponse proto. Per-header mutation details (each header being set/added/appended/removed). `\"ext_authz filter calling authorization server\"` - Auth check initiation. `\"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\"` - Fail-open triggered. `\"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error\"` - Fail-closed triggered.\n- **debug**: `\"ext_authz cluster '{}' does not exist\"` - Authz cluster missing. `\"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\"` / `\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {buffer is full | stream is ended}\"` - Body buffering lifecycle.\n\n**How to enable:** Set `--log-level ext_authz:trace` on the Envoy command line, or use the admin `/logging?ext_authz=trace` endpoint at runtime.\n\n### Distributed Tracing\n\nThe filter integrates with Envoy's tracing subsystem and adds tags to trace spans:\n\n| Trace Tag | Values | Meaning |\n|-----------|--------|---------|\n| `ext_authz_status` | `ext_authz_ok` or `ext_authz_unauthorized` | Authorization result |\n| `ext_authz_http_status` | HTTP status code string (e.g., `\"200\"`, `\"403\"`) | HTTP authz service response code (HTTP client only) |\n\nThe authorization check itself appears as a child span named `\"async <cluster_name> egress\"` under the parent request span. This lets you see authz latency in your tracing backend.\n\n### Response Code Details (Access Logs)\n\nThe filter sets `response_code_details` in stream info, which appears in access logs:\n\n| Detail String | Meaning |\n|---------------|---------|\n| `ext_authz_denied` | Request explicitly denied by authorization service |\n| `ext_authz_error` | Authorization service error (fail-closed) |\n| `ext_authz_invalid` | Invalid authorization response (bad mutations) |\n\nAdditionally, the `UnauthorizedExternalService` response flag is set for all denied/error responses, visible in access logs as the `%RESPONSE_FLAGS%` field (flag: `UAEX`).\n\n### Dynamic Metadata\n\nThe authz service can inject dynamic metadata into the request's stream info under the namespace `envoy.filters.http.ext_authz` (HTTP) or `envoy.filters.network.ext_authz` (network). This metadata is accessible in access log format strings via `%DYNAMIC_METADATA(envoy.filters.http.ext_authz:key)%`.\n\nThe filter automatically adds `ext_authz_duration` (in milliseconds) to dynamic metadata when the authorization check succeeds, providing a built-in latency measurement.\n\n### Filter State (ExtAuthzLoggingInfo)\n\nWhen `emit_filter_state_stats` is enabled, the HTTP filter stores an `ExtAuthzLoggingInfo` object in filter state containing:\n- `latency()` - Authorization check latency (microseconds)\n- `bytesSent()` - Bytes sent to authz service (gRPC only)\n- `bytesReceived()` - Bytes received from authz service (gRPC only)\n- `clusterInfo()` - Upstream authz cluster information\n- `upstreamHost()` - Specific upstream host used for the authz check\n\nThis data can be accessed by custom access log formatters or downstream filters for advanced observability.\n\n### Debugging Checklist\n\n1. **Check stats**: Look at `ext_authz.<prefix>.error` and `ext_authz.<prefix>.failure_mode_allowed` for service health issues.\n2. **Check access logs**: Look for `ext_authz_denied`, `ext_authz_error`, or `ext_authz_invalid` in `%RESPONSE_CODE_DETAILS%` and `UAEX` in `%RESPONSE_FLAGS%`.\n3. **Enable trace logging**: Set `ext_authz:trace` to see full CheckRequest/CheckResponse payloads.\n4. **Check tracing**: Look for `ext_authz_status` span tags and the authz child span latency.\n5. **Verify cluster health**: Check the authz service cluster stats and health check status via Envoy admin (`/clusters`).\n6. **Check dynamic metadata**: Use `%DYNAMIC_METADATA(envoy.filters.http.ext_authz:ext_authz_duration)%` in access logs to monitor authz latency.\n7. **Verify configuration**: Ensure the authz cluster exists, timeouts are reasonable, and `failure_mode_allow` is set appropriately for your use case.\n",
  "filePath": "/logs/agent/onboarding.md",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

        Code Changes (1)
        
1. WRITE /logs/agent/onboarding.md
# ext_authz Filter Handoff Document

## 1. Purpose

The ext_authz filter is an external authorization filter for Envoy proxy that delegates authorization decisions to an external service. Rather than embedding authorization logic directly in Envoy, it sends check requests to a separate authorization server and acts on the response (allow, deny, or error).

**What problems does it solve?**

- **Centralized authorization**: Decouples authorization logic from the proxy, allowing a single authorization service to enforce policies across an entire fleet of Envoy proxies.
- **Fine-grained access control**: The authorization service receives rich request context (headers, path, client certificate, peer info, request body) and can make nuanced authorization decisions.
- **Dynamic policy updates**: Authorization policies can be updated in the external service without redeploying Envoy.
- **Protocol flexibility**: Supports both gRPC and HTTP protocols for communicating with the authorization service, enabling integration with a wide variety of auth backends (OPA, custom services, cloud IAM, etc.).

**When would someone use it?**

- Enforcing authentication/authorization at the edge or service mesh layer.
- Implementing zero-trust networking where every request must be explicitly authorized.
- Integrating with external policy engines (e.g., Open Policy Agent).
- Adding authorization to services that lack their own auth implementation.

The filter operates at two layers:
- **HTTP filter** (`envoy.filters.http.ext_authz`): Operates on HTTP requests with full access to headers, path, method, body, and can mutate request/response headers and query parameters based on the authorization response.
- **Network filter** (`envoy.filters.network.ext_authz`): Operates at the TCP connection le
... (truncated)

1. $ find /workspace -path "*ext_authz*" -name "BUILD" -o -path "*ext_authz*" -name "*.bzl" 2>/dev/null | head -20
2. $ find /workspace/api/envoy/service/auth -name "*.proto" -type f 2>/dev/null
3. $ find /workspace/api/envoy -path "*ext_authz*" -name "*.proto" -type f 2>/dev/null
4. $ find /workspace/test/integration -name "*ext_authz*" -type f
5. $ grep -r "ext_authz" /workspace/test/integration --include="*.cc" --include="*.h" -l | head -20
6. $ grep -n "class.*Test\|^TEST.*(\|^TEST_P.*(" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc | head -100
7. $ grep -n "class.*Test\|^TEST.*(\|^TEST_P.*(" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/config_test.cc
8. $ find /workspace -type f -name "*ext_authz*" | head -20
9. $ grep -n "failureModeAllow\|failure_mode_allow\|CheckStatus::Error\|CheckStatus::Denied" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -30
10. $ wc -l /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc
11. $ grep -n "TEST_P.*ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest\|TEST_P.*ExtAuthzHttpIntegrationTest" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc | wc -l
12. $ grep -n "^TEST_P.*ExtAuthzGrpcIntegrationTest\|^TEST_P.*ExtAuthzHttpIntegrationTest" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc
13. $ grep -n "CheckStatus::Error\|onFailure\|timeout\|Timeout" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc | head -40
14. $ grep -n "^TEST.*(" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_test.cc | tail -30
15. $ head -50 /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_fuzz_test.cc
16. $ head -50 /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_fuzz_test.cc
17. $ grep -n "onFailure\|FailureReason" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl.cc
18. $ grep -o "class.*IntegrationTest" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc
19. $ ls -lah /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_*corpus/ 2>/dev/null | head -20
20. $ ls -lah /workspace/test/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz_*corpus/ 2>/dev/null
21. $ grep -n "onFailure\|5xx\|is5xx" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_http_impl_test.cc | head -40
22. $ grep -n "class.*IntegrationTest\|TEST_P.*LocalReply" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz_integration_test.cc | tail -20
23. $ grep -n "onFailure" /workspace/test/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz_grpc_impl_test.cc
24. $ grep -r "ext_authz\." /workspace/test/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ --include="*.cc" | head -30
25. $ grep -r "ExtAuthzLoggingInfo" /workspace --include="*.cc" | head -20
26. $ grep -n "ResponseCodeDetails\|AuthzDenied\|AuthzError" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc
27. $ grep -r "emit_filter_state_stats\|filterMetadata\|setDynamicMetadata" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz --include="*.cc" --include="*.h" | head -20
28. $ grep -rn "circuit\|Circuit\|retry\|Retry" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ 2>/dev/null | head -20
29. $ grep -rn "DeadlineExceeded\|timeout\|Timeout" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/*.cc
30. $ grep -r "ENVOY_LOG\|ENVOY_STREAM_LOG" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz --include="*.cc" | head -15
31. $ grep -A 3 "ALL_EXT_AUTHZ_FILTER_STATS\|ALL_TCP_EXT_AUTHZ_STATS" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h /workspace/source/extensions/filters/network/ext_authz/ext_authz.h
32. $ grep -n "void Filter::onComplete\|case CheckStatus::OK\|case CheckStatus::Denied\|case CheckStatus::Error" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -20
33. $ find /workspace -type f -name "BUILD" -path "*/test/*" | xargs grep -l "ext_authz" | head -20
34. $ grep -r "ext_authz" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/common/ --include="*.h" --include="*.cc" --include="BUILD" 2>/dev/null | head -20
35. $ ls -la /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ | grep -E "\.(h|cc)$"
36. $ grep -A 50 "envoy_cc_library" /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/BUILD | grep -E "^\s+\"[^\"]+\"" | head -30
37. $ mkdir -p /logs/agent
